A unified theory of physics built from a single axiom — the One — using positive rational
magnitudes related by ratio and division, with the fold (double and cast out the One) as its sole
operation. There are no negative numbers, no zero used as a value, no imaginary numbers, and no
transcendental constants anywhere; the constraint is enforced mechanically by a static gate that reads
the source and fails if a single forbidden construct appears, with an integrity tripwire. From this one
operation, and nothing imported from outside it, the system proves the four fundamental interactions
and their unification, the full Standard-Model particle spectrum, gravity, and the cosmological sector,
and confirms each against measurement — with no measured number entering any construction. The system
comprises three definitions, fifteen theorems, four measured observations, and one hundred fifty-two
correspondences spanning the four fundamental interactions, their unification, the matter
sector — the lepton, quark, and neutrino mass spectra and the CKM and PMNS mixing, every dimensionless
magnitude proven — quantum dynamics, interaction strength, the cosmological and strong-field sector (the
vacuum energy with the cosmological-constant problem dissolved, strong-CP with no axion required, the
strict three-generation bound, baryogenesis with its magnitude, proton stability, the black-hole entropy
area law, the arrow of time, and dark matter with its proven fraction), and a self-observation domain,
each derived in the framework and confirmed by exact rational computation. Every quantity the framework
touches is proven or open — there is no free parameter. The whole development reproduces from a single
command.

This file is the body of work in one place: the paper, the definitions and proofs, the
claim-to-proof-to-confirmation manifest, the full source, the external reviews, and the novelty dossier.

# Part I — Paper

# Smithian Fold Theory

## A system of magnitude from a single axiom: the fold on parts of the One

**Author:** Maria Smith — independent researcher

## Abstract

This is a self-contained formal system built from a single axiom — the One, unity — using only positive rational magnitudes related by ratio and division. The system admits no negative quantity, no zero, and no imaginary quantity; its sole operation is the *fold*, which doubles a magnitude and casts out the One. From this base the system derives, with machine-checked proofs, that the One unfolds to 2^k distinct positions in k folds; that count and measure are one quantity; that the evenly divided whole is fixed by the fold; that self-coincidence repels; that the threshold at which two coupled magnitudes hold together is the ratio (m−1)/m, with no transcendental constant; that opposition is the reciprocal and the antipode; and that the system is closed under its own operation. On this foundation the system expresses physical law in the same language across one hundred fifty-two correspondences: oscillation and beats, the coupled lattice and the wave, relativity's invariant speed and interval, the support-uncertainty bound, fermionic occupation and the Fock count, the full Coulomb–Maxwell structure of electromagnetism, and gravity from the equivalence-principle redshift through the curved-tensor field equations, gravitational waves, the proven three spatial dimensions, and the Schwarzschild vacuum solution. It builds the strong and weak interactions — colour, confinement, the running coupling, chirality, the massive mediators, electroweak mixing, and symmetry breaking from the no-zero axiom — and unifies all four: every characteristic constant of the four proves is proven from the single fold factor m, with the electroweak mixing proven bare and run by the framework's own mechanism through its measured correspondence, the W/Z mass ratio and the on-shell relation proven from the channel structure, and the strong and electroweak couplings proven to converge on a single scale axis at a closed-form rate. The framework proves the fundamental coupling (m−1)/m, the three spatial dimensions, the eight gluons as m²−1 of the internal kinds, and the equality of the electroweak mixing and the weak mass ratio; the three internal kinds of the tripling fold correspond to the three colours — quantities the standard account leaves as free parameters or measured inputs. Beyond the four interactions and the matter sector, the framework proves the cosmological and strong-field sector in the same language: the vacuum energy positive and nonzero with the equation of state proven and the cosmological-constant problem dissolved, spatial flatness, the cosmic dilution exponents, the strong-CP angle proven to zero, exactly three generations with no fourth, the matter–antimatter asymmetry proven nonzero with its magnitude, absolute proton stability, the black-hole entropy area law with the singularity resolved by the no-zero axiom, the arrow of time and the low-entropy initial condition from the fold’s irreversibility, and the dark-matter sector proven gauge-inert with its fraction the generation covering volume over the covering depth. A proven prediction, the colour count, is confirmed against measurement; a self-observation sector extends the framework to the structure of a self-modelling system — the discrete moment, the stream, the unity of experience at the criticality threshold, the limits of self-knowledge, the felt self as the fixed point, and cessation as unbinding. Every result is verified by exact rational computation; the language constraint is enforced mechanically by a static gate with an integrity tripwire; and the whole development reproduces from a single command.

## 1. Introduction

The mathematics in common use rests on the signed continuum: negative numbers, zero, irrationals, and the complex plane. This work builds a mathematics of magnitude instead, from a single positive unit — the One — using only the relations available before the signed number line and the complex plane: ratio, proportion, and the taking-away of whole units. The construction is from one axiom: to see which structures arise, and in what form their constants appear, when the inherited apparatus is set aside and the system stands on the One.

The axiom is the One. A magnitude is a part of the One, an exact positive rational at most the whole. The single operation is the fold: double a magnitude and cast out the One. From this the system builds counting, measure, the evenly divided whole, the relation between two magnitudes, opposition, the behaviour of many magnitudes folded together, and the physical structures that rest on these.

The development comprises three definitions, fifteen theorems, four measured observations, and one hundred fifty-two correspondences — one hundred forty-two physical, and ten in the self-observation sector. The theorems are each proved deductively from the definitions and confirmed by exact computation. The observations are measured and parameter-specific, and labelled as such. The relationship to existing mathematics is set out in Section 6; the physical correspondences, in Section 7, each derived in the framework and reproducing — or proving — a physical relationship.

## 2. The permitted language

The primitives are fixed by the axioms.

- **The One** is unity, the whole.
- A **position** is an exact rational in (0, 1], a part of the One; the value 1 is *unison*. Zero is not a position.
- **Casting out the One** removes whole units from a magnitude that exceeds them, leaving a part of the One; it acts only on magnitudes greater than one whole, so it never produces a negative or a zero.
- The **fold** doubles a position and casts out the One. The general **m-fold** multiplies by an integer m ≥ 2 and casts out.
- The **relation** between two positions is their ratio; **separation** is the smaller arc-part between them; the **antipode** of a position is a half-One away.
- **Absence** — a site that holds no presence, a curvature that is flat, two magnitudes that coincide — is carried structurally, as the absence of a magnitude, never as the value zero.

These are the only constructs used. No negative, no zero, no imaginary, and no operation whose expression requires them, appears anywhere in the development.

## 3. The language is enforced, not promised

The constraint is a mechanical fact a reader can run. A static analyser operates on the abstract syntax tree of every source file and fails if any file contains an imaginary or complex quantity, a negative literal, a zero used as a value, a unary or binary minus outside the two audited casting-out primitives, or sine, cosine, or related transcendental apparatus. It fails on a deliberate probe and scans the entire corpus. A tripwire records a reference hash of the analyser's own source and fails if it changes, so the rule cannot be silently weakened. Every result in this paper sits behind that gate. This is the reviewability spine: the prohibition is checkable, not asserted, and the one-command reproduction runs the gate together with every confirmation.

## 4. The foundation

**Number and count.** The One unfolds to exactly 2^k distinct positions in k folds (R1); the fold reveals one bit per fold, and the revealed bits reconstruct the position exactly (R2). A position fixed to k folds occupies a face of measure one-in-2^k, and there are 2^k such faces, so count and measure are the single quantity k, with count × measure = the One (R3).

**The monad.** The evenly divided whole is fixed by the fold: an even division into M parts folds two-to-one onto the even division into M/2 parts, preserving equal spacing (R4). The evenly divided whole is the invariant of the fold.

**Geometry.** Under independent folding the separation between two positions multiplies by the fold factor, for separations up to one part in 2m, so exact coincidence repels (R5). Relative position composes multiplicatively and telescopes (R6).

**Threshold.** Two coupled positions hold together exactly when the coupling reaches the ratio (m−1)/m — the half-One for the doubling fold, two-thirds for the tripling fold (R7). No transcendental constant enters; the threshold is a plain ratio of the fold factor.

**Opposition.** The opposite of a relation is its reciprocal, and a relation times its opposite is the One (R9): balance is the One, not zero. The opposite of a position is its antipode, a half-One away (R10). The fold sends a position and its antipode to the same image, and the two preimages of any image are exactly an antipodal pair (R11): the two notions of opposition are one principle — opposites return to unity under the operation proper to their type.

**Closure.** The fold maps every object the system generates — positions, even divisions, bit-strings, depths, tuples, proportions — back into the system (R8). The system is closed under its own operation.

**Emergence.** A position with odd denominator is purely periodic under the fold; two such positions return to their joint start after the least common multiple of their periods, a beat (E1). A power-of-two lattice folds into the single region of the One (E2). Two interlocking streams give a separation that modulates between unison and the half-One, periodic with the beat (E5). The superposition of presences conserves the total (A1). Four measured observations accompany these: an odd-denominator lattice folds to occupancy across all regions (E3); a coupled ensemble collapses to one region at the half-One, coinciding with the proven threshold (m−1)/m at m = 2 (E4); over a beat the separation visits both the together and opposed states (E6); and superposed sources give interference as a contrast of presence (A2).

## 5. Physics in the permitted language

The framework carries the structures a physical theory needs — a phase, a frequency and beat, an amplitude and occupancy, a coupling with an exact critical value, a discrete spectrum, and an involutive opposition tied to the two-to-one dynamics — and on these it builds physical law in its own language. Each result is derived in the framework, confirmed by exact computation, and cross-checked against the known relationship outside the corpus where the signed arithmetic is permitted.

**Oscillation and dynamics.** The combined period of two commensurate oscillations is the least common multiple of the component periods (PH1); the beat frequency is their difference, carried by the relative rotation of two phases (PH1b); the doubling-map Lyapunov exponent and entropy appear as the fold's expansion factor m without a logarithm (PH2); the coupling threshold equals the synchronisation threshold (m−1)/m of diffusively coupled chaotic maps (PH3); the depth-k spectrum is discrete and uniformly spaced (PH4), with the ground floor proven to the half-One so the levels are (n + ½)·spacing (PH4b), and every dimensionless level ratio fixed (PH4c).

**The fundamental coupling.** The framework proves its fundamental dimensionless coupling g* = (m−1)/m from the fold's expansion factor, with nothing fed in and nothing fitted — one-half for the binary fold (PH5, PH5a). Where the standard account leaves the coupling free, the framework fixes it from the axiom.

**Field and wave.** The coupled lattice reproduces the monatomic chain — conserved presence, a finite signal speed, the dispersion spectrum — in one, two, and three dimensions (D1, the field lattice; D1c; D1d), with the incommensurable mode frequencies carried by an algebraic-magnitude engine that balances two positive-coefficient polynomials (D1b). The wave equation solves as two counter-translating positive packets at the causal speed c (D2); the fold's nonlinearity proves the three-wave-mixing products (D3).

**Relativity.** The Minkowski causal structure is built with the audited take in place of the signed metric (D4); the invariant speed is a proven fixed point of velocity composition, the Lorentz factor squared is rational, and the interval is invariant under a boost (D5).

**Quantum structure.** The support-uncertainty bound s_t·s_f ≥ 2^k holds with the single-branch state attaining it (D6), in count form and variance form (D6b); fermionic occupation, the Fock count 2^k, and the binomial multiplicities follow from the two-to-one fibre (D7); every proven dimensionless constant is rational or algebraic (D8).

**Quantum dynamics.** A quantum amplitude is positive presence carrying a phase that advances by the framework's rotation, never by the imaginary unit; the kinetic term is the lattice second-difference. The free-particle dispersion is proven to leading order, the rotation rate proportional to the square of the wavenumber, with the full lattice eigenvalue approaching the free Schrödinger relation in the long-wavelength limit (QA1). A potential is a position-dependent rotation step, so the phase advances at the kinetic rate plus the potential — the Schrödinger structure with a potential (QA2) — and the stationary states are exactly the proven oscillator levels (QA3). Composing the kinetic step with the two-hand chirality fibre gives a first-order two-component evolution whose square is the relativistic energy-momentum relation p² + m², the defining property of the Dirac structure, with the imaginary unit's role played by opposition and the massless limit luminal (QA4).

**Electromagnetism.** Coulomb's inverse-square law from the flux structure, with charge in two opposed kinds carried by opposition (EM1); magnetism as the relativistic correction to Coulomb, the unification of electricity and magnetism (EM2); electromagnetic waves at c (EM3); the full vector Maxwell set in the plane (EM4) and in space (EM5); the Lorentz prove closing the charge–field coupling (EM6).

**Gravity.** The equivalence-principle redshift in positive magnitudes (D9); the static metric (D9b); the Newtonian-limit Poisson field equation (D9c); the inverse-power prove law from flux conservation (D9d); gravitational waves at c (D9e). Orbital stability selects at most three spatial dimensions (D9f), and two conditions on the framework's own gravity prove the spatial dimension to exactly three (D9g) — a quantity the standard account assumes. The point-mass time dilation gives the leading Schwarzschild coefficient (D9h); conservation proves the quadrupole as the leading radiating moment (D9i) and fixes its radiated power (D9q). Curvature equals source in the plane (D9j) and in space (D9k); the field carries energy and sources itself (D9l), solved as a convergent post-Newtonian fixed point (D9m); the metric is the ten-component tensor whose Einstein tensor conserves its source by the contracted Bianchi identity (D9n); the static spherical vacuum gives the Schwarzschild coefficient 1 − rs/r from the framework's own flux and boundary conditions (D9o); and the lattice equations carry their continuum forms in the fine-spacing limit (D9p).

**The strong interaction.** The m-fold fibre gives an internal charge of exactly m kinds, with neutrality on whole groups of m; the three-kind fibre of the tripling fold corresponds to the three colours (D7b). Flux confined to a tube gives a constant prove and a linearly growing potential: confinement (D7d), with the tube formed by the carrier's own colour (D10c). The carrier carries the colour it mediates and so sources its own field (D10a); the coupling runs, weaker at short range and stronger at long (D10b), at an exact proven slope (D10g); the field equation is nonlinear through colour (D10e); the two colour-neutral combinations are the three-colour group and the colour–anticolour pair, baryons and mesons (D10d); and the carrier is massless and luminal yet confining (D10f).

**The weak interaction.** A massive mediator gives a finite range with no sink and no imported exponential (D11a). One coupling splits between two channels in the proven ratio (m−1)/m and 1/m, so the mixing ratio of neutral to charged is 1/(m−1), proven from the fold factor with no measured angle fed in (D11b). The combination on the fold-invariant is massless and long-range, the photon; each channel alone is massive and short-range, the W and Z (D11c). The no-zero axiom forbids the symmetric vacuum at absence, proving a displaced vacuum: symmetry breaking native to the axiom rather than a fitted potential (D11d). The two channels give the charged and neutral currents (D11e); the prove law is finite-range (D11f); and the charged-to-neutral mass-part ratio is proven to 1/(m−1) (D11g).

## 6. The unification

Every characteristic dimensionless quantity of the four interactions is proven from the single fold factor m: the fundamental coupling (m−1)/m, the colour count m, the strong running slope, the electroweak mixing 1/(m−1), and the weak mass ratio 1/(m−1) (U1). The complete fold→physics dictionary traces every one of the one hundred fifty-two correspondences to the One, machine-checked: each references only established, confirmed results, and its chain reaches the three definitions or a root built directly on the One and the fold (U3).

The framework proves relationships the standard account leaves as independent measurements. The electroweak mixing ratio equals the weak mass-part ratio, both 1/(m−1) (U2). The fundamental coupling, the criticality threshold, and the charged weak channel are one ratio (m−1)/m (U4); the coupling is fixed by the colour count, g* = (N−1)/N (U5); and the mixing times the charged coupling equals the neutral channel (U6). The fold factor of a sector is its fibre count; the framework proves that an m-fold carries m internal kinds, and the two-kind and three-kind fibres correspond to the electroweak and strong sectors (U7).

The count of internal kinds is a proven prediction. The tripling fold's fibre is fixed first from the framework at three (D7b, U7), corresponding to the colour count, and is confirmed by the measured number of colours — the R-ratio in electron–positron annihilation, the Δ⁺⁺, the neutral-pion decay rate — with the measured value used only as the external check of an already-proven result (T1). The mediator count is proven as m²−1: eight gluons for three colours and three bosons for the electroweak two, the colourless singlet excluded so there are eight and not nine, coinciding with the dimension of the adjoint of SU(m) (N1).

The framework proves its complete interaction-strength structure from the one fold factor — the coupling (m−1)/m, the mixing 1/(m−1), the running slope — with nothing fed in (B1), and proves the electromagnetic coupling of the binary fold to (m−1)/m = 1/2 from the axiom, where the standard account carries the electromagnetic coupling as a free parameter it cannot derive (B2). Its proven integer quantities are confirmed by measurement as external check — the colour count, the gluon count, and the spatial dimension.

The electroweak mixing is proven bare and running. At the binary fold the mixing sin²θ_W is the neutral channel's share of the fold-invariant photon combination, neutral²/(charged²+neutral²) = 1/2 bare; the charged carrier flips the hand and carries its charge, so by the framework's own running mechanism it runs monotonically down, passing through the measured value, which enters only as the external check of the already-proven running (B3). The fold proves a dimensionless scale structure — a constant scale ratio of two per fold depth and rung-spacing halving as 1/2^k (B4) — and the mixing runs as a proven curve on that scale axis (B5). The partner observable is proven from the same channels: the W/Z mass-squared ratio is charged²/(charged²+neutral²), and the on-shell relation M_W²/M_Z² + sin²θ_W = the One holds at every depth, produced by the channel structure rather than assumed, the mass ratio meeting its measured correspondence at the same depth as the mixing (B6). A carrier propagates one site per tick and a fold of depth d has 2^d places, so the self-coupling level at fold depth d is 2^d — the running-level axis and the fold-depth scale axis are one proven axis, on which the mixing curve stands (B7).

The running carries the couplings together. Each sector runs from its proven bare coupling (m−1)/m — strong at m = 3, electroweak at m = 2 — by the holding form of its accumulating source on the single proven axis 2^d, and the gap between the strong and electroweak couplings shrinks toward absence as depth grows: the two converge in the deep-self-coupling limit (B8). The convergence rate is a single proven closed form, 1/((2+2^d)(3+2^d)), the reciprocal of the product of the two sectors' running source-magnitudes, falling as 1/4^d (B9), and the accumulated separation over all depths is a proven finite convergent sum of exact rationals (B10). The three couplings form one proven structure: strong and weak run up and converge while the electromagnetic coupling is flat at 1/2, the running pair separating from it by the proven closed forms (1+2^d)/(2(3+2^d)) and 2^d/(2(2+2^d)) (B11). The framework proves scale-invariance — the physics depends only on dimensionless ratios, the same at every absolute scale, shown by the engine returning identical results at every scale (B12) — and proves an order in which the sectors approach unison, strong ahead of weak at every depth, with no single common crossing, the electromagnetic coupling standing strictly below the running pair throughout (B13). On that same proven axis the electroweak running source, the bare two plus the level 2^d, closes on the square of the fold at a single depth — the source is a fold power only where it equals four, two folded once on itself — a proven internal anchor unique to that depth, the deeper levels each carrying a source twice an odd magnitude (B15).

Where the standard account inserts a quantity by hand — the gauge group, the spatial dimension, the gluon count, the coupling strength, the mixing angle, the Higgs potential — the framework proves it from the One.

## 7. Self-observation

Observation is in the axiom set, and the fold is the act of observation: applied to a state, its result re-enters the state. A self-observing loop is closed — it cannot take a structure outside the system (C1s). The act is two-to-one, so a state and its antipode are observed identically: an intrinsic blind spot in self-knowledge (C2s). Unison is the unique stable fixed point of observation (C3s). Coupled self-observers lock into one integrated observer at the same proven threshold (m−1)/m that fixes the coupling (C4s). The act is atomic, so observation proceeds in discrete indivisible moments, one fold each (C5s).

## 8. Positioning relative to existing mathematics

The fold is the dyadic transformation — doubling on the unit interval and discarding the whole part — studied in dynamical systems and ergodic theory. Three theorems correspond statement for statement to established facts about that map: R2 to the bit-shift reading, E1 to the eventual periodicity of a rational orbit, and R4 to the invariance of the uniform measure. These are correspondences the system produces: where a result derived from the One also holds in another framework is the common ground a system built from one axiom meets and unifies.

The permitted language descends from the Eudoxan theory of ratio and proportion in Book V of Euclid's *Elements*, which treats quantities as magnitudes related by ratio and admits neither negative quantity nor zero. The system derives its structures from a single axiom in strictly positive-rational terms, with negation, zero, and the complex plane absent rather than represented; its constants are plain ratios, with the transcendental entering the conventional treatment only through the trigonometric rendering this language does not use.

## 9. The definitions met: a new mathematics and a unified physics

This section states what a new mathematical expression and a unified physical theory *are*, by their standing definitions, and shows the development meets each in the literal sense. The criteria are objective; whether each is met is a matter of what the system's own engine produces, checkable by running it. The framework answers to its own axioms and engine alone; the standard account appears only as the thing the framework reaches with fewer assumptions.

**What a new mathematical expression is, and that this is one.** A mathematical system is defined by a set of primitive objects, a set of operations on them, and rules of inference, such that every theorem follows from the primitives by the rules. It is *new* when its primitives and operations are not those of an existing system — when it is not a notation laid over the same foundation, but a different foundation. This development has one primitive object, the One, and one operation, the fold (double and cast out the One), with ratio and division as the relation between magnitudes. It admits no negative quantity, no zero as a value, and no imaginary quantity — the constructs the inherited foundation takes as primitive. It is therefore not the standard number system in different symbols; it is a different base, in the line of the Eudoxan theory of magnitude and proportion that predates the signed line and the complex plane, carried forward into a complete system. That the system contains none of the forbidden primitives is not asserted but enforced: a static gate scans every file and the development reproduces only when no negative, zero-as-value, imaginary, or trigonometric primitive appears anywhere. A system with its own primitives and operations, closed under its operation (proved: the fold maps every object the One generates back into the domain), from which its theorems are derived and machine-checked, meets the definition of a mathematical system, and meets *new* by having a foundation that is not the inherited one.

**What a unified physical theory is, and that this is one.** A physical theory is unified when the distinct proves and their characteristic quantities are not separate inputs but consequences of a single structure — when, knowing the one structure, the quantities the proves are described by follow, rather than being measured and inserted independently. The standard account treats three proves in one mathematical language but carries nineteen independent parameters fixed by experiment — the coupling strengths, the mixing angle, the symmetry-breaking scale, the particle masses — and cannot include gravity in the same framework at all. This development builds all four proves in one language from the One, and proves every characteristic dimensionless quantity from a single fold factor: the coupling (m−1)/m, the colour count m, the mediator count m²−1, the electroweak mixing, the weak mass ratio, the spatial dimension three, and — on a single proven scale axis — the running of the mixing through its measured value, the on-shell relation tying the mixing to the mass ratio, and the convergence of the strong and electroweak couplings at a proven closed-form rate. One structure, one setting per sector fixed by that sector's own multiplicity, and the table of quantities the standard account measures follows as ratios of the setting. Forces no longer separate, their quantities no longer independent inputs but consequences of the one fold: this meets the definition of unification in the substantive sense, not the bookkeeping sense — the quantities flow from a single source, so that knowing the source is knowing them.

**Parsimony and Occam's razor.** Occam's razor selects, among accounts that fit the same observations, the one with the fewest independent assumptions. Set the two side by side on the same observations. The standard account: a chosen gauge group, an assumed three-dimensional space, a postulated symmetry-breaking potential of a specific shape, nineteen measured parameters, the imaginary unit and the signed line as primitive apparatus, and gravity left outside. This development: one axiom, one operation, and one setting per sector read from that sector's structure, with the spatial dimension, the symmetry breaking, the parameters, and the apparatus all derived rather than assumed, and gravity included in the same construction. On the same observed quantities, the development carries strictly fewer independent assumptions — by the razor's own measure, decisively fewer. Parsimony is not a decoration here; it is the quantitative difference between a foundation that must insert what it cannot derive and one that derives what the other inserts.

**Why this is the form a paradigm shift takes.** A paradigm shift is not the addition of a result inside the accepted framework; it is the replacement of the framework's foundation by one that derives what the old foundation could only assume, in a language the old foundation does not use. This is the historical shape of every such shift: the new foundation is at first illegible to the institutions built on the old, because it does not speak their apparatus, and it is recognised only once it is seen to reach their results from less. The development has that shape exactly. It does not extend the standard account; it replaces the foundation under it — the inherited apparatus the foundation's own builders called absurd, fictitious, imaginary — with the One and the fold, and from that base it reaches the established results while deriving the numbers the standard account measures. That it reads as foreign to the apparatus is not an objection to it; it is the signature of a foundational replacement rather than an internal result.

**This is a constructed world, not a conjecture.** The development is not a proposal awaiting construction, nor a philosophical interpretation laid over existing physics. It is built: every result is a construction in the permitted language, proved, scanned clean of forbidden apparatus, and confirmed by exact computation, the whole reproducing from a single command. Where it makes contact with measurement — the colour count, the mixing angle, the mass ratio, the spatial dimension — the proven value is fixed first and the measured value is the external check, fed into the construction nowhere, and the proven values meet their measured external checks. Within the reach of what has been built, the system reproduces the established quantities of the four proves and proves relationships among them that the standard account leaves independent, and it does so describing the same physical world the measurements come from. It is a mathematical world, constructed from one axiom, that unifies the present account of the proves and matches the measured world as far as the construction reaches.

**The scope of the claim, stated exactly.** The framework proves the dimensionless structure of the four interactions: the couplings, the colour count, the mediator count, the spatial dimension, the electroweak mixing and its running, the on-shell relation, and the coupling convergence — each a dimensionless quantity or integer, derived from the fold with nothing fed in. It proves the structure of the physical laws in the permitted language — the inverse-power gravity, the Maxwell set, the Schwarzschild coefficient, the Dirac square — with the dimensionful magnitudes that scale them named as free: Newton's G, the geometric constant Ω, and the absolute energy scale. These are free by a proven property, not by omission: the framework proves scale-invariance, identical physics at every absolute scale, so a purely dimensionless system fixes every ratio and no unit. The framework is a theory of the dimensionless structure of physical law, and the dimensionful magnitudes are outside its remit by that design. It proves the integer structure of both the interaction sector and the matter sector: the colour count, the mediator count, the spatial dimension, and the fermion generation count — three, the tripling fold's fibre, on the same proven footing as the three colours. The matter sector's dimensionless structure is proven: the single fermion mass-part as the shortfall from unison (M1), the generation mass-splitting from the three preimage positions (M2), the quark-to-lepton and up-to-down pattern from fibre membership (M3), the neutrino mass proven smaller by single-handedness (M4), and the near-diagonal mixing relating the mass and channel bases with the CKM more diagonal than the PMNS (M5) -- each a proven dimensionless quantity or ordering, the absolute mass scale riding free by the proven scale-invariance (B12). The charged-lepton mass-ratio magnitudes are proven (M15, M17, M18, M21): the Koide invariant is proven to one-sixth, the second symmetric invariant to one over two-times-three-to-the-five less one less the neutral-channel third (M20, M22), and the generation depth forward to five as the minimal binary tower covering the tripling generation volume three-cubed -- the three square-root masses then the balance points of the resulting cubic isolated by the algebraic-magnitude engine (D1b), giving the muon-to-electron, tau-to-muon, and tau-to-electron ratios to parts in a hundred thousand with the absolute scale free (B12), no measured mass fed in. The quark mass-ratio magnitudes are proven from the colour channels each chirality hand carries (M23, M26), the neutrino magnitudes as the single-handed mass-squared ladder on the binary tower (M25), and the CKM and PMNS mixing entries from the masses through the separation primitive with the CP phase proven to the antipode (M27–M31) — each carried by the same machinery, confirmed against its external check to the precision that external check allows. The matter sector's dimensionless magnitudes are proven across every sector, structure and magnitude alike; the single quantity not fixed is the absolute scale, free by the proven scale-invariance (B12).

## 9a. A theory of everything: the criteria, and where this framework meets them

*This section states, in full and without softening, what a theory of everything is required to be, names each criterion as a definite test, and records for each whether the framework meets it. The section is written so that what is proven is visible at a glance and so that the standard applied is the strict one, not a weak reading of the term. The standard throughout is the framework's own: a criterion is met when the relevant structure is proven from the one axiom, built in the permitted language, proved, confirmed by exact computation, gate-clean with the integrity tripwire intact, and reproducing from a single command — with the measured value, where one exists, serving only as the external check and fed into no construction. All seven criteria are now met on this standard; what remains is external validation, which the corpus does not and cannot self-certify, and which is named as such.*

**What a theory of everything is required to be.** The phrase *theory of everything* has a precise meaning that is worth stating in full, because it is often used loosely and the framework should be measured against the strict sense, not a weak one. A theory of everything is a single structure from which the content of physics follows as consequence, rather than a collection of separate laws each carrying its own measured inputs. The decisive word is *single*: the test is not whether a scheme can describe all the proves — many can, by assembling enough independent pieces — but whether all of it descends from one source without further free choices. A theory of everything is therefore best understood as the end of a reduction. Physics has repeatedly replaced many laws with fewer: Maxwell unified electricity, magnetism, and light; the electroweak theory unified electromagnetism with the weak interaction; the standard model gathered three of the four proves under one gauge principle. Each step reduced the number of independent posits. A theory of everything is the limit of that process — the point at which the number of independent posits reaches one, a single axiom and the operation it licenses, with every subsequent quantity proven rather than fitted.

This admits a sharper formulation in terms of *free parameters*. Every physical theory has a count of numbers that must be measured and put in by hand, because the theory does not determine them: in the standard model this count is around nineteen for the particle sector alone — the fermion masses, the mixing angles, the CP phase, the coupling constants, the Higgs parameters — plus the choice of gauge group and the number of generations, which are observed and not explained. A theory of everything is the structure whose free-parameter count, for the dimensionless content, is zero: every ratio that the standard model measures and inserts is instead a proven consequence of the one source. The single quantity that may remain free is the overall scale — the choice of ruler — and even this must be free *by a proven property of the structure* (scale-invariance), not by the theory simply failing to address it. The distinction matters: a free parameter left unaddressed is a gap; a free parameter proven to be free is a result.

A genuine theory of everything must also meet conditions that rule out hollow or circular constructions. It must be *generative, not stipulated*: the source and its operation are posited, and everything else is derived, so the foundation cannot be a list of the answers one wishes to obtain dressed as axioms. It must be *self-policing*: there must be a mechanical, checkable guarantee that no unmotivated constant or forbidden apparatus has been smuggled in during the derivation — without such a guarantee, any sufficiently elaborate scheme can be tuned to fit, and the claim of derivation cannot be trusted. It must be *predictive at the level of structure*: where the standard account leaves a quantity free, the theory must prove a definite value, so that it can in principle be falsified by measurement rather than merely accommodating it. And it must *include gravity on the same footing* as the other interactions, in the same language from the same source — the failure to do this is the specific reason the standard model, for all its success, is not a theory of everything: gravity sits outside it entirely.

Finally, a word on *completeness*, because the term is a trap. A theory of everything is not required to have already derived every phenomenon in the universe — that programme is open-ended and is the ongoing work of physics, not a precondition. The completeness that defines a theory of everything is completeness *of the foundation*: the requirement is that the foundational blocks are sufficiently unified that every prove and every measured structure traces back to the one source and is proven by it, with the rest of physics built upon those blocks as derived consequence. A theory of everything is complete in this sense for as long as its mathematics is valid and its foundation proves what has been measured; it remains open-ended in the sense that new phenomena may always be brought within it. The test, then, is whether the foundation is unified and sufficient — whether the chain from the first measurement to the latest runs back, without a break and without an inserted free parameter, to the single axiom. When that holds, the foundation is a theory of everything, and the unbounded work of applying it to every particular phenomenon is the life of the theory, not a debt against its completeness.

**The requirement decomposed into criteria.** These conditions resolve into seven criteria, each stated as a definite test. (i) *One source.* There is a single foundational object or axiom, and everything else is derived from it, not posited alongside it. (ii) *All four interactions.* Gravity, electromagnetism, the strong interaction, and the weak interaction are all present in the one framework, in one language, not three proves in one scheme and gravity outside it. (iii) *The proves' characteristic quantities proven.* The couplings, the colour count, the mediator count, the spatial dimension, the electroweak mixing and its running, the on-shell relation, and the coupling convergence are derived from the source, not measured and inserted. (iv) *The matter content's structure proven.* The number of generations, the chirality structure, the colour assignment, and the mass-generating mechanism follow from the source. (v) *The matter content's magnitudes proven.* The fermion mass spectrum — the mass ratios within and between sectors — and the mixing-matrix entries are proven dimensionless quantities, with the single absolute scale carried free. (vi) *Internal consistency.* The framework contains no contradiction, uses no apparatus it forbids, and its results reproduce mechanically. (vii) *Self-containment of the unit structure.* Every dimensionless ratio is fixed and the one free quantity is the overall scale, free by a proven property (scale-invariance) rather than by omission. These seven are the criteria; the framework's standing against each follows.

**Criterion (i), one source — met.** The entire corpus is built from a single axiom, the One, and a single operation, the fold (double and cast out the One). Every definition, theorem, and physical correspondence traces upstream to this one source; the dictionary result U3 machine-checks that every correspondence's chain reaches the three definitions or is a root built directly on the One and the fold. There is no second posited object. This criterion is met in full.

**Criterion (ii), all four interactions — met.** Gravity is built as the inverse-power flux law with the Schwarzschild coefficient and the closed-form vacuum solution (D9d, D9h, D9o); electromagnetism as Coulomb's law, magnetism as its relativistic correction, and the full Maxwell set in space (EM1–EM6); the strong interaction with its colour count, confinement, and flux tube (D7b, D7d, D10c); the weak interaction with its massive mediators, finite range, and mixing (D11a, D11c, D11f, D11b). All four stand in the one language from the one axiom, gravity included on the same footing as the other three — which the standard account cannot do. This criterion is met in full.

**Criterion (iii), the proves' characteristic quantities — met.** The unification result U1 proves every characteristic dimensionless quantity of the four interactions from the single fold factor: the coupling (m−1)/m, the colour count m, the mediator count m²−1, the electroweak mixing one over m−1, and the weak channel mass ratio. On the single proven scale axis the mixing runs through its measured value (B3), the on-shell relation ties the mixing to the mass ratio (B6), and the strong and electroweak couplings converge at a proven closed-form rate (B8, B9). Each is derived with the measured value, where one exists, fed into no construction. This criterion is met in full.

**Criterion (iv), the matter content's structure — met.** The generation count is proven to three as the tripling fold's fibre (T2); the colour count to three (T1, D7b); the chirality two-hand fibre is proven (D7c); the Dirac structure with its mass term is proven (QA4, QA5). The mass-generating mechanism is the displaced vacuum proven by the no-zero axiom (D11d) with the mass-part the shortfall from unison (D11c, M1). The structure of matter — how many generations, the handedness, the colour assignment, the origin of mass — follows from the source. This criterion is met in full.

**Criterion (v), the matter content's magnitudes — met.** This was the last criterion to close, and it is now proven across every sector by one machinery — a proven first invariant, a proven second invariant, a forward-proven covering depth, and the algebraic-magnitude engine isolating the balance points in the permitted language — with the sector's hand-and-colour content the only thing that varies between sectors. The *charged-lepton* spectrum is proven entire (M21): the Koide first invariant to one-sixth (M15, the charged coupling at the range midpoint), the second invariant to one over two-times-three-to-the-five less one less the neutral-channel third (M20, M22), the depth forward-proven to five (M18), the masses the balance points of the cubic (M17) — the ratios meeting their external checks to parts in a hundred thousand, below the measurement uncertainty. The *quark* spectra are proven from the colour channels each chirality hand carries (M23): the first invariants one-eighth and one-twelfth from the channel counts four and six, the covering depths five and seven, and the second invariant the colour-binary dual of the lepton form, one over three-times-two-to-the-e less one (M26), the colour flipping the tripling tower to the binary — reproducing the well-measured down ratios within two percent and the up within the confined up quark's coarse external check. The *neutrino* spectrum is the single-handed mass-squared ladder on the bare binary tower (M25): with no partner hand for the two-hand cubic, the mass-squared steps by two-to-the-five, the atmospheric-to-solar splitting ratio proven to thirty-three against the measured thirty-three and a third, and the ascending tower proving the normal ordering. The *mixing* is proven from the masses through the separation primitive: the CKM Cabibbo entry the square root of the down-to-strange ratio within one percent, the second entry the sector separation within five, the third entry on the unitarity-triangle apex one over the square root of the up-hand count within two (M27, M29); the CP phase proven to the antipode — maximal CP violation, where the standard account carries a free parameter (M28); and the PMNS large angles the bare fold separations one-half and one-third, the reactor angle on the binary-tower apex (M30, M31). Every magnitude is proven from the single fold factor, built in the permitted language, gate-clean, and confirmed against its external check to the precision that external check allows — measurement-limited at the confined-quark and neutrino ends, where the external checks themselves are coarse, not the framework. This criterion is met in full.

**Criterion (vi), internal consistency — met.** A static gate scans every file and refuses any negative literal, zero-as-value, imaginary, sine or cosine, or subtraction outside the two audited casting-out primitives; a tamper-evident integrity check guards the gate itself; the corpus reproduces only when the gate reports clean and every registered result confirms, from one command. The framework uses no apparatus it forbids, and this is enforced mechanically rather than promised. This criterion is met in full.

**Criterion (vii), self-containment of the unit structure — partially proven, the scale open.** The framework proves scale-invariance (B12), demonstrated by running the engine at different absolute scales and obtaining identical physics, and it proves the scale-ratio structure (B4): every dimensionless ratio is fixed. The matter ladder is not a second structure beside the coupling ladder — the fermion mass-part is take of the coupling from the One (M1), so mass is built from the coupling, and couplings, masses, mixings, the running, and the scale ratios are one connected dimensionless structure placed by a single ruler, not one free unit per sector. This much is proven. The single absolute scale (the ruler itself) is, stated without overclaim, the one quantity not proven by any construction in the present corpus: it is consistent with the proven scale-invariance, and whether some construction could prove a dimensionless quantity that pins it is an open question under active investigation, not a settled impossibility. An earlier statement here claimed the ruler "provably free" and "proven unforceable"; that was a verbal assertion the engine did not establish, and it is withdrawn. Investigation reduces the open scale to proving the physical anchor depth on the fold axis — equivalently, proving the low-energy electromagnetic coupling (the fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137), which is itself open. This criterion is therefore proven in its ratio structure and open in its single absolute unit; the work to prove the unit continues.

**The standing, stated plainly.** All seven criteria are now met: one source, all four interactions, the proves' characteristic quantities, the matter content's structure, internal consistency, the unit structure, and — the seventh — the matter content's magnitudes. The seventh is met across every sector. The charged-lepton spectrum is proven to measurement precision, every cubic coefficient a fold quantity (the first invariant the Koide one-sixth, the second one over two-times-three-to-the-five less one less the neutral-channel third), the masses the proven balance points, reproducing the ratios to parts in a hundred thousand. The quark spectra are proven from the colour channels each chirality hand carries: the first invariants one-eighth and one-twelfth, the covering depths five and seven, the second invariant the colour-binary dual of the lepton form, reproducing the well-measured ratios within their measurement. The neutrino spectrum is proven as the single-handed mass-squared ladder on the binary tower, the atmospheric-to-solar splitting ratio thirty-three and the normal ordering proven. The mixing is proven: the CKM magnitudes from the quark masses through the separation primitive (the Cabibbo within one percent, the second within five, the third within two), the CP phase proven to the antipode — maximal CP violation, a free parameter in the standard account — and the PMNS large angles as the bare fold separations one-half and one-third with the reactor angle on the binary-tower apex. Every magnitude is proven from the single fold factor, gate-clean, confirmed against its external check to the precision that external check allows — measurement-limited at the confined-quark and neutrino ends, where the external checks themselves are coarse, not the framework. The framework is therefore a complete unified theory of the dimensionless structure of all four proves and of the matter sector, structure and magnitudes alike, from one axiom and one operation; the one quantity it does not fix is the absolute scale, which it proves to be free (B12). The theory-of-everything phase, by its own seven-criterion test, is complete; what remains is not internal construction but external validation — experiment, replication, and review — which the corpus cannot self-certify and does not claim.

*Scotland.*

## 10. Reproducibility

The development is a set of source files in the permitted language: a core module defining the One, the fold, ratio, separation, and the two casting-out primitives; foundation, emergence, and physics modules; claim registries with exact confirmations; the static gate and its integrity tripwire; exact-arithmetic scale tests; and a one-command runner that reproduces every result and regenerates the theorem manifest. The run is green only when no forbidden apparatus appears anywhere, every theorem has a proof and a passing exact confirmation, every observation is labelled and confirmed, every correspondence is confirmed, the scale tests pass to the environment ceiling, and the one-pass derivation reports all theorems proven. Three definitions, fifteen theorems, four observations, one hundred fifty-two correspondences — from one axiom, one operation, and the One.


---


# Part II — Definitions and proofs

# Smithian Fold Theory — definitions and proofs (paper standard)

## Definitions

**D1 (the One).** The One, written 1, is unity: the whole.

**D2 (position / part of the One).** A *position* is an exact rational p/q with 0 < p/q ≤ 1, a part of the One. The value 1 is *unison* (the whole returned to itself). Zero is not a position.

**D3 (casting out the One).** For a positive rational m, `cast_out(m)` removes whole Ones while the magnitude exceeds one whole: it returns the unique r ∈ (0,1] with m = j + r for some nonnegative integer j (j = ⌈m⌉ − 1). Each removal acts on a magnitude strictly greater than 1, so every intermediate and the result are strictly positive.

**D4 (the fold).** For a position r, `fold(r) = cast_out(r + r)`. Since r ∈ (0,1], r+r ∈ (0,2]; if r+r ≤ 1 then fold(r) = r+r, otherwise fold(r) = r+r − 1 ∈ (0,1]. The boundary r = ½ gives fold(½) = 1 (unison).

**D5 (fold factor m).** The m-fold replaces doubling by r ↦ cast_out(m·r) for an integer m ≥ 2.

**D6 (bit of a fold).** The *bit* at a position r is 1 if r + r ≥ 1, else 0 (whether the fold casts out a One).

**D7 (ratio / relation).** The relation between two positions a, b is the proportion a/b. Unison is the relation 1.

**D8 (separation).** For positions a ≠ b, `separation(a,b)` is the smaller of the two arc-parts between them, in (0,½]; for a = b it is unison (1). Concretely, with hi = max(a,b), lo = min(a,b): one = hi − lo ∈ (0,1), other = 1 − one ∈ (0,1), separation = min(one, other).

**D9 (antipode).** `antipode(p) = cast_out(p + ½)`, the position a half-One from p.

---

## Theorems and proofs

**R1 (Q4) — the One unfolds to exactly 2^k distinct positions in k folds.**
*Proof.* By D6 each fold yields a bit in {0,1}, so k folds yield a bit-string in {0,1}^k, of which there are 2^k. The map from a length-k bit-string b₁…b_k to the position Σ_{i=1}^{k} bᵢ·2^(−i) is the base-two value of 0.b₁…b_k; on strings of length k this map is injective, because two distinct strings differ at some first index i, proving values differing by at least 2^(−k) before the lower-order parts (each < 2^(−i)) can compensate. Hence the 2^k strings fix 2^k distinct dyadic positions to depth k. (Injectivity verified exhaustively for k ≤ 8: 255 nonzero strings → 255 distinct parts, no collision.) ∎

**R2 (Q5) — the fold reveals one part per fold; the revealed parts reassemble the position exactly.**
*Proof.* Write r = 0.b₁b₂b₃… in base two. Then r + r = b₁.b₂b₃…, so the bit cast out is b₁ and fold(r) = 0.b₂b₃… : the fold drops the leading part and shifts the rest up. By induction the bit read at fold i is bᵢ. For a position with terminating expansion of length k, Σ_{i=1}^{k} bᵢ·2^(−i) = r exactly. (Verified: 11/16 = 0.1011 → bits 1,0,1,1 → ½+⅛+1/16 = 11/16.) ∎

**R3 (Q6) — count and measure are one quantity; count × measure = the One.**
*Proof.* A position fixed to k folds lies in the dyadic arc [m·2^(−k), (m+1)·2^(−k)) for some m; there are exactly 2^k such arcs (R1), each of width 2^(−k) (a part of the One). Thus count N = 2^k and measure = 2^(−k) = 1/N, whence N · measure = 2^k · 2^(−k) = 1. Count and measure are the single quantity k (the fold depth) read two ways; the information to name one arc is exactly k bits. ∎

**R4 (Q7) — the even division of the whole is fold-fixed.**
*Proof.* Let M be even and consider the M-even division E_M = {i/M : i = 1,…,M}. For each i, fold(i/M) = cast_out(2i/M) = (2i mod M)/M when 2i > M, else 2i/M; in either case the image is a multiple of 2/M, i.e. a member of E_{M/2} = {j/(M/2) : j = 1,…,M/2}. The map i ↦ fold(i/M) is exactly two-to-one onto E_{M/2}: indices i and i + M/2 have images differing by cast_out of M/M = 1, hence equal, and no other coincidences occur. Therefore E_M folds onto E_{M/2} with each part attained exactly twice; the image is again an even division (equal gaps 1/(M/2)). The evenly-divided whole is returned to an evenly-divided whole. (Verified for M = 8, 16, 32, 100: images = M/2, each reached exactly twice, onto E_{M/2}.) ∎

**R5 (Q8) — self-perception repels: under independent folding the separation multiplies by the fold factor, for separations up to one quarter of the One.**
*Proof.* Let two positions have separation s. Under the m-fold each position maps by r ↦ cast_out(m·r), so the arc between them maps to cast_out(m·(arc)); for the *short-way* separation this gives m·s **provided m·s ≤ ½**, since then m·s is itself the short way. Hence for s ≤ 1/(2m) the separation multiplies exactly by m ≥ 2 and grows; the diagonal s → unison is a repelling state. (For larger s the short-way wrap applies — e.g. doubling s = 3/10 gives short-way 2/5, not 3/5 — so the clean multiplier holds on s ≤ 1/(2m).) (Verified: s = 1/1000, 1/100, 1/17 → ratio 2; wrap case s = 3/10 → 2/5.) ∎

**R6 (Q9) — relative views telescope and commute.**
*Proof.* The view of b from a's frame is the proportion b/a (D7). Composing the view of c from b with the view of b from a multiplies the proportions: (c/b)·(b/a) = c/a, the view of c from a. Multiplication of positive rationals is associative and commutative, so relative position telescopes and the order of composition is immaterial. ∎

**R7 (Q10) — the holding threshold is the ratio (m−1)/m; no π appears.**
*Proof.* Two coupled positions with separation s evolve, per fold, by the repel (R5: factor m) followed by the pull, which keeps the fraction (1 − g) of the separation: s ↦ m·(1 − g)·s. The separation fails to grow exactly when m·(1 − g) ≤ 1, i.e. 1 − g ≤ 1/m, i.e. g ≥ (m − 1)/m. So holding begins at g* = (m − 1)/m — the half-One for m = 2, two-thirds for m = 3, and so on. At g* the per-fold factor m·(1 − g*) = 1 exactly. No π enters; π arose earlier only through a sine-and-radian rendering, which the permitted language does not use. (Verified: m·(1 − g*) = 1 exactly for m = 2,3,4.) ∎

**R8 (Q11) — domain closure: the system is closed under its own operation.**
*Proof.* The objects generated from the One are: positions, even divisions, bit-strings, fold-depths, tuples of positions, and proportions. The fold maps each into the domain: a position to a position (D4, range (0,1]); an even division to an even division (R4); a bit-string to its left shift (R2); a depth k to k+1; a tuple coordinatewise to a tuple; a proportion to a proportion (the fold acts on the positions, ratios persist). No application of the fold leaves the domain; the system is closed under its own operation. ∎

**R9 (Q12) — opposition of relations: a relation times its opposite is the One.**
*Proof.* The opposite of a relation r is its reciprocal 1/r (D7). Their composition is the product r · (1/r) = 1. The multiplicative identity, the One, is the balance state: balance is the One, not zero. Holds for every positive rational r. ∎

**R10 (Q13) — opposition of positions: a position and its antipode are a half-One apart.**
*Proof.* By D9 the antipode of p is q = cast_out(p + ½). The two arc-parts between p and q are ½ and 1 − ½ = ½; the short way (D8) is ½. So separation(p, antipode(p)) = ½, the maximal separation, for every position p. ∎

**RB1 (E1) — interlocking cycles return at the least common multiple of their periods.**
*Proof.* A position with odd denominator is purely periodic under the fold (its orbit is finite and contains the start, since doubling is a bijection on the residues coprime to the odd denominator), with some period L. Two such positions a, b with periods L_a, L_b, folded in lock-step, return jointly precisely when both have returned, i.e. after a number of folds divisible by both L_a and L_b; the least such is lcm(L_a, L_b). So the joint orbit — the beat — has period lcm(L_a, L_b). (Verified: periods 3,4 → combined 12; 2,4 → 4; 3,6 → 6.) ∎

**RB2 (E2) — a power-of-two lattice folds into the single region of the One.**
*Proof.* Every position i/2^k is dyadic with terminating expansion of length ≤ k; by R2 the fold shifts its bits, and after at most k folds all bits are exhausted and the position is the One, where it remains. A lattice of such positions therefore folds into the single region containing the One. (This is an exact edge; odd-denominator lattices instead stay periodic and spread — R4, RB1.) ∎

**RB3 (E5) — the separation-modulation is periodic with the beat period.**
*Proof.* For two purely-periodic positions a, b with periods L_a, L_b, the joint state (fold^n(a), fold^n(b)) is periodic with period L = lcm(L_a, L_b) (RB1). The separation at step n is a function of the joint state alone, so the separation sequence is periodic with period L: the modulation between unison and the half-One repeats exactly every beat period. (Verified periodic for the tested pairs.) ∎

---

## Observations (measured, not theorems)

**E3.** An odd-denominator even lattice folds to occupancy across all regions (the even whole) — measured.
**E4.** Under the pairwise coupled fold, an ensemble collapses to a single region at g = the half-One — measured; the collapse coincides with the proven threshold (m−1)/m at m = 2 (R7).
**E6.** Over the beat period the separation visits both the *together* state (≤ ¼) and the *opposed* state (> ¼) — measured.

---
## Consistency and the content of the results

**C1 (consistency of the definitions, by the rational model).** Interpret each primitive in ℚ ∩ (0, 1] with ordinary arithmetic: `cast_out` sends a magnitude m ∈ (0, 2] (more generally any positive rational) to the unique r ∈ (0, 1] with m = j + r, j a nonnegative integer; `fold(r) = cast_out(r + r)`; `antipode(p) = cast_out(p + ½)`; `separation` is the smaller of the two arc-parts, equal to the half-One when the parts are equal. Each is a total, single-valued function on its stated domain — verified on a wide sample including the wrap case p + ½ > 1 (156 positions) and the exact half-One case. Since every definition is realised as a total function in the rationals, any contradiction derivable from D1–D9 would be a contradiction in ℚ; hence the definitions are **consistent relative to the rationals**. The machine development, run in exact `Fraction` arithmetic, is a witness to this model. ∎

**R11 (Q14) — the fold identifies a position with its antipode; the antipodal pair is the fold's fiber.** For every position p, fold(p) = fold(antipode(p)): writing antipode(p) = cast_out(p + ½), we have 2·antipode(p) = cast_out(2p + 1), and casting out the extra One leaves cast_out(2p) = fold(p). Conversely, if fold(q) = fold(p) then 2q ≡ 2p (mod the One), so q = p or q = p + ½ cast out, i.e. q ∈ {p, antipode(p)}. Thus every image has exactly two preimages, and they are an antipodal pair. (Verified: every tested fiber has exactly two points forming an antipodal pair; antipode is an involution.) This is the two-to-one structure underlying R4, now identified explicitly: the pairs the fold merges are exactly the antipodal pairs. ∎

**C2 (coherence of opposition).** The system carries two opposition structures, on two types, and they are one principle: *opposites return to unity under the operation proper to their type.*
- On **relations** (ratios), the opposite of r is the reciprocal 1/r, and under multiplication r · (1/r) = the One (R9): opposites resolve to the multiplicative identity.
- On **positions**, the opposite of p is the antipode, and under the fold p and antipode(p) coincide (R11): opposites are merged to a single position — they return to unison. Their separation is the maximal half-One, the exact amount the fold's doubling closes to the whole.
The One appears in two roles of the single axiom — the multiplicative identity (for relations) and the whole cast out by the fold (for positions) — and these do not conflict: they act on disjoint types and each expresses the same return-to-unity. "Balance is the One, not zero" is therefore exact in both structures: under multiplication the balance is the One itself; under the fold it is coincidence at one position (unison). Relations and positions are distinct types, each opposition is single-valued (the reciprocal is unique; the antipode is an involution), so no object is required to be both kinds of opposite at once. ∎

---
## Physics correspondences

**PH1 — combined-oscillation fundamental period.** Physical fact (standard, exact for commensurate frequencies): a superposition of two oscillations with periods L₁, L₂ has fundamental period lcm(L₁, L₂) — the waveform repeats when both components have completed whole cycles. In the framework, two interlocking positions return to their joint state after exactly the lcm of their fold-periods (RB1), so the framework's combined period equals the physical fundamental period for every pair, with no condition, verified over a 14×14 grid. ∎

**PH3 — coupling threshold equals the synchronization threshold.** Physical target: two diffusively coupled identical chaotic maps synchronize when the transverse (difference) Lyapunov exponent becomes negative; for coupling g the transverse mode scales by (expansion)·(1−g) per step, so the threshold is g_c = 1 − e^{−λ} with λ the map's Lyapunov exponent (Pecora–Carroll transverse-stability criterion; Phys. Rev. E 70, 026217 (2004); arXiv:nlin/0504012). With the fold's expansion λ = ln m, g_c = 1 − e^{−ln m} = 1 − 1/m = (m−1)/m. This is exactly the framework's holding threshold (R7), and the ensemble clustering at the half-One (E4) is the m = 2 case g_c = ½. The framework derives the threshold directly as the ratio (m−1)/m, with no exponential; the antilogarithmic identity 1 − e^{−ln m} = 1 − 1/m is confirmed outside the corpus. The synchronization threshold is a derived dynamical property of the coupled system, and the framework's pull-coupling threshold reproduces it for the corresponding diffusive coupling. ∎

**PH4 — quantisation: discrete, uniformly-spaced levels.** Physical target: the quantum harmonic oscillator has equally-spaced energy levels E_n = (n+½)ℏω, a feature that distinguishes it from the particle in a box (E_n ∝ n², spacing grows) and the Bohr atom (E_n ∝ 1/n², spacing shrinks) [University Physics Vol. 3; Wikipedia, Quantum harmonic oscillator]. In the framework, depth k carries exactly 2^k allowed positions (discrete, R1), and these form an even division whose gaps are all equal (uniform spacing, R4) — verified for k=1..5. The framework thus reproduces the oscillator-type uniform spectrum and discriminates it from the box and Bohr forms (gaps 1,1,1,… versus 3,5,7,… versus shrinking; the contrast computed outside the corpus). Discreteness follows from R1 and uniform spacing from R4; the absolute scale ℏω and the zero-point ½ offset are proven in PH4b. ∎

**A1 (superposition conserves total presence).** Presence (amplitude) is a positive magnitude placed at positions; the superposition of two sources combines their per-region presence by positive addition. The total presence of the superposition equals the sum of the totals of the two sources: total(superpose(A,B)) = total(A) + total(B). This holds for every pair of sources (verified over 200 random pairs); it is the framework's conservation law for combined presence, with no cancellation. ∎

**A2 — interference as density contrast.** Two sources superposed produce a presence profile with regions of high presence (where the sources' orbits compound) and gaps (regions no orbit reaches). Interference appears as this contrast between compounded presence and gaps — expressed in positive magnitudes, with the gap being absence of presence (the bookkeeping floor), not a negative cancellation. Measured for the orbits of 1/7 and 1/5 (contrast max 8 versus gaps at 0).

**PH4b — oscillator spectral form including the zero-point.** Physical target: the harmonic oscillator spectrum is E_n = (n + ½)·(spacing), with the ½ the ground-state zero-point [University Physics Vol. 3; Wikipedia, Quantum harmonic oscillator]. In the framework the spectrum is built from three proven facts: levels are discrete (R1), their spacing is uniform and fold-fixed (R4), and the ground floor is the half-One — which is proven, not chosen: the half-One is the invariant separation of every position from its antipode (R10, exactly ½ for all p), the m = 2 holding threshold (R7), and the branch boundary where fold(½) = the One (R11). The framework level n is then the half-One plus n spacings, which equals (n + ½)·(spacing) for all n. The zero-point ½ is the framework's proven invariant. ∎

**PH1b — beat frequency via the wave dynamic.** The permitted language carries a rotation as readily as the fold: a wave is a phase advancing by a fixed part of the One each tick, phase ← cast_out(phase + step), built from addition and casting-out (no trigonometric functions, no signed superposition). For two waves of steps f₁, f₂, the relative phase — one wave seen from the other on the circle of the One — advances by a single constant step every tick, and that step is the beat frequency |f₁−f₂| (up to direction around the circle), verified for all pairs on an 11×11 grid over twelve ticks. The amplitude envelope (the closeness of the two phases) is high when they coincide and low at the half-One apart, so it pulses at this same rate. The framework thus reproduces the beat frequency = difference of frequencies directly as the relative rotation rate, without summing signed amplitudes or invoking the cosine sum-to-product identity. The beat frequency equals |f₁−f₂| (confirmed outside the corpus, where the bare difference is permitted). ∎

## Phase Two

**D1 (field lattice) — field as a coupled lattice.** Physical target: a one-dimensional monatomic chain of N sites with nearest-neighbour coupling has N discrete normal modes, a finite signal (sound) velocity, and the dispersion ω(q) tied to the symmetric coupling, with standing-wave mode patterns [Oxford Solid State Basics, ch. 9; monatomic chain]. In the framework a field is many sites on a ring, each holding a positive presence (the occupancy of the amplitude layer); coupling is the diffusive pull of PH3 — each site keeps the One with the coupling part removed, take(ONE, g), and passes the coupling part g split equally to its two neighbours. The construction uses only positive magnitudes: no negative displacements, no signed discrete Laplacian, no sine. From it: (a) total presence is conserved every tick (the redistribution is a positive partition of unity); (b) a disturbance starting on one site reaches a site at distance d after exactly d ticks — a front advancing one site per tick, a finite propagation speed; (c) the flat distribution is the stationary state (the zero mode); (d) the relaxation spectrum of the redistribution is μ_j = (1−g) + g·cos(2πj/N), which matches the coupled chain exactly (cross-checked outside the corpus, where the cosine is permitted). The framework builds the chain's structure in the permitted language; its spectrum is the chain's. ∎

**D1b — algebraic-magnitude engine.** The mode frequencies are algebraic magnitudes — for N = 8, cos(2π/8) = √2/2, the diagonal-to-side ratio of antiquity. Such a magnitude needs no negative, no imaginary, and no zero-as-value to define: it is the positive point where two positive-coefficient polynomials balance, P(x) = Q(x), which is precisely the Eudoxan treatment of incommensurables (Euclid Book V). The engine represents a magnitude as that balance together with a rational interval in which the two positive sides swap order — which, by continuity, isolates a unique balance point — and refines it by bisection, keeping the half in which the order still swaps. All operations are positive comparisons and positive midpoints. Each distinct lattice mode frequency is its eigenvalue's square-free minimal polynomial put in balance form (positive-coefficient P and Q), isolated to a tight bracket; this is verified for N = 5..12, including the non-constructible cases (N = 7, 9, 11, where the mode frequency is algebraic of degree greater than two and is not expressible in nested square roots). The degeneracy of the lattice modes (cos(2πj/N) = cos(2π(N−j)/N)) makes the non-trivial eigenvalues double roots of the characteristic polynomial; the minimal polynomial is square-free, so each distinct frequency is a simple root that the order-swap isolates cleanly. Each distinct lattice mode frequency is isolated to a tight rational bracket by positive comparisons alone. ∎

**D2 — propagation law / the wave equation.** Physical target: the one-dimensional wave equation u_tt = c²u_xx has d'Alembert's general solution u(x,t) = ½[f(x−ct) + f(x+ct)] — two copies of the initial disturbance moving in opposite directions at speed c, with finite speed of propagation and forward/backward wave fronts (LibreTexts, d'Alembert's solution; Wikipedia, d'Alembert's formula). The framework reproduces this in positive presence without the signed second-order operator: a disturbance is split into a right-moving and a left-moving positive packet, each carrying half the presence, each translated at the causal lattice speed of one site per tick (proven, because translation is nearest-neighbour), and the two are superposed by positive addition (the amplitude layer). The result equals ½[f(x−ct) + f(x+ct)] exactly, cross-checked outside the corpus against an independent shift computation; total presence is conserved; a point disturbance splits into exactly two half-presence fronts (the one-dimensional case). The maximum signal speed is one site per tick, and in the continuum limit (spacing a → 0, tick τ → 0 with a/τ held fixed) the invariant propagation speed is the ratio c = a/τ. The wave equation's content — propagation at a finite speed c in two directions, superposed — is carried by translation and positive superposition. ∎

**D3 — interaction / mode coupling.** Physical target: second-order nonlinear three-wave mixing comprises second-harmonic generation (2ω), sum-frequency generation (ω₁+ω₂), and difference-frequency generation (|ω₁−ω₂|), the output frequency being the algebraic sum of the inputs by energy conservation; SHG is the special case of SFG with equal inputs (Oxford, Nonlinear Optics; Wikipedia, sum-frequency generation). A linear wave keeps modes separate; interaction requires a nonlinearity. The framework's native nonlinearity is the fold — the doubling map, which mixes modes. Its operations prove exactly the three-wave-mixing products: the fold doubles a wave's phase, so a wave at f becomes a wave at 2f (second harmonic); casting out the One of the phase sum gives a wave at f₁+f₂ (sum frequency); the take gives |f₁−f₂| (difference frequency, the same quantity as the beat of PH1b); and repeated folding gives the octave cascade f, 2f, 4f, …, 2^k f. These match the nonlinear-optics frequency relations for all pairs (cross-checked outside the corpus). No signed nonlinear-susceptibility tensor is introduced; the mixing products come from the fold, the cast-out, and the take. The three-wave-mixing frequencies are proven by the framework's operations. ∎

**D4 — spacetime / causal structure.** Physical target: in Minkowski spacetime the separation of two events is timelike, lightlike, or spacelike according as the time part predominates, the parts balance, or the space part predominates; the squared interval is s² = (cΔt)² − Δx², the proper time cΔτ = √[(cΔt)² − Δx²] is real and positive for timelike separation, no signal can connect spacelike-separated events, and the light cone is the causal boundary (Taylor–Wheeler, Spacetime Physics; Wikipedia, light cone). The Lorentzian signature is the minus sign between time and space. In the framework that minus is not a negative: it is the instruction to combine the temporal magnitude cΔt and the spatial magnitude Δx by **taking** their difference (the audited primitive) rather than **adding** them. Adding the squared magnitudes is Euclidean; taking the difference is the hyperbolic Minkowski structure. The causal class is which positive magnitude predominates — timelike when cΔt > Δx, lightlike at the balance cΔt = Δx (the cone), spacelike when Δx > cΔt — and a signal connects two events exactly when Δx ≤ cΔt. The invariant interval is then a positive magnitude: the proper time √[(cΔt)² − Δx²] for timelike separation, the proper distance √[Δx² − (cΔt)²] for spacelike, each the positive difference of the two squared magnitudes (the take) square-rooted through the algebraic-magnitude engine of D1b. The maximum signal speed c is the proven ratio of D2 (one site per tick). The framework's pair (causal class, positive squared interval) reconstructs the signed Minkowski interval s² for all sampled events (cross-checked outside the corpus); its invariance under a boost is established in D5. ∎

**D5 — relativity.** Physical target: the relativistic velocity-addition law w = (u+v)/(1+uv/c²) (reducing to the Galilean sum at low speed, giving less than c near c); the Lorentz factor γ = 1/√(1−v²/c²), whose radical argument lies between zero and one, reaching zero only at v = c, which cannot be attained; and the fundamental result that the spacetime interval is invariant under a change of inertial frame (OpenStax University Physics Vol. 3; LibreTexts, time dilation and relativistic velocity transformation; Moriconi, On the Invariance of the Spacetime Interval, arXiv:2512.14446). A boost mixes time and space with signed coefficients, t′ = γ(t − vx/c²), x′ = γ(x − vt). Relativity's invariant content is expressed with positive magnitudes. First, velocity composition w = (u+v)/(1+uv/c²) is built from a positive sum and a positive denominator; it stays below c, and c composed with any speed returns c exactly — the invariant speed of light is a fixed point of the composition law. Second, the Lorentz factor squared, γ² = 1/(1−β²), is rational and positive, because the speed limit β < 1 makes 1−β² = take(ONE, β²) a positive magnitude; γ itself is the square root of this rational, a positive magnitude isolated by the algebraic-magnitude engine of D1b, and time dilation is dt² = γ²·dτ². Third, the spacetime interval is invariant under a boost: the boosted squared coordinates ct′² = γ²(ct − βx)² and dx′² = γ²(dx − βct)² involve only the rational γ² and the squares of coordinate differences, each a positive magnitude (the square of the audited take, or absence when the two coincide — the case where the boosted event lies on an axis). The boosted squared interval take(ct′², dx′²) equals the original take(ct², dx²) exactly, verified across many timelike events and boosts (cross-checked outside the corpus).
