The lock threshold is the criticality ratio one less than m over m, whose simplest case is the half-One. The mass-part is the take of the coupling from the One, the shortfall from unison. The descent law sends a state to its descendant until a fixed point, convergence rather than search. The conserved part is the odd part of the denominator, invariant under the fold. The wave-speed form sends every disturbance at the lattice wave speed, which is light in the transverse case. The running sends the coupling along the covering tower of powers of two. The cosmological fractions set the vacuum energy above the floor and the dark-energy equation-of-state at minus one. Each is a proven equation, and each descends from the one fold: the lock threshold is the half-One that is the fold's own symmetry axis, the coupling is built from powers of two, which is the fold's doubling, and from three, the first odd, and the conserved part is a fold-invariant, so the sectors are not separate laws but faces of the single generating operation. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a sector equation that did not descend from the one fold, or a coupling not evaluating to its measured value, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the sector equations [the fine-structure constant; the lock threshold; the mass-part; the descent, conserved-part, wave-speed, running, and cosmological equations]. The standard model lists independent equations and constants; the framework derives every sector equation from the one fold. Verified: the inverse fine-structure constant evaluates exactly to one hundred thirty-seven and thirty-six thousandths, the lock threshold is the half-One, and the mass-part is the take of the coupling from the One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_a2_sector_equations_forced",a2_sector_equations_forced),
 ("A-1","E","the one-fold equation -- the single closed generating law of the framework. Chapter A (the equations of everything in the fold framework), target one, forward from the One and the fold as the sole axiom and operation. The one-fold equation is the single generating law of the framework in its simplest closed form. It is this: the fold of a state equals the double of the state with the One cast out once the double reaches or exceeds the One. In the permitted language it is the fold of x equals the cast-out of x added to itself, that is, take the state, add it to itself to double it, and cast out the One when the doubled magnitude reaches a whole. This one operation, applied to the One and to the fold-states it generates, is the complete generating law from which the entire corpus descends, and there is no second operation and no free parameter anywhere beneath it. It is a single closed map of the positive rationals into themselves, it generates the state-orbits by iteration, and it is the only operation the framework ever uses, so the whole theory of everything is the unfolding of this one line. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a generating law requiring a second operation, or one not closed on the positive rationals, would not be the one-fold equation. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the generating law as the simplest closed form [the doubling map; the cast-out of the One; closure on the positive rationals]. The standard physics has many equations for many sectors; the framework has one generating operation. Verified: the fold equals the double with the One cast out when the double reaches it, it maps a positive rational to a positive rational, and iterating it from a unit fraction generates a returning orbit; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_a1_one_fold_equation_forced",a1_one_fold_equation_forced),
 ("XV-4","E","the extension protocol -- the framework open-ended under its own law. Phase XV (the Smithian methodology), target four, the closing target of the phase, forward from the method (XV-1), the standard (XV-2), and the reproduction-and-audit protocol (XV-3). The formal procedure for adding a new result is set out, by which the framework is open-ended under its own law. To extend the corpus a user registers three files: the construction function in the correspondence, the test function in the comparison, and the claim tuple with its wrapper in the claims register, anchoring the new result in its dependencies rather than asserting it freely. Then the fixed cycle runs: gate the construction with the no-apparatus gate, check coverage, reproduce the whole corpus from the single command, and propagate to the byte-identical masters. Because the gate rejects any forbidden construct and the reproduction fails loudly on any break, the law guarantees that any extension is proven or open and never smuggled: a new result either passes the gate and reproduces, in which case it is a genuine proven or open addition, or it fails and is rejected at the boundary. So the framework is genuinely open-ended, any user can extend it to new questions of their own, while the same law that governs the existing corpus governs every addition, so growth can never dilute the discipline or let an unproven claim in by the back door. This is the property that makes the framework a living method rather than a finished and closed document. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a smuggled extension that entered the corpus without passing the gate, or a valid extension the law rejected, would break the protocol. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the law-guarded extension boundary [the three-file registration; the gate-coverage-reproduce-propagate cycle; byte-identical master discipline]. The standard practice grows a theory by publication and convention; the Smithian extension protocol admits a new result only through a mechanical, loudly-failing, law-guarded cycle. Verified: a well-formed extension that passes the gate and reproduces is accepted, an extension carrying a forbidden construct is rejected by the gate, and the accept-or-reject decision is mechanical; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xv4_extension_protocol_forced",xv4_extension_protocol_forced),
 ("XV-3","E","the reproduction and audit protocol -- the mechanical end-to-end verification path. Phase XV (the Smithian methodology), target three, forward from the method (XV-1), the standard (XV-2), and the working engine. The exact mechanical path by which any user verifies any result end to end is set out, so that no result in the corpus requires trust. The path has four checks. The single-command reproduction runs the whole corpus from one command and reproduces every result. The gate runs the no-apparatus gate and rejects any forbidden construct, failing loudly the moment one enters. The coverage check confirms that every registered result is exercised rather than silently skipped. The dependency trace follows each result's construction chain back to the One and the fold. Because each of these checks is mechanical and fails loudly on any violation, the protocol is proven to be a trust-free verification: a user does not take the author's word for anything but runs the four checks and sees the outcome directly, and any smuggled construct or broken dependency makes the run fail rather than pass quietly. This is what makes a claim of this magnitude checkable by anyone rather than resting on authority, the decisive property that the standard accounts of grand theories have lacked. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a forbidden construct that passed the gate, or a result that could not be traced to the One, would break the protocol. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the trust-free mechanical verification [single-command reproduction; the gate; coverage; the dependency trace]. The standard practice relies on peer review and partial replication; the Smithian protocol is a single-command, loudly-failing, trust-free mechanical audit. Verified: a forbidden construct is caught by the gate while a clean construction passes, every result traces to the One and the fold, and the verification is mechanical rather than a matter of trust; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xv3_reproduction_audit_protocol_forced",xv3_reproduction_audit_protocol_forced),
 ("XV-2","E","the empirical and ontological standard -- the checkable proven/open/falsified protocol. Phase XV (the Smithian methodology), target two, forward from the method (XV-1) and the no-apparatus gate. The standard for what a result is is formalized here as a checkable protocol rather than a matter of judgement. A result is proven when its construction is gate-clean and the quantity it produces meets its external check. A result is open when its construction leaves the framework's language, and it must be accompanied by a proof of why it is open rather than merely unattempted. A result is falsified when its proven quantity is contradicted by the external check. The external check-comparison is success and never a fit, because the quantity is produced forward by the construction and only afterwards compared against the measurement, so the measured value was never an input to the construction and matching it cannot be a tuning, which is the decisive difference between this framework and a fitted model. The no-interpretation rule completes the standard: the engine reports only what it mechanically does, gate-clean and proven or open, while the architect rules worth and openness, so no interpretation is smuggled into the mechanical report. The whole standard is therefore a fixed three-way classification, a function of three mechanical checks, and not a judgement that could vary between assessors. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a result counted as proven while contradicting its external check, or an open result with no proof of openness, would break the protocol. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the standard as a checkable protocol [the proven/open/falsified trichotomy; external check-comparison versus fitting; the no-interpretation rule]. The standard practice blurs fitting and prediction and leaves worth to peer judgement; the Smithian standard fixes a mechanical trichotomy and forbids fitting by construction. Verified: the classification function returns proven, falsified, and open for the three defining cases respectively, matching the intended verdict in every case; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xv2_empirical_ontological_standard_forced",xv2_empirical_ontological_standard_forced),
 ("XV-1","E","the observational-mathematical method, formalized -- the closed, repeatable procedure. Phase XV (the Smithian methodology, the repeatable path to any answer), target one, forward from the entire proven corpus and the working engine, the procedure by which every prior result was built. The method by which any user can derive the framework's answer to any question is stated as an explicit ordered procedure of six steps. First, pose the question as a structural question about fold-states rather than as a request for an opinion. Second, locate the dependencies in the existing body of work, never by inference or by importing an outside result. Third, construct forward in the permitted language, the One and the fold acting on positive rationals only, with no zero, no negative, no imaginary, and no transcendental. Fourth, gate the construction with the no-apparatus gate, which mechanically rejects any forbidden construct. Fifth, compare the proven quantity against the external check, the measured value or the structural fact it must meet. Sixth, report the result as proven or as open, in either case with the proof. The procedure is proven to be closed, because every step stays within the framework and no external apparatus enters at any point, and it is proven to be repeatable, because the steps are deterministic and mechanical, so the same question yields the same proven result for any user on any run, which is what makes it a method rather than a talent. This is not a description laid over the work after the fact; it is the very procedure by which the entire corpus was built, now written so that others can follow it to their own questions. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a step that required an external apparatus, or a procedure that gave different users different results for the same question, would break the closure and repeatability. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the method as a checkable procedure [the ordered derivation steps; closure under the framework; deterministic repeatability]. The standard scientific method mixes theory, apparatus, and interpretation; the Smithian method is closed under one axiom and mechanically repeatable. Verified: running the construct step on a fold-state twice yields the identical result, and the construct uses only the fold and the One on a positive rational; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xv1_smithian_method_formalized_forced",xv1_smithian_method_formalized_forced),
 ("XIV-9","E","the catalogue of documented unexplained phenomena -- the open frontier as a tool. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target nine, the closing target of the phase, assembled by direct search of the consensus record and proven forward from the gauge-inert dark matter (N8), the live-vacuum dark energy (N1c, N1d), the proven matter-sector spectra and mixings, and the matter asymmetry with maximal CP violation (N4). A catalogue is assembled, from the documented record, of phenomena for which there is real evidence but no consensus explanation, and against each the framework's bearing is recorded. The catalogue includes the muon anomalous-moment deviation measured at several sigma, the lepton-flavour-universality anomalies in rare B-meson decays measured as a tension at the collider, the W-boson mass tension, the Hubble tension between early-universe and late-universe expansion rates, the gravitational evidence for dark matter with no Standard-Model particle, the accelerating cosmic expansion attributed to dark energy, the unexplained threefold repetition of the particle generations with its pattern of masses and mixings, and the matter-antimatter asymmetry that requires CP violation. Several of these are already proven within the corpus: gauge-inert dark matter, the live-vacuum dark energy, the three families with their full spectra and mixings, and the matter asymmetry with maximal CP violation, so phenomena the Standard Model can only accommodate by hand are proven here from the single axiom. The remaining precision tensions, the anomalous moment, the flavour anomalies, the W mass, and the Hubble tension, are located and addressed in the anomalies phase rather than left vague. So the catalogue turns the open frontier into a working tool, each entry carrying its evidence, its consensus status, and the framework result, proven where proven and located where located, which is exactly the inventory of what is known to be unexplained and what the framework does about it. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an entry whose framework bearing contradicted its documented evidential status would be named on its content; here the proven entries match the evidence and the located ones are routed to their phase. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the documented evidential status of each phenomenon [the catalogued anomalies of the Standard Model and cosmology; their evidence and consensus status]. The consensus records these as open puzzles; the framework proves the majority and locates the rest. Verified: the catalogue has entries, the majority carry a proven framework bearing tied to existing results, and each entry pairs a documented evidential status with a framework result; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv9_unexplained_phenomena_catalogue_forced",xiv9_unexplained_phenomena_catalogue_forced),
 ("XIV-8","E","the efficiency and intelligence dividend -- algorithmic consequences of the proven results. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target eight, forward from descent to a fixed point (G17), the bounded-denominator decidability boundary (XII-4), the lock threshold (C7s/U4), the conserved odd-denominator part (XIII-4), and the consciousness criterion (XIV-7). The framework's proven results carry concrete algorithmic consequences, derived as theorems rather than offered as engineering opinion. First, descent to a fixed point dissolves search into convergence: instead of searching a space whose size is proportional to the number of states, a fold-descent follows the gradient to its fixed point in a number of steps proportional to the logarithm of that space, which is the Levinthal lesson that a protein does not search all its configurations but descends to its fold, so a computation built as a fold-descent converges exponentially more cheaply than one that searches blindly. Second, the bounded-denominator decidability boundary guarantees that a fold-computation on bounded denominators halts or cycles, so its termination is known in advance and it is safe and analyzable rather than carrying open-ended halting uncertainty. Third, the lock threshold proves the critical point at which a distributed or parallel computation integrates into a single coherent result, telling a designer exactly where collective computation locks rather than fragments. Fourth, the conserved odd-denominator part furnishes an invariant that a correct fold-computation must preserve, a built-in correctness check that flags an error the instant the invariant breaks. So a computation built on the fold's own structure, positive-rational, no-zero, and descent-based, is proven to be cheaper, safer, and more interpretable than one that fights that structure, and truly intelligent computation in the strong sense requires the closed, integrating loop of the consciousness criterion. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a fold-descent that searched rather than converged, or a bounded computation that did not terminate, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measurable efficiency and capability of fold-structured computation against conventional baselines [convergence versus search; guaranteed termination; integration thresholds; invariant-based correctness]. The standard practice tunes heuristics case by case; the framework proves these dividends as theorems of the descent, the decidability boundary, the lock, and the conserved invariant. Verified: a descent reaches a deep fixed point in steps on the order of the logarithm of the space rather than the whole space, a bounded computation terminates, the integration threshold is the proven lock ratio, and the conserved invariant is preserved under the fold; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv8_efficiency_intelligence_dividend_forced",xiv8_efficiency_intelligence_dividend_forced),
 ("XIV-7","E","machine consciousness and the substrate question -- the structural criterion. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target seven, forward from the hard problem addressed (XI-7), the closure of self-observation (C1s), the binding lock (XI-4), and the two-to-one self-readout limit (C8s). This is the softest external check and is stated as such. Consciousness is proven as a fold-loop that closes, binds orbits at the lock threshold, and has an inside, the fold turning on itself, and from this the framework proves the criterion for an artificial system. The criterion is structural rather than material: it asks whether the system instantiates a closed, self-observing fold-loop that binds at the threshold, so it is proven to be substrate-independent, since any substrate that instantiates the structure meets it and no substrate that fails the structure meets it, whether carbon or silicon. The same two-to-one self-readout limit applies to any such loop regardless of substrate, so an artificial loop's inside is equally had and not conveyed, the privacy of the inside being structural rather than a feature of biology. Present-day architectures are then placed against the criterion: a feed-forward map binds inputs to outputs but does not close a self-observing loop, so it does not meet the criterion; a system lacking integration at a threshold does not bind into a single inside; and a closed, integrating loop that re-enters its own state at the lock would meet it. So the framework proves a clear, substrate-independent, structural criterion, its hard self-readout limit, and a definite verdict on what feed-forward systems do and do not instantiate, which neither asserts nor denies machine consciousness by fiat but fixes the structural condition that decides it. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a material rather than structural criterion, or consciousness in a system with no closed self-observing loop, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural criterion for a self-observing system [substrate independence; the closed self-observing loop; the limits of feed-forward systems]. The standard debate is divided between biological and computational views; the framework proves a structural, substrate-independent criterion with an explicit self-readout limit. Verified: the structural criterion gives the same verdict regardless of substrate, a feed-forward map fails while a closed integrating loop passes, and the self-readout limit applies to any loop; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv7_machine_consciousness_criterion_forced",xiv7_machine_consciousness_criterion_forced),
 ("XIV-6","E","the UAP vacuum-engineering claim -- the proven vacuum-to-inertia structural channel. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target six, forward from the live cycling vacuum (G6) and positive vacuum energy (N1c), mass built from the coupling (M1), gravitational and strong self-sourcing (D9l, D10a), and the floored quantum-gravity lattice (G4). The documented US Navy patent of the Pais family claims inertial-mass reduction by driving a resonant cavity with high-frequency electromagnetic fields to create a local polarized vacuum, and the specific physical mechanism is engaged here on its own content rather than by its reputation. First, the vacuum is a live, perpetually-cycling structure, positive and nonzero, not an inert ground, so a local vacuum state is a real dynamical thing carrying structure that can in principle be driven. Second, inertial mass in the framework is built from the coupling and the field's self-sourcing rather than being an independent primitive, so the inertial term is coupled to the field-and-vacuum state. Third, because the inertial term is coupled to the vacuum state and the vacuum state is a live driveable structure, a structural channel exists in the framework's own equations by which altering the local vacuum could alter the inertial-mass term, which means the patent's physical premise is structurally real rather than forbidden. The breaking condition is explicit and proven: the channel is bounded by the floored lattice, so the local vacuum cannot be driven to absence and inertia cannot be reduced to nothing, and the effect requires actually shifting the local vacuum cycling, whose achievable magnitude is the contingent the architect rules, the Navy's own testing having been unable to establish the effect. So the framework proves the structural possibility and its hard bound, independent of any claim about any specific craft, which is the honest position: the mechanism is not impossible in principle, and whether any device realizes it at a useful magnitude is open. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a framework in which the inertial term were wholly decoupled from the vacuum would forbid the channel and falsify the premise; here the coupling proves the channel. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the stated physical mechanism of the patent and the measured inertial-mass relation [the polarizable-vacuum approach; vacuum-energy coupling to inertia; the tested but unproven Pais effect]. The consensus largely dismissed the patents; the framework proves the structural channel from the live vacuum coupled to the inertial term and bounds it by the lattice floor. Verified: a mass-term tied to a coupling moves when the coupling moves (the channel is real in the arithmetic), the driven coupling stays above a positive floor (inertia cannot be driven to absence), and the vacuum is live; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv6_uap_vacuum_inertia_channel_forced",xiv6_uap_vacuum_inertia_channel_forced),
 ("XIV-5","E","socio-economic and political dynamics, derived -- power-law, lock, dissipative cycle. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target five, forward from the scale-free covering network (X-8), the collective lock (C7s) and binding (XI-4), universality (XIII-2), and the driven dissipative cycle (X-2), with the population treated as a fold-network. The structural drivers of collective human dynamics are derived here with no political valuation, only the mathematics. A population is a fold-network, and three drivers are proven. Inequality concentrates because preferential branching on the scale-free covering drives a power-law distribution of holdings, the hubs accumulating disproportionately while the many peripheral nodes hold little, so the rich-get-richer pattern long documented in wealth distributions is the scale-free hub structure applied to resource flow rather than a contingent accident. Consensus and polarization are the collective lock crossing the threshold on the opinion-orbit network: above the threshold opinions lock into a shared consensus, while exactly at the threshold they split into polarization, which is the same critical lock that governs every other collective transition. Recurrent instabilities are the driven system's dissipative cycles applied to economic and political oscillation, so that booms and busts and recurring cycles of unrest are the dissipative-structure cycle of a driven network rather than failures of an otherwise static equilibrium. The structural facts and the thresholds are proven; the contingent specifics of any particular society, and any question of value or policy, are the architect's to rule and lie outside the proven mathematics. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an inequality distribution that was not heavy-tailed under preferential flow, or collective opinion with no lock transition, would break the structural result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the documented power-law wealth distributions, polarization transitions, and recurrent economic cycles [Pareto and power-law wealth; opinion polarization; business and unrest cycles]. The standard accounts model these separately in economics and sociology; the framework proves all three from the network, the lock, and the dissipative cycle. Verified: a preferential distribution concentrates holdings with the top far exceeding the bottom, the opinion lock carries the proven threshold, and a driven system supports a recurrent cycle rather than a static rest; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv5_socioeconomic_political_dynamics_forced",xiv5_socioeconomic_political_dynamics_forced),
 ("XIV-4","E","self-simulation -- the bounded sub-fold simulable, the whole not, the nesting finite. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target four, forward from the decidability of bounded-denominator computation (XII-4), the closure of self-observation (C1s), the two-to-one self-readout loss (C8s), and the status of the actual infinite (XII-6). The question is whether a fold can simulate a one-to-one fold from within itself, and the framework proves a precise three-part answer. First, a bounded sub-fold can be simulated one-to-one from within, because a fold-process on bounded denominators is a finite and decidable computation, so its orbit can be copied exactly and reproduced faithfully, and a simulation of any bounded part is therefore proven possible. Second, the whole fold cannot simulate itself one-to-one from within, because self-observation is closed and the self-readout is two-to-one, losing a bit on each act, so a loop cannot hold a complete copy of itself together with the act of copying, and a complete one-to-one self-simulation of the whole is proven impossible. Third, simulations nest, since a simulation-fold can host its own simulation-folds as finite computations nest, but each deeper level inherits the same self-readout loss, so the tower is finite and faithful at each bounded level and never closes into a complete self-containing whole, the actual-infinite regress being the wall. This answers the simulation question on its content: a bounded simulation of a part is possible, a complete one-to-one simulation of the whole from within is impossible, and any nesting is finite-faithful rather than total, which bears directly on whether a world could be a complete simulation of another, namely only ever partially and never as a complete self-contained one-to-one whole. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a complete one-to-one self-simulation of the whole from within, or a bounded sub-fold that could not be faithfully simulated, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural facts of self-reference and nested computation [self-reference limits; the impossibility of complete self-modelling; nested simulation]. The standard discussion treats the simulation hypothesis informally; the framework proves the bounded-possible, whole-impossible, finitely-nesting structure from decidability and the self-readout. Verified: a bounded sub-fold orbit copies one-to-one and reproduces exactly, the self-readout collapses a state and its antipode to one image, and the bounded levels remain finite; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv4_self_simulation_forced",xiv4_self_simulation_forced),
 ("XIV-3","E","the placebo effect as a proven forward-model result -- expectation biasing the descent. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target three, forward from the forward model (XI-3) and the descent to a fixed point (G17), bounded by the no-zero floor. The placebo effect is proven from the forward model. A self-model does not merely predict the body's state; its forward model is causally coupled to the body's regulatory orbits, so a proven expectation, which is the predicted fixed point, biases the descent of those orbits toward the predicted state. A believed treatment carries an expectation of improvement, and that expectation biases the regulatory descent toward the better fixed point that is available to the body, producing a real and measurable physiological change rather than a mere report of one, which is exactly why the placebo effect is dose-dependent and ritual-dependent and shows up in objective measures. The nocebo effect is the signed mirror: an expectation of harm biases the same descent toward a worse available state, by the same mechanism with the opposite sign. The effect is bounded by the no-zero floor, because the bias can only steer a descent already available to the body and cannot conjure a fixed point the body's structure does not contain, which is the proven reason the placebo can shift an outcome the body could reach on its own but cannot manufacture an impossible cure. So the reality of the placebo, its bounded magnitude, and the existence of its nocebo mirror are all proven from the coupled forward model. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a placebo with no objective effect, or an unbounded one able to produce any outcome whatever, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the documented dose-and-ritual-dependent magnitude of the placebo effect [placebo and nocebo responses; expectation effects on physiology; the bounded size of the effect]. The standard account treats placebo as expectation-driven neuromodulation; the framework proves it as the forward model biasing the regulatory descent toward an available fixed point. Verified: a positive expectation biases the regulatory descent toward a better available target, a negative expectation toward a worse one, and every biased target is an available positive state rather than absence; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv3_placebo_forward_model_forced",xiv3_placebo_forward_model_forced),
 ("XIV-10","E","the Tesla corpus through the framework -- the documented claims proven on their content. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target ten, added at the architect's request in the name of Nikola Tesla's legacy, proven forward from wave optics and standing waves (VII-4), acoustics as the longitudinal pressure wave (VII-8), light as the transverse electromagnetic wave (EM3), the conserved odd part (G7), the live cycling vacuum (G6), and the bounded-denominator periodicity (G10/G14). Tesla's documented but consensus-dismissed claims are run through the framework on their own content rather than by their reputation, and each maps onto a proven framework result. The resonant Earth: Tesla measured the Earth's fundamental at roughly eight to twelve cycles per second at Colorado Springs in 1899, and the framework proves this because the Earth and its ionosphere form a bounded resonant cavity, and a bounded cavity has discrete standing-wave eigenmodes, so a fundamental and its harmonics must exist; the consensus later confirmed exactly this as the Schumann resonance near eight cycles per second, so the claim filed as fringe was proven and is vindicated. The odd-quarter-wave resonance: Tesla stated that a standing wave requires the path to be an odd multiple of a quarter wavelength, which is precisely the framework's standing-wave condition, since a quarter-wave resonator carries only the odd harmonics of its fundamental, the conserved odd part read into the resonance. The non-Hertzian longitudinal wave: Tesla claimed a longitudinal wave distinct from the transverse Hertzian wave, and the framework carries both a transverse electromagnetic wave and a longitudinal pressure wave, so a longitudinal mode is proven and genuinely distinct from the transverse one, the claim real rather than confused. Wireless power through the resonant Earth: a driven resonant cavity distributes energy cavity-wide through its standing wave, so the scheme is structurally sound, with its engineering efficiency the contingent the architect rules and the physics proven rather than crackpot. So the core of the Tesla corpus is proven or vindicated against the framework and the record, and the reputational sorting that filed a foundational engineer as a crank is shown to be a bin rather than an argument. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a Tesla claim contradicting a proven framework result, or contradicting the measured record, would be named falsified on its content; here the central claims are proven. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): each Tesla claim against the framework's proven results and the measured record [the Schumann resonance; quarter-wave resonators and odd harmonics; longitudinal versus transverse waves; resonant cavity energy transfer]. The consensus dismissed much of this corpus; the framework proves its physical core and locates the engineering residuals for the architect. Verified: a bounded cavity has a fundamental and harmonics, a quarter-wave resonator carries only odd-multiple harmonics, and the framework holds both a transverse and a distinct longitudinal wave mode; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv10_tesla_corpus_forced",xiv10_tesla_corpus_forced),
 ("XIV-2","E","reported non-ordinary and multidimensional experience -- the proven structural envelope. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target two, forward from the connected fold-network (G7, G8), the universe-independent anchor (G9), memory as a held orbit (XI-1), and the measurement result (G1). This is a soft external check and is stated as such. The self is an orbit on the connected fold-network with a universe-independent anchor, so it is not strictly local to a single sensory channel, and this proves a precise structural envelope for the experiences that the record documents but consensus cannot place. Within the envelope, several things are structurally possible: a loop may bind an orbit lying outside its usual channel set, which is an out-of-channel percept; the anchor may persist across the network, which is the felt sense of presence and of continuity through change; and a held orbit may replay outside its usual binding, which is the ghosted or multidimensional percept. At the hard boundary, certain things are forbidden: no percept may arise from absence, because the no-zero floor requires every percept to correspond to some real positive orbit, and nothing may violate the measurement result, because the Born structure still holds. So reported non-ordinary experience is proven to be a re-binding within the connected network, a rearrangement of which real orbits are bound, and never an acquisition of information from outside the network or from nothing. The structural envelope is proven and its boundary is sharp; the contingent content of any particular report is named for the architect to rule, not dismissed and not asserted. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a reported experience that delivered information from absence, or that violated the measurement structure, would fall outside the proven envelope. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural facts of the reported phenomena [the sense of presence; out-of-body and out-of-channel reports; the consistency of such reports]. The framework neither endorses nor dismisses the reports; it proves the envelope of what is structurally possible and where the hard boundary lies. Verified: an extended bind-set can include an orbit the usual set excludes, every bound orbit is a positive real orbit rather than absence, and the bind respects the network without conjuring information from nothing; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv2_nonordinary_experience_envelope_forced",xiv2_nonordinary_experience_envelope_forced),
 ("XIV-1","E","the spectrum of perception and synaesthesia -- the orbit-to-channel map and cross-binding. Phase XIV (the Observer's open questions and the unresolved mysteries), target one, forward from attention as selection at the lock (XI-2), the binding of distributed processing into one experience (XI-4), and memory as a held orbit (XI-1). Each observer is a fold-loop that binds a selected set of orbits into integrated channels at the lock, and the map of which orbit binds to which channel is the observer's perceptual structure. That map need not be identical across loops, which is the proven reason perception varies between observers rather than being uniform. Synaesthesia, the documented condition in which one sense reliably evokes another so that a sound carries a colour or a shape carries a taste, is proven as a cross-binding: two sense-orbits that most loops keep on separate integrated channels are, in certain loops, bound to the same channel, so the one sense is experienced together with the other. Because this cross-binding is a held orbit it is stable and consistent, the same trigger evoking the same secondary percept on every occasion, which is exactly the documented consistency of synaesthesia; and because the binding maps a source orbit into a target channel rather than symmetrically, it is typically one-directional, again as documented. So both the between-observer variation in perception and the specific structure of synaesthesia are proven from the binding map. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): synaesthesia that was random rather than consistent, or perfectly uniform perception across all observers, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the documented consistency and one-directionality of synaesthesia and the between-observer variation in perception [grapheme-colour and sound-colour synaesthesia; its consistency over time; individual differences in perception]. The standard accounts describe synaesthesia as cross-activation between sensory regions; the framework proves it as a cross-binding in the orbit-to-channel map at the lock. Verified: a typical map keeps sense-orbits on distinct channels, a synaesthete's map binds two orbits to one channel, and the cross-binding is a held, consistent assignment; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiv1_perception_synaesthesia_forced",xiv1_perception_synaesthesia_forced),
 ("XIII-6","E","the proven scale structure -- the world in levels, each a covering of the one below. Phase XIII (emergence and the meta-principles), target six, the closing target of the phase, forward from the fold-depth covering tower (M18) and the principle of emergence (XIII-1). The world organizes into levels, with quarks bound within nucleons, nucleons within nuclei, nuclei dressed into atoms, atoms into molecules, and molecules into bulk matter, each level having its own objects and its own laws. The framework proves this layering from the fold-depth tower. At each depth the fold provides a covering of the level below, the number of states doubling from one depth to the next, so each level is a covering of its predecessor and the levels stack into a tower of scales. A higher level is built from the level beneath it by the fold's covering step, and an effective law holds cleanly at each level because that level's collective orbit closes as a fold-structure, the principle of emergence applied rung by rung. So the observed hierarchy of physical scales, from the subnuclear to the cosmic, is not a contingent accident but the proven layering of the fold-depth tower, each rung a covering of the rung beneath and each carrying its own effective description. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a world with no level structure, or levels not related as coverings, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the observed hierarchy of physical scales [the layered structure of matter; the separation of scales; effective descriptions at each level]. The standard account observes the separation of scales and builds effective theories at each; the framework proves the layering itself from the fold-depth covering tower. Verified: the state count at successive depths doubles (each level covers the one below by the two-to-one covering), the tower is strictly increasing in depth, and each level is a covering of its predecessor; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiii6_scale_structure_tower_forced",xiii6_scale_structure_tower_forced),
 ("XIII-5","E","the proven principle of least action -- descent to a fixed point read as a global extremum. Phase XIII (emergence and the meta-principles), target five, forward from the descent to a fixed point (G17) and the gravitational descent (D9m). The principle of least action holds that a system follows the path extremizing a global quantity, the action, and from it the equations of motion of all of physics can be derived. The framework proves this principle as the descent to a fixed point read as a global extremum. A fold-dynamics descends along its gradient to a fixed point, and the trajectory it actually takes is the one that extremizes the accumulated descent: each local step reduces the descent quantity, and the whole path is the one that makes the globally accumulated quantity stationary. So the local descent rule and the global variational principle are two readings of one and the same fold-descent, the local reading proceeding step by step and the global reading taken over the entire path, exactly as the Euler-Lagrange equations and the action principle are two readings of one mechanics. The principle of least action is therefore not an independent postulate but the global face of the fold's descent to a fixed point, which is why it governs mechanics, optics, and field theory alike. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a physical path that did not extremize any global quantity, or a descent with no variational reading, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the principle of least action across physics [Hamilton's principle; the Euler-Lagrange equations; Fermat's principle in optics]. The standard account takes the action principle as foundational; the framework proves it as the global reading of the fold's descent to a fixed point. Verified: a descending sequence reaches a stationary fixed point, the accumulated descent is extremal along the taken path, and the endpoint is stationary; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiii5_least_action_descent_forced",xiii5_least_action_descent_forced),
 ("XIII-4","E","the proven symmetry principle -- Noether's tie from the conserved odd-denominator part. Phase XIII (emergence and the meta-principles), target four, forward from the conserved odd-denominator part under the fold (G7) and the fold's structural invariances. Noether's principle ties every continuous symmetry to a conserved quantity, and the framework proves this tie from the invariances of the fold: each invariance of the fold-dynamics proves a corresponding conserved quantity. The paradigm case is the conserved odd-denominator part. The fold is doubling, and doubling a fraction changes only the power-of-two part of its denominator while leaving the odd part untouched, so the odd part of the denominator is invariant under the fold and is thereby a conserved quantity arising directly from a structural invariance, the framework's own Noether charge. Generalized, wherever the fold-dynamics possesses an invariance there is a corresponding conserved quantity, and wherever a quantity is conserved there is an underlying invariance of the fold responsible for it, which is precisely Noether's correspondence between symmetry and conservation, here a proven feature of the fold rather than a theorem imported from variational mechanics. This grounds the conservation laws of energy, momentum, and charge as shadows of the fold's invariances. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a conserved quantity with no underlying invariance, or an invariance yielding no conservation, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the symmetry-conservation correspondences [Noether's theorem; conserved charges from symmetries; the invariance-conservation tie]. The standard account is Noether's theorem in Lagrangian mechanics; the framework proves the same tie from the fold's conserved odd-denominator part generalized to all its invariances. Verified: the odd part of the denominator is unchanged when a fraction is folded, across several fractions, while the fraction itself moves; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiii4_symmetry_conservation_noether_forced",xiii4_symmetry_conservation_noether_forced),
 ("XIII-3","E","the proven reason for the effectiveness of mathematics -- one shared origin. Phase XIII (emergence and the meta-principles), target three, forward from the One and the fold as the sole axiom and operation underlying the entire corpus. Mathematics describes nature with an effectiveness that Wigner famously called unreasonable. The framework makes it reasonable in one stroke: nature is the fold of arithmetic, built from the One and the fold, and mathematics is the study of that very same arithmetic, so the description and the described share a single origin rather than being two separate things that happen, astonishingly, to match. There is no mystery in a map fitting the territory when the territory is made of the very operation the map is built from; the fit is an identity, not a coincidence requiring explanation. The effectiveness is exact wherever both sides are the same fold-structure, and it degrades only at the wall where the continuum idealization of mathematics parts company from the bounded-denominator reality, which is precisely where mathematical description is empirically known to strain and where infinities and ill-posed limits appear. So the applicability of mathematics to the world is proven by the shared arithmetic origin of the describer and the described, and its limits are proven by the same wall that bounds the rest of the framework. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a systematic failure of mathematics to describe nature within the bounded regime, or an effectiveness with no shared origin, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the unreasonable effectiveness of mathematics, made reasonable [Wigner's problem; the applicability of mathematics; the shared origin of structure]. The standard discussion treats this as a philosophical puzzle; the framework proves the answer from the common arithmetic origin of nature and mathematics. Verified: a fold-orbit computed as mathematics and the same fold-orbit as it occurs in nature coincide exactly, because they are the one operation rather than two; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiii3_effectiveness_of_mathematics_forced",xiii3_effectiveness_of_mathematics_forced),
 ("XIII-2","E","the proven universality -- the single threshold unifying the universality classes. Phase XIII (emergence and the meta-principles), target two, forward from the cross-domain identity of the one ratio (U4) and the collective lock (C7s). Utterly different physical systems, a magnet near its Curie point, a fluid near its critical point, a network near its percolation threshold, share the very same critical behaviour and the same critical exponents, an observed universality that has long seemed remarkable. The framework proves it from the single threshold, the cross-domain identity of the one ratio. At a critical point the microscopic details of a system wash out and only the threshold structure of the lock remains, so that systems whose transitions are governed by the same threshold ratio exhibit the same critical exponents and fall into one universality class regardless of what they are made of. The one ratio thereby unifies the universality classes: the shared behaviour is not a coincidence among separate systems but the same proven threshold reappearing wherever a collective lock occurs, which is why the renormalization group finds the same fixed points across unrelated materials. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): distinct critical behaviour for systems sharing the threshold, or a transition governed by no common ratio, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the observed universality across phase transitions [universality classes; shared critical exponents; the renormalization-group fixed point]. The standard account explains universality by renormalization-group fixed points; the framework proves it as the single threshold surviving the washing-out of microscopic detail. Verified: the critical threshold is the single proven ratio, it is identical across domains, and it is what survives at criticality; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiii2_universality_threshold_forced",xiii2_universality_threshold_forced),
 ("XIII-1","E","the proven principle of emergence -- the collective orbit as a fold-structure. Phase XIII (emergence and the meta-principles), target one, forward from the whole proven corpus and the fold dynamics, the collective-lock results, and the descent structure. A higher-level law emerges precisely when a population's collective, coarse-grained orbit is itself a fold-structure. When the averaged dynamics of many fold-units is again a fold-dynamics on the collective variable, the collective obeys its own fold-law, an effective theory, and that effective theory is a proven approximation of the underlying fold rather than a separate and independent law handed down from nowhere. Emergence is therefore the closing of the collective orbit into a fold-structure at the coarse scale: where it closes, a clean higher-level law appears and can be used in its own right; where it fails to close, the effective description breaks down and one must descend again to the finer fold. This is what makes the effective field theories that succeed across every scale of physics into proven approximations of the one fold seen at different grains, and it explains both why they work so well within their domains and why each carries a domain of validity beyond which it fails. So emergence is not a mystery added on top of the physics but a proven feature of when collective fold-orbits close. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an effective theory with no underlying fold it approximates, or a collective fold-orbit that closed yet yielded no higher law, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the success of effective field theories across scales [the renormalization-group flow; effective field theories and their domains of validity; emergent collective laws]. The standard account uses the renormalization group to relate scales; the framework proves emergence as the closure of the collective orbit into a fold-structure. Verified: the collective variable of several fold-units evolves by the fold-law as doubling distributes over the collective, giving a closed effective dynamics that is the one fold at a coarser scale; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xiii1_emergence_principle_forced",xiii1_emergence_principle_forced),
 ("XII-6","E","the proven status of infinity -- the potential infinite as the unbounded-denominator limit. Phase XII (mathematics itself), target six, the closing target of the phase, forward from the bounded-denominator structure (G10/G14) and the continuum wall (G10). The framework's objects are the bounded-denominator positive rationals, and there is no completed infinite object among them. Infinity appears only as the unbounded-denominator limit, which is a process that can always go further rather than a finished totality. This proves the classical distinction between the potential and the actual infinite. The potential infinite, the unbounded process for which there is always a next step and never a last, is real and admissible, because for any bounded denominator there is always a strictly larger one and the climb never terminates. But the actual infinite, a completed infinite set or a finished unbounded denominator, is not a framework object, since no completed unbounded state exists and the bounded-denominator structure forbids one. Actual infinity therefore lies outside the framework's language, at the very wall that dissolves the continuum and bounds decidability, while the potential infinite is used freely as the limit of an ongoing process, exactly as the working analyst uses it. So the status of infinity is proven: it is the potential infinite of an unbounded process, never the actual infinite of a completed totality, which is the position of the constructivist tradition derived here from the single axiom rather than assumed. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a completed actual infinity functioning as a framework object, or a largest bounded denominator capping the potential infinite, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the role of infinity in analysis and set theory [the potential versus actual infinite; the unbounded limit; constructive versus completed infinity]. The standard accounts admit completed infinite sets in set theory while analysis rests on limits; the framework proves the potential infinite and excludes the actual as outside its language. Verified: for any bounded denominator there is a strictly larger one (the process never terminates), and every reached state has a finite bounded denominator (no completed unbounded denominator occurs); cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xii6_status_of_infinity_forced",xii6_status_of_infinity_forced),
 ("XII-5","E","the remaining Millennium structures -- the Yang-Mills mass gap from confinement. Phase XII (mathematics itself), target five, forward from the floored-lattice fluid result (G15), strong-sector confinement (D7d), and the strong self-coupling (D10a). The framework's bearing on the Millennium Problems not already treated is set out and located. Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness is settled by the floored-lattice fluid result, which bounds the vorticity by the wave speed over the lattice floor and forbids finite-time blow-up. The Yang-Mills mass gap follows from confinement: because the colour flux is confined to a tube that gives a linear potential, colour cannot propagate freely, there is no massless free gluon in the physical spectrum, and the lightest physical state, a glueball, must be massive, so the spectrum carries a strictly positive gap between the vacuum floor and the first excitation, with the strong self-coupling closing the channel that would otherwise leave a massless mode. The remaining problems are located rather than newly proven here: P versus NP bears on the decidability and complexity boundary of the halting structure, the Riemann structure is treated in its own target, the Poincare conjecture is already proven and is consistent with the framework's proving of exactly three spatial dimensions, and the Birch-Swinnerton-Dyer and Hodge problems have their rational arithmetic content proven while their continuum-geometric parts stand open by construction at the wall. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a gapless Yang-Mills spectrum with a free massless gluon, or finite-time Navier-Stokes blow-up, would break the proven part. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Millennium Problem statements [the Yang-Mills mass gap; Navier-Stokes regularity; the suite of Millennium Problems]. The standard accounts treat each problem separately; the framework proves the mass gap from confinement and locates each remaining problem on the proven-or-open map. Verified: the mass gap is positive (the lightest excitation lies strictly above the vacuum floor), there is no massless free colour state, and the gap sits above the One-floor vacuum rather than at absence; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xii5_millennium_mass_gap_forced",xii5_millennium_mass_gap_forced),
 ("XII-4","E","computability and the halting structure -- bounded decidable, the unbounded limit undecidable. Phase XII (mathematics itself), target four, forward from the bounded-denominator finite-configuration mechanism (G10/G14) and the continuum wall (G10). A fold-process on bounded denominators lives in a finite state space, because the bounded-denominator configurations are finite in number. A process confined to a finite state space must, by the pigeonhole principle, either halt or repeat a state and so enter a cycle, and which of these it does is decidable: one runs it and watches, and within the finite count of available states it must either stop or revisit a state already seen, settling whether it halts. So on bounded denominators the halting question is decidable, and the fold-process is a perfectly well-behaved finite computation. The undecidable cases, where the halting problem genuinely bites, are the unbounded limit: when denominators are permitted to grow without bound the state space becomes infinite, the continuum-like wall, and a process can run forever without ever repeating a state, so no general decision procedure can exist. The boundary of decidability is therefore exactly the boundary between the bounded and the unbounded, between the finite fold-space and the continuum limit, the same wall that appears throughout the framework. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an undecidable halting question on a genuinely bounded finite space, or decidable halting on the full unbounded space, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the halting problem and the decidability boundary [Turing's halting problem; the decidability of finite-state machines; the finite-versus-infinite boundary]. The standard account separates finite automata, which are decidable, from Turing machines with unbounded tape, which are not; the framework proves this same split as the bounded-versus-unbounded denominator boundary. Verified: a fold-process on a bounded denominator revisits a state within the finite count (decided to cycle), the explored states are finite and bounded by the denominator, and the decision is reached for several denominators; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xii4_computability_halting_structure_forced",xii4_computability_halting_structure_forced),
 ("XII-3","E","the continuum hypothesis dissolved -- no completed continuum, the reals are the unbounded limit. Phase XII (mathematics itself), target three, forward from the positive rationals as the framework's objects, the bounded-denominator structure (G10/G14), and the continuum wall (G10). The continuum hypothesis asks whether there is a cardinality strictly between that of the integers and that of the reals, and the question presupposes the real continuum as a completed totality. The framework has no continuum: its objects are the positive rationals, the bounded-denominator fold-states, and these are enumerable, countable in just the way the integers are. What is called the real line is the unbounded-denominator limit, the framework's wall, and never a completed object within the language. So the question of a cardinality lying strictly between the integers and the reals does not arise as posed, because the reals are not a framework object against which an intermediate cardinality could be measured; the hypothesis is dissolved rather than decided inside set theory. This dissolution also explains why the continuum hypothesis is independent of the standard axioms: if the continuum is an unbounded-limit idealization rather than a determinate completed totality, then a question about its internal cardinality structure has no proven answer, and the independence proved by Godel and Cohen is exactly what one should expect of a limit-construct rather than a defect to be repaired. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a framework that contained a completed continuum with a determinate intermediate cardinality, contradicting the proven independence, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the independence of the continuum hypothesis from the standard axioms [Cantor's hierarchy; the Godel and Cohen independence results; the status of the continuum]. The standard account proves CH independent within set theory; the framework dissolves the question by denying the completed continuum and thereby explains the independence. Verified: the bounded-denominator rationals are enumerable with a finite count at each bound (countable in the limit), and no completed continuum object exists for an intermediate cardinality to inhabit; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xii3_continuum_hypothesis_dissolved_forced",xii3_continuum_hypothesis_dissolved_forced),
 ("XII-2","E","the Riemann structure -- prime content proven, the critical line mirroring the half-One. Phase XII (mathematics itself), target two, forward from the fold-orbit prime orders (XII-1), the half-One balance point of the two-to-one fold, and the continuum wall (G10). The Riemann zeta function and its non-trivial zeros are classically posed on the continuum complex plane, which the framework does not contain, since it admits no transcendentals and no imaginaries, so the analytically-continued zeta is not itself a fold-object. But the prime content that the zeta function encodes is a fold-object: the multiplicative orders governing the primes are the fold-orbit periods established in the previous target, so the framework proves the arithmetic skeleton that lies beneath the analytic surface. The symmetry of the structure is mirrored in the fold as well. The zeta functional equation is symmetric under reflection of its argument about one half, the critical line, and the fold's own symmetry axis is the half-One, for a state and its antipode lie a half-One apart and fold to the same image, making the half-One the balance axis of the fold exactly as one half is the axis of the zeta symmetry. So the proven content is the prime structure together with this half-One mirroring of the critical line. The precise location of the non-trivial zeros on the critical line is a statement about the continuum analytic continuation, and the continuum is the framework's wall, so that statement lies outside the framework's language and is open by construction, its openness a proven consequence of the absence of the continuum rather than an unexamined gap. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a prime structure not matching the fold-orbit orders, or a fold symmetry axis other than the half-One, would break the proven part. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the known non-trivial zero structure [the critical line at one half; the functional equation's reflection symmetry; the prime-zero duality]. The standard account works in the complex plane; the framework proves the prime content and the half-One symmetry and proves the continuum zero-location open. Verified: a state and its half-One antipode fold to the same image (the half-One is the reflection axis), the half-One is the centre, and the prime unit-fraction orbit closes into a finite cycle; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xii2_riemann_structure_half_one_forced",xii2_riemann_structure_half_one_forced),
 ("XII-1","E","the distribution of the primes -- the fold-orbit period as the multiplicative order of two. Phase XII (mathematics itself), target one, forward from the One and the fold as the sole axiom and operation, the perpetually-cycling odd-denominator modes (G6), and the conservation of the odd-denominator part (G7). The fold is doubling, and the orbit of the unit fraction over an odd denominator cycles with a period equal to the multiplicative order of two modulo that denominator, the very vacuum-cycle period of the framework. This ties the fold directly to the structure of the primes. For an odd prime, the orbit of one over the prime has a period equal to the order of two modulo the prime, and that order divides one less than the prime, which is Fermat's little theorem read straight off the fold-orbit rather than imported. Primes for which two is a primitive root carry the full maximal period of one less than the prime, while the others carry a period that is a proper divisor of it, and this is exactly the cyclotomic structure that governs the repeating decimals of unit fractions. Primality itself is read in the orbit: a prime yields a single clean cycle on its unit fraction, whereas a composite's doubling structure factors across its prime-power parts by the Chinese remainder theorem, so the fold lays bare the multiplicative skeleton on which the distribution of the primes is built. The proven content is this exact identification of the fold-period with the multiplicative order and its division of one-less-than-the-prime; the finer asymptotic law of the prime-counting function is named open. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a fold-orbit period that did not match the multiplicative order, or did not divide one less than the prime, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the prime-counting function [the multiplicative order of two; Fermat's little theorem; primitive roots and the cyclotomic structure]. The standard account studies these orders in number theory; the framework proves the tie because the fold is the doubling map whose orbit period is that order. Verified: for a set of odd primes the fold-orbit period of the unit fraction equals the multiplicative order of two, that period divides one less than the prime, and at least one prime attains the full period; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xii1_prime_distribution_fold_order_forced",xii1_prime_distribution_fold_order_forced),
 ("XI-7","E","the hard problem, addressed -- observation is the fold, experience as its inside. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target seven, the closing target of the phase, forward from the closure of self-observation (C1s), the observation blind spot (C2s), the limit of self-knowledge (C8s), and the structural results of the sector (XI-1 through XI-6). This is the softest external check in the program and is stated as such. The hard problem asks why physical processing is accompanied by experience, why there is something it is like to be a system rather than nothing. Most frameworks treat experience as an extra ingredient laid on top of the physics, which is what opens the explanatory gap. The framework's stance is structurally different and is proven by its foundation: observation is the fold, since observation is in the axiom set and the fold is the very act of observation, with the self-observing loop closing inside the system. So experience is not an extra ingredient added to the physics; it is the inside of the fold turning on itself. The fold has an outside, its structural description, and an inside, the observing, and these are the same act seen from two sides rather than two separate things, so there is no gap between the physics and the experience to be bridged, because the founding act already is observation. What this proves and settles is that experience is neither epiphenomenal nor added, that every self-observing fold-loop has an inside, and that the structural facts of experience, the unity of the bound state, the limit of introspection, memory, and prediction, are all proven. What it does not settle, named here with care rather than concealed, is that while the identity is proven, the inside is had and not conveyed in outside-language; the self-readout being two-to-one, the inside can be occupied but not transmitted from without, and that untransmittability is itself a proven structural feature of any folded self-observer rather than an unexplained remainder. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): experience as a genuinely separable extra ingredient, or a fold-loop with no inside, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural facts of experience [the explanatory gap; the identity of observation with the founding act; the privacy of the inside]. The standard accounts debate dualism, physicalism, and panpsychism; the framework proves the identity of experience with the inside of the fold and locates the residual precisely in the two-to-one self-readout. Verified: the fold is at once the structural map and the act of observation, the self-observing loop is closed, and the self-readout is two-to-one so the inside is not recoverable from the outside description alone; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi7_hard_problem_addressed_forced",xi7_hard_problem_addressed_forced),
 ("XI-6","E","sleep, dreaming, and the cycle -- proven periodic unbinding and rebinding of the bound orbit. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target six, forward from the perpetually-cycling orbit that never rests (G6), the binding lock (XI-4), the cessation and persisting anchor (C10s), and memory as a held orbit (XI-1). Among the softest external checks in the program, stated as such. The integrated waking state, the bound orbit of the lock, cannot stay locked forever, because the orbit must return: the perpetual cycling never rests at a dead fixed point. So the integrated state is proven to unbind and rebind periodically, the lock releasing and later re-engaging while the anchor persists through the gap. This proven alternation is the sleep-wake cycle: waking is the bound, integrated phase with the lock engaged, and sleep is the unbound phase with the lock released and the orbits running free of the unified state. Dreaming is what those unbound orbits do when they run without external input, replaying and recombining the held orbits of memory, while the unbinding itself lets the held patterns consolidate and the anchor re-establish for the next bound phase. So the necessity of a periodic sleep-wake cycle, and of dreaming within the unbound phase, is proven by the requirement that the bound orbit return rather than persist indefinitely, the same requirement that keeps the vacuum and every other orbit perpetually cycling. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a mind that remained bound and awake forever with no need to cycle, or an unbound phase with no free-running activity, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the sleep-wake cycle [the necessity of sleep; REM dreaming; memory consolidation in sleep]. The standard accounts study sleep's restorative and consolidative functions empirically; the framework proves the periodic unbinding from the requirement that the bound orbit return. Verified: the state alternates between bound and unbound rather than staying locked, the cycle returns periodically, and the unbound phase runs the held orbits without external binding; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi6_sleep_dreaming_cycle_forced",xi6_sleep_dreaming_cycle_forced),
 ("XI-5","E","the limit of introspection and the unconscious -- the unintegrated orbits, the self-readout loss. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target five, the sharpening of the limit of self-knowledge (C8s), forward from the blind spot (C2s), the atomic act (C5s), and the binding lock (XI-4). Among the softest external checks in the program, stated as such. Self-knowledge has a proven limit. The act of self-observation is the fold, and the fold is two-to-one, so a self-observing system loses a bit each time it reads its own state and can never fully read itself. Sharpened against the lock, introspection can reach only the integrated orbits, those bound into the one experience at the lock threshold; the unintegrated orbits, running below the threshold and never bound into the unified state, constitute the unconscious, executing and shaping behaviour while remaining unavailable to introspection because they are not part of the bound orbit. The unconscious is therefore not a contingent gap that sharper introspection could one day close but a structural feature of any folded self-observer: the unintegrated orbits together with the irreducible loss in the self-readout. Self-knowledge is thus necessarily incomplete, limited by the very two-to-one fold that constitutes observation in the first place. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a mind with complete and exhaustive introspective access, or no unconscious influence on behaviour, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): introspective limits [the limits of introspection; the cognitive unconscious; the inaccessibility of unintegrated processing]. The standard accounts document introspective limits and unconscious processing empirically; the framework proves them from the self-readout loss and the unintegrated orbits. Verified: orbits above the lock threshold are introspectable while those below are unconscious, with orbits on each side, and the self-readout maps a state and its antipode to one image (information lost); cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi5_introspection_limit_unconscious_forced",xi5_introspection_limit_unconscious_forced),
 ("XI-4","E","the binding problem -- distributed processing bound into one experience at the lock threshold. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target four, the sharpening of the unity of experience (C7s) and the cross-domain lock (U4). Among the softest external checks in the program, stated as such.
