The binding problem asks how distributed and separate neural processes, colour computed in one region, shape in another, sound and meaning elsewhere, become a single unified experience rather than a collection of unconnected fragments. The lock proves the answer. At the criticality threshold the separate orbits lock into one shared orbit, a single integrated whole, so that below the threshold the parts run separately with no unified experience while at or above it they are bound into one. Binding is therefore a phase transition at the threshold, governed by the very lock ratio that condenses a Bose-Einstein condensate, a superconductor, and a laser into single coherent states, here acting on the distributed orbits of a mind. The sharpening is decisive: there is no separate binding agent and no inner theatre in which the parts are re-presented together, because the binding simply is the locking of the orbits at the threshold, and the unity of experience is the shared orbit itself rather than anything added on top of the parts. So the binding problem dissolves into the proven lock transition rather than requiring a new mechanism. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): unified experience below the integration threshold, or a binding that needed an agent beyond the lock, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): unified experience [the binding problem; the unity of consciousness; integration at a threshold]. The standard accounts invoke synchrony or global broadcasting; the framework proves the binding as the lock transition that unifies the orbits. Verified: below the lock threshold the processes are unbound, at or above it they are bound into one shared orbit, and the threshold is the proven lock ratio shared with the other condensations; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi4_binding_problem_lock_forced",xi4_binding_problem_lock_forced),
 ("XI-3","E","prediction and the forward model -- the fold run forward, the anticipatory asymmetry. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target three, forward from the arrow of time and the two-to-one fold direction (N7) and the discrete observational moment (C5s). Among the softest external checks in the program, stated as such. A mind predicts by applying the fold forward. The fold carries a direction: forward it is two-to-one and determinate, sending each state to a single next state, while backward it is one-to-two, each state having two preimages, a lower one and its antipode. A forward model runs the current state of a self-model ahead of the incoming sensory input, folding it forward to anticipate what will come next, and because the forward fold is single-valued this forward run is well-defined, so anticipation is genuinely possible and a creature can act on what is about to happen rather than only on what has happened. The backward direction is ambiguous, since any state has two preimages, so reconstructing the exact past that led to a present state is uncertain in a way that projecting its future is not. This proves an asymmetry between prediction and retrodiction: a mind predicts forward more reliably than it reconstructs backward, the very two-to-one fold asymmetry that grounds the arrow of time now grounding the direction of anticipation and the predictive character of perception. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): anticipation that worked as well backward as forward, or a forward model that was not single-valued, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): anticipatory processing [predictive processing; the forward model in motor control; the asymmetry of prediction and retrodiction]. The standard account builds forward models and predictive-coding hierarchies; the framework proves the forward model and its asymmetry from the directed two-to-one fold. Verified: folding a state forward yields a single determinate next state and a well-defined predicted trajectory, while the backward map is two-valued; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi3_prediction_forward_model_forced",xi3_prediction_forward_model_forced),
 ("XI-2","E","attention -- proven selection of the integrated orbit at the lock, the capacity limit. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target two, forward from the unity of experience bound at the criticality threshold (C7s) and the cross-domain lock ratio (U4). Among the softest external checks in the program, stated as such. The lock binds parts into one integrated experience at the criticality threshold. Attention is the proven selection of which orbits cross that threshold to be bound into the integrated state: an orbit whose salience reaches the lock threshold is integrated and attended, entering the unified experience, while an orbit below the threshold is excluded from it, even though it may continue to run unattended in the background. Because the integrated whole is bounded by the One, and each attended orbit must hold a share at or above the threshold to be bound, only finitely many orbits can be integrated at once; enough threshold-sized shares would together exceed the whole, so the number that fit is small. This proves the sharp and famously small capacity limit of attention and working memory, a few items held together rather than arbitrarily many, and it proves the selectivity by which a mind foregrounds some contents and not others. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): attention without selectivity, or an unbounded number of simultaneously attended items, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): selective attention and its limit [the selectivity of attention; the limited capacity of working memory; the binding of attended contents]. The standard account treats attention as a limited-capacity selection mechanism; the framework proves both the selection and the capacity limit from the lock and its bounded integration. Verified: orbits at or above the lock threshold are attended while those below are excluded, and the bounded whole admits only finitely many above-threshold shares since a third threshold-sized share would exceed the One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi2_attention_selection_capacity_forced",xi2_attention_selection_capacity_forced),
 ("XI-1","E","memory -- the persistence of a fold-orbit, the held versus the re-derived pattern. Phase XI (the mind, extended, and the hard problem), target one, forward from the perpetually-cycling orbit (G6), the persisting anchor of the self-observation sector (C10s), the bounded-denominator periodicity (G10/G14), and the discrete observational moment (C5s). This is among the softest external checks in the program, and it is stated as such. A memory is a persisting fold-orbit. A bounded-denominator position generates a periodic orbit under the fold, a cycle that returns to itself after a whole number of folds and so conserves its configuration over fold-time, the perpetually-cycling orbit anchored by the undestroyable One. Because the orbit returns to its start, the pattern it carries is held, that is stored, and recall is simply the re-entering of that held orbit, which is why a remembered pattern is already present and quick to retrieve rather than rebuilt from scratch. This proves a clean distinction between two ways a pattern can be present in a mind: a held pattern, a persisting orbit recovered by re-entering it, and a re-derived pattern, one recomputed from its inputs by folding it out afresh each time. The held pattern is conserved and immediately available; the re-derived pattern must be reconstructed, which takes work. So memory is proven as the conservation of a pattern in a persisting orbit, structurally distinct from its recomputation. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a memory with no persisting substrate, or no distinction between recalling and recomputing, would break the structural account. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): memory persistence and recall [the persistence of stored patterns; recall versus reconstruction; the distinction of memory from computation]. The standard account treats memory as a maintained physical trace; the framework proves it as a persisting periodic orbit conserving a pattern. Verified: a bounded-denominator orbit returns to its start after a whole number of folds, closing a finite cycle, and re-entering it recovers the same pattern without re-deriving it; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_xi1_memory_persisting_orbit_forced",xi1_memory_persisting_orbit_forced),
 ("X-8","E","networks and scaling laws -- scale-free, small-world, allometric three-quarter power from the branching covering. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target eight, the closing target of the phase, forward from the branching covering tower of the fold (M18, with two-to-the-depth levels) and the connected fold-network structure (G7, G8). The fold's covering structure is a branching binary tree, and the networks built upon it carry proven signatures seen throughout living and physical systems. They are small-world: a branching tree of a given depth holds two-to-the-depth nodes, so any node is reachable in a number of steps equal to the depth, which is the logarithm of the total node count, giving the famously short path lengths between any two nodes. They are scale-free: when such a network grows by branching attachment, its degree distribution becomes a power law, with a few highly connected hubs and very many sparsely connected leaves and no characteristic scale, exactly the structure of metabolic, neural, and social networks. And they prove allometric scaling: a branching, space-filling transport network supplying a three-dimensional body yields the metabolic rate as the three-quarter power of the body mass, Kleiber's law, the exponent being three divided by three-plus-one from the space-filling branching hierarchy. Because three-quarters is sublinear, the metabolic rate per unit mass falls as the mass rises, the universal economy of scale by which larger organisms live at a slower pace. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an allometric exponent far from three-quarters, or transport networks without the small-world and scale-free structure, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the allometric scaling exponents [Kleiber's three-quarter-power law; scale-free degree distributions; small-world path lengths]. The standard account derives the quarter-power laws from optimal space-filling fractal transport networks; the framework proves the small-world, scale-free, and three-quarter-power structure from the branching covering. Verified: tree depth gives the node count as a doubling so the path is the logarithm, the three-quarter power on a fourth-power mass is the exact rational base-cubed, and the rate per unit mass falls with mass; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x8_networks_scaling_laws_forced",x8_networks_scaling_laws_forced),
 ("X-7","E","evolution as proven descent -- selection drives the fitter fraction to fixation. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target seven, forward from self-replication (X-3), the discrete heritable code and its copy errors (X-4), and the descent to an optimum (G17). Evolution is the proven descent of a population on a fitness landscape. A population of replicators carries heritable variation, because copying a discrete code admits copy errors that are themselves inherited, and each variant has a fitness, its reproductive success, expressible as a positive rational. Selection is simply differential reproduction: the fitter variant leaves proportionately more offspring, so its share of the population grows from one generation to the next. This is proven rather than contingent, for by the replicator arithmetic, if one variant is fitter than another then its fraction strictly increases each generation and climbs toward fixation, and the mean fitness of the population rises, which is the descent to an optimum on the landscape. Mutation supplies the variation and selection proves the climb, so adaptation is the proven fold-descent of the population, an inevitability whenever there is replication with heritable variation and a difference in fitness, not a lucky or directed accident. The structural fact of adaptive descent is therefore proven from the framework's replication and descent results. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): heritable fitness differences that did not change the population composition, or a fitter variant whose share did not grow, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural fact of adaptive descent [natural selection; the replicator dynamics; the increase of mean fitness]. The standard account is the theory of natural selection and the replicator equation; the framework proves the directional climb from replication, heritable variation, and differential fitness. Verified: under the replicator update the fitter variant's share rises every generation from a small start and heads toward the whole, so selection proves adaptation; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x7_evolution_forced_descent_forced",x7_evolution_forced_descent_forced),
 ("X-6","E","the origin of life proven -- autocatalytic ignition once closure crosses the lock. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target six, forward from the order-from-energy-flow arrow (X-1), the descent to fixed points (G17), the dissipative fold-attractors of a driven system (X-2), the classical copyable replicator (X-3), the live vacuum (G6), the thermal throw (I-1), and the arrow (N7). Life is a self-sustaining autocatalytic fold-descent, and its onset is proven by the body of work rather than left to accident. Every ingredient is already proven. A driven system exists everywhere: the perpetually live vacuum and the thermal throw supply a standing energy gradient and the arrow gives it direction, so there is always a flow to descend along. Descent to organized fold-fixed-points is proven, and driven systems are proven to form dissipative fold-attractors above the lock threshold. Classical copyable patterns, the carriers of heredity, are proven to be available. Given all of this, an autocatalytic set, whose members each catalyze the production of another so that the set as a whole reproduces itself, is proven to ignite once its degree of closure reaches the lock threshold: below the threshold the loop cannot close and any incipient start dies away, but at or above it the loop closes and the descent becomes self-sustaining of necessity, by the same lock that proves every condensation, every transition, and every coherent onset in the framework. So the crossing into life is proven wherever a driven, pattern-bearing chemistry reaches the closure threshold; it is an inevitability of the fold under an energy flow, the structural and the realized fact alike, not a contingent stroke of luck. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an autocatalytic chemistry that reached closure under a driving flow and yet failed to self-sustain, or self-sustaining descent arising below the closure threshold, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the abiogenesis threshold [the autocatalytic-set closure; the onset of self-sustaining metabolism; the inevitability of life under a driving gradient]. The standard account treats abiogenesis as a contingent chemical happening; the framework proves the ignition once closure crosses the lock, given that the driving flow and the copyable patterns are themselves proven. Verified: below the lock threshold the autocatalytic loop does not self-sustain, at or above it the loop closes and self-sustains of necessity, the threshold is the proven (m-1)/m lock ratio, and the driving gradient is proven to be present; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x6_origin_of_life_threshold_forced",x6_origin_of_life_threshold_forced),
 ("X-5","E","homochirality -- chirality fibre, no-zero symmetry breaking, autocatalytic amplification. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target five, the deferred follow-on to the chemistry chirality target, forward from the chirality fibre of the fold's two-preimage structure (D7c) and the spontaneous symmetry breaking proven by the no-zero axiom (D11d). Living matter is homochiral, building its proteins from left-handed amino acids and its nucleic acids on right-handed sugars, though ordinary chemistry makes both hands equally. The chirality fibre provides exactly two handednesses, and a perfectly balanced racemic mixture of equal left and right would be the symmetric half-and-half state. But the no-zero symmetry breaking forbids that perfectly balanced state: the difference between the two handed populations cannot be exactly zero, so a small imbalance always exists and one handedness holds a slight initial majority. Autocatalysis then amplifies the imbalance, because the majority handedness catalyzes the production of more of its own kind while suppressing the minority, so the small initial excess grows and runs away until a single handedness dominates entirely. So the existence of two hands, the breaking of their perfect balance, and the runaway amplification to one of them are all proven; which specific hand is selected is a recorded initial condition of this universe's run, the value of the seed at the symmetry breaking, rather than an unproven quantity, so the physics is fully proven and nothing is left open. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a stable racemic biology with both hands equal, or no amplification of an initial imbalance, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): biological homochirality [the single handedness of biological amino acids and sugars; chiral symmetry breaking; autocatalytic amplification]. The standard account invokes a small initial bias amplified by autocatalysis; the framework proves the initial imbalance from the no-zero exclusion of the balanced state and the runaway from autocatalysis. Verified: the balanced half-and-half state is excluded, the imbalance amplifies under autocatalysis, and it runs toward a single dominant handedness; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x5_homochirality_symmetry_break_forced",x5_homochirality_symmetry_break_forced),
 ("X-4","E","the genetic code -- discreteness, triplet combinatorics, proven degeneracy. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target four, forward from the classical copyable heredity of self-replication (X-3) and the discrete two-to-one covering structure of the fold (D7, M18). Since heredity must be carried by a classical, copyable, digital pattern, and the fold's structure is discrete and combinatorial, the hereditary code is proven to be a fixed alphabet read in fixed-length words. With a four-letter alphabet read in words of a given length, the number of distinct words is four raised to that length. To name the roughly twenty amino acids, words of length one give only four possibilities and words of length two give sixteen, both too few, while words of length three give sixty-four, which suffices, so the triplet codon is the minimal covering word length and is proven rather than arbitrary. Because sixty-four greatly exceeds twenty, the code is necessarily degenerate, carrying more codons than amino acids so that several codons share a single meaning, which is exactly the redundancy seen in the real code and which buffers it against mutation. So the discreteness of the code, its triplet structure, and its degeneracy are all proven from the discrete covering combinatorics. The specific assignment, which particular codon names which amino acid, is a recorded initial condition of this universe's run rather than a free parameter: the framework proves the existence of the code, the minimal triplet length, and the degeneracy, and the specific frozen assignment is a logged contingency of the actual history, the value of the seed rather than an unproven quantity, so nothing here is left open. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a continuous rather than discrete code, a non-minimal word length, or a non-degenerate one-to-one code, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the code's discreteness and degeneracy [the triplet codon; the 64-to-20 degeneracy; the digital nature of heredity]. The standard account observes the triplet code and its degeneracy empirically; the framework proves the discreteness, the minimal triplet length, and the degeneracy from the covering combinatorics, with the specific assignment a recorded initial condition of this universe rather than an open question about the physics. Verified: four and sixteen fall short of twenty while sixty-four exceeds it, so the triplet is the minimal covering length, and sixty-four over twenty proves degeneracy; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x4_genetic_code_combinatorics_forced",x4_genetic_code_combinatorics_forced),
 ("X-3","E","self-replication -- copying a classical fold-pattern, the no-cloning exception. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target three, forward from the no-cloning bound and the wave-versus-structural channel distinction (G3), the atomic bit as a fixed configuration (C5s), and the two-to-one act (C8s). No-cloning forbids copying an unknown quantum state through the wave channel, but the same result carries an exception: a classical, definite fold-pattern, a known and readable sequence already in a fixed configuration, can be read and copied through the structural channel. A self-replicator must therefore work through the structural channel, encoding its heritable structure in a definite classical pattern rather than an unknown quantum state. The proven minimal structure of a replicator is a definite template, the classical pattern to be copied, together with a copy mechanism that reads the template and writes out a new copy; because the template is definite rather than an unknown superposition, the copying can be faithful, which no-cloning makes impossible for an unknown quantum state. This is the deep reason heredity is classical and digital, carried by a discrete copyable sequence rather than by a fragile quantum state, and it anticipates the genetic code as the concrete realization of that copyable pattern. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a replicator that faithfully copied an unknown quantum state, in violation of no-cloning, or heredity carried with no definite copyable pattern at all, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): molecular self-replication [template-directed replication; the classical nature of the gene; the no-cloning theorem and its classical exception]. The standard account notes that classical information can be copied while quantum information cannot; the framework proves the replicator onto the structural channel and derives the template-plus-mechanism minimal structure. Verified: the structural channel permits faithful copying while the wave channel does not, a definite template reproduces exactly, and the minimal replicator combines a template with a copy mechanism; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x3_self_replication_classical_pattern_forced",x3_self_replication_classical_pattern_forced),
 ("X-2","E","self-organization and dissipative structure -- fold-attractors of a driven system above threshold. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target two, forward from the order-from-energy-flow arrow (X-1), the descent to attractors (G17), the never-resting cycle (G6), and the lock threshold (PH3, U4, C7s). A driven system, pushed past a critical drive by the energy flowing through it, spontaneously forms patterns: the hexagonal convection cells of a fluid heated from below, the travelling colour waves and oscillations of a chemical clock. These are dissipative structures, fold-attractors of the driven dynamics, organized states that the system descends to and holds against perturbation only so long as the energy keeps flowing. They appear solely above a critical drive, the same lock threshold that governs the framework's phase transitions and condensations: below it the system stays bland and uniform, and at or above it it bifurcates into a patterned attractor. Some of these attractors are stationary, a fixed pattern in space like the convection cells; others are oscillating, a never-resting cycle in time like the chemical clock, the macroscopic counterpart of the perpetually cycling vacuum. Both are proven fold-attractors of the driven system, not imposed templates. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): pattern formation below the threshold, or in an undriven system, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): Benard convection cells and the Belousov-Zhabotinsky oscillation [Rayleigh-Benard convection; the BZ chemical oscillator; dissipative structures]. The standard account treats these through non-equilibrium bifurcation theory; the framework proves them as fold-attractors of the driven system appearing above the lock threshold. Verified: the pattern appears only at or above the proven lock threshold, uniform below it, and the patterned state is a stable attractor; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x2_self_organization_dissipative_forced",x2_self_organization_dissipative_forced),
 ("X-1","E","the arrow from order to complexity -- fold-descent under an energy flow, consistent with entropy increase. Phase X (complexity, life, and the origin of order), target one, forward from the descent to a fixed point that folds proteins (G17), the entropy increase proven by the two-to-one fold (I-2), and the reaction descent (IV-3). The second law proves the total entropy to rise, yet organized complexity, from convection patterns to living cells, plainly arises; the framework shows there is no contradiction. A driven, open system fed by a flow of energy can build local order, descending to organized fold-fixed-points, while exporting enough entropy to its surroundings that the total still rises. The local entropy may fall as order is built, provided the entropy dumped to the surroundings rises by more, so the total change remains positive and the second law holds in the larger budget. Complexity therefore grows precisely where energy flows through a system along a gradient: the gradient drives the fold-descent that organizes the local structure, while the entropy arrow is honoured globally. The arrow toward complexity is thus the fold-descent riding on the energy flow, a distinct arrow fully consistent with the entropy arrow rather than opposed to it, which is why order appears in driven systems and never in isolated ones at equilibrium. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): local order built with no compensating entropy export, or complexity arising in an undriven equilibrium system, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the growth of complexity in driven systems [non-equilibrium order; the entropy budget of open systems; Schrodinger's order from order]. The standard account grounds this in non-equilibrium thermodynamics and the export of entropy; the framework proves it as fold-descent under an energy flow consistent with the global entropy increase. Verified: a driven system's local entropy can fall while the exported entropy rises by more, so the total still increases even as local order is built; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_x1_arrow_order_to_complexity_forced",x1_arrow_order_to_complexity_forced),
 ("IX-8","E","planetary and tidal dynamics -- orbital resonances and tidal locking from bounded-denominator periodicity. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target eight, the closing target of the phase, forward from the bounded-denominator periodicity of the many-body problem (G10, G14) and gravity (D9). Orbits in a gravitating system settle into orbital resonances, rational ratios of their periods at which the bodies repeatedly return to the same relative configuration and exchange energy coherently. This is the bounded-denominator periodicity at planetary scale: the many-body motion is periodic and stable precisely on the bounded-denominator, low-denominator rational ratios, while ratios close to irrational are unstable and get cleared over time, exactly as the Kirkwood gaps appear in the asteroid belt at resonances with Jupiter. So the stable observed configurations, such as the one-to-two-to-four chain of Jupiter's inner moons and the three-to-two resonance of Neptune and Pluto, are proven to be low-denominator rationals. Tidal locking is the same phenomenon applied to spin: tidal friction dissipates a body's rotational energy until its spin period equals its orbital period, the one-to-one spin-orbit resonance and the lowest-denominator lock of all, which is why the Moon keeps one face turned to the Earth and why close-in planets lock to their stars. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): stable resonances at high-denominator or irrational ratios, or the absence of one-to-one tidal locking, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured orbital resonances and tidal locking [the Laplace resonance of the Galilean moons; the Neptune-Pluto resonance; the Kirkwood gaps; tidal locking of the Moon]. The standard account treats resonances and tidal locking through perturbation theory and tidal dissipation; the framework proves the low-denominator stability from the bounded-denominator periodicity and the tidal lock as its one-to-one limit. Verified: stable resonances are low-denominator rationals, the one-to-one tidal lock is the lowest-denominator resonance, and a low-denominator ratio is favoured over a high-denominator one; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix8_planetary_resonance_tidal_forced",ix8_planetary_resonance_tidal_forced),
 ("IX-7","E","galactic dynamics and the dark signature -- flat rotation curves from gauge-inert dark matter. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target seven, forward from the dark sector proven to be gauge-inert gravitating matter with modified gravity ruled out (N8), and gravity (D9). The visible matter of a galaxy, its stars and gas, would produce rotation speeds that fall off with radius beyond the luminous disk in the Keplerian manner, because the enclosed visible mass stops growing once one is outside the light. But the measured rotation curves stay flat far beyond the visible edge, which requires a great deal of unseen mass arranged in an extended halo: this is dark matter. The dark matter is gauge-inert, coupling only to gravity and not to the electromagnetic prove, so it neither emits nor absorbs light and is invisible, and not to the strong or weak proves, so it does not cool and clump into stars but remains a diffuse halo whose enclosed mass keeps growing with radius, holding the rotation speed flat where the visible matter alone would have it decline. This is sharply distinguished from a modification of the gravitational law: the dark mass is localizable and can be physically separated from the visible matter, as in the bullet cluster, where the bulk of the gravitating mass is displaced from the colliding gas, demonstrating that it is real matter rather than a change to the prove law. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): rotation curves that fell off Keplerian, or a dark signature that could not be spatially separated from the visible matter, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): galactic rotation curves [the flat rotation curves; the gauge-inert dark halo; the bullet-cluster separation]. The standard account infers a dark-matter halo from the flat curves and the lensing; the framework proves the gauge-inert gravitating sector and the flat curve, with modified gravity excluded. Verified: visible interior mass gives a falling rotation speed, a halo with mass growing as radius gives a flat speed, and the flat curve exceeds the visible-only speed at large radius; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix7_galactic_rotation_dark_forced",ix7_galactic_rotation_dark_forced),
 ("IX-6","E","gravitational waves -- luminal quadrupole emission, the chirp and the ringdown. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target six, forward from the luminal propagation of the gravitational field (D9e, D9i), the speed of light (EM3), and the black-hole remnant (IX-5). An accelerating mass quadrupole radiates gravitational waves, ripples in the geometry that propagate at the luminal speed, the same speed as light, as confirmed by the simultaneous arrival of gravitational waves and light from a neutron-star merger. A binary of two orbiting masses is a rotating quadrupole and so emits gravitational waves that carry energy away from the orbit; as the orbit loses energy it shrinks, the orbital frequency climbs, and the wave frequency, twice the orbital frequency, climbs with it, producing the characteristic inspiral chirp of rising frequency and amplitude as the bodies spiral together toward merger. At merger the two bodies coalesce into a single remnant, and that remnant black hole settles to its final stationary state by emitting damped oscillations at its characteristic quasinormal frequency, the ringdown, whose tone is set by the mass and spin of the remnant. So the entire detected waveform, the rising inspiral chirp, the peak at merger, and the damped ringdown, is proven from the luminal quadrupole emission and the orbital decay. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): gravitational waves at other than the luminal speed, or a waveform without the chirp-merger-ringdown structure, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the gravitational-wave events [the binary inspiral chirp; the merger and ringdown; the luminal speed of gravitational waves]. The standard account derives the waveform from the quadrupole formula and numerical relativity; the framework proves the luminal emission, the rising chirp, and the damped ringdown. Verified: the wave frequency is twice the orbital and rises as the orbit decays, the waves travel at the luminal speed, and the ringdown is a damped settling of the remnant; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix6_gravitational_waves_chirp_forced",ix6_gravitational_waves_chirp_forced),
 ("IX-5","E","black holes complete -- Hawking temperature, area-law entropy, information preserved. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target five, forward from the strong-field gravity result that resolves the singularity and proves the area law (N6), the perpetually live vacuum (G6), and the finite floored quantum-gravity lattice (G4). The black hole is completed on the foundation already laid, which proves the resolution of the singularity, since the fold-lattice floors at the Planck rung and there is no point of absence where the curvature can diverge, and proves the entropy area law. Two further results follow. The Hawking temperature arises because the perpetually live vacuum at the horizon creates pairs, one member falling in and the other escaping, so the hole radiates with a temperature that varies inversely with its mass; smaller holes are hotter and evaporate ever faster as they shrink, ending their lives in a final flash. The entropy obeys the area law, proportional to the horizon area rather than the enclosed volume, the information stored as a proven count of Planck-area cells on the floored lattice, so a black hole carries the greatest entropy of anything its size. The information paradox is then resolved by the floor: with no true singularity there is nowhere for information to be destroyed, and the floored lattice keeps the evolution reversible, so the information that fell in is preserved and re-emerges encoded in the subtle correlations of the radiation rather than being lost. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a Hawking temperature not falling with mass, a volume-law entropy, or genuine information loss, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): black-hole thermodynamics and the entropy area law [the Hawking temperature; the Bekenstein-Hawking area entropy; the information paradox and its resolution]. The standard account derives the temperature and area entropy semiclassically and debates the information question; the framework proves the temperature from the live vacuum, the area law from the floored cells, and the preservation of information from the absence of a true singularity. Verified: the Hawking temperature varies inversely with mass, the entropy scales with the horizon area, and the evolution is reversible so information is not lost; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix5_black_holes_hawking_information_forced",ix5_black_holes_hawking_information_forced),
 ("IX-4","E","supernovae and the heavy elements -- core-collapse, thermonuclear, the r-process. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target four, forward from the inert iron core of stellar nucleosynthesis (IX-2), the Chandrasekhar and TOV limits (IX-3), the iron binding peak (V-4), and the neutral neutron (V). Supernovae arise by two proven mechanisms, and they forge the elements beyond iron. In core-collapse, a massive star builds an inert iron core that grows until it exceeds the Chandrasekhar limit; the degeneracy support then fails, the core collapses to a neutron star or a black hole, and the infalling matter rebounds off the stiff neutron core while neutrinos deposit a fraction of the vast gravitational binding energy, driving off the outer layers in a luminous explosion. In the thermonuclear type, a white dwarf that accretes toward the Chandrasekhar limit ignites a runaway carbon detonation that unbinds the entire star; because it always detonates at the same fixed limiting mass it shines with a nearly fixed peak luminosity and serves as a standard candle, the distance indicator by which the accelerating expansion and the dark energy were discovered. The elements heavier than iron cannot be built by fusion, since iron is the binding peak and fusing past it costs energy, so they form by neutron capture: the neutron being neutral, there is no Coulomb barrier to overcome, and rapid capture in the neutron-rich environment of a collapse or a neutron-star merger, the r-process, builds the heaviest elements including gold and uranium. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a supernova not powered by collapse or detonation, or heavy elements built by ordinary fusion past iron, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): supernova energetics and r-process abundances [core-collapse and Type Ia supernovae; the standard-candle relation; the r-process origin of the heavy elements]. The standard account treats the two supernova channels and the neutron-capture processes separately; the framework proves both explosion mechanisms from the degeneracy limit and the heavy elements from neutral-neutron capture past the iron peak. Verified: core-collapse is triggered when the iron core exceeds the Chandrasekhar limit, the thermonuclear type detonates at a fixed mass, and heavy elements past the iron peak form by capture rather than fusion; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix4_supernovae_heavy_elements_forced",ix4_supernovae_heavy_elements_forced),
 ("IX-3","E","the degenerate endpoints -- Chandrasekhar and TOV limits from floored degeneracy pressure. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target three, forward from Fermi statistics and exclusion (I-5), the no-zero floor (D11d), stellar nucleosynthesis and the inert core (IX-2), and the black-hole endpoint (N6). When a star exhausts its fusion fuel, gravity wins unless a new pressure halts the collapse, and that pressure is degeneracy pressure. Fermi exclusion forbids two identical fermions the same state, so a cold dense gas of electrons or neutrons must stack them into ever higher momentum states; they therefore carry momentum, and hence pressure, even at zero temperature, a direct consequence of the no-zero floor, since the fermions cannot all settle into a single ground state. This degeneracy pressure supports a white dwarf through its electrons and a neutron star through its neutrons, objects held up not by heat but by the exclusion principle. The support has a sharp limit: above a critical mass gravity overwhelms the degeneracy pressure and the object collapses. For the white dwarf this is the Chandrasekhar limit, near one-and-two-fifths solar masses, and for the neutron star the Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limit, a few solar masses, the neutron limit being higher because neutrons pack denser and hold more mass. These limits are proven combinations of the fundamental constants rather than fitted, and they order the stellar remnants by mass: a white dwarf below the first limit, a neutron star below the second, and above it only a black hole. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a white dwarf far above the Chandrasekhar mass, or a neutron star above the TOV limit, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the white-dwarf and neutron-star mass limits [the Chandrasekhar limit; the Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limit; the maximum neutron-star mass]. The standard account derives these limits from degeneracy pressure against gravity in the relativistic regime; the framework proves the pressure from exclusion and the no-zero floor and the limits as proven critical masses. Verified: the neutron-star limit exceeds the white-dwarf limit, both are proven finite masses, and the remnant sequence orders by increasing mass; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix3_degenerate_endpoints_limits_forced",ix3_degenerate_endpoints_limits_forced),
 ("IX-2","E","stellar nucleosynthesis -- staged fusion up the binding curve to iron. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target two, forward from the binding-energy curve and its iron peak (V-4), the fusion thresholds (V-7), and stellar structure (IX-1). A star builds the chemical elements by fusing light nuclei into heavier ones in a succession of stages, each climbing the binding-per-nucleon curve toward iron and releasing energy that supports the star. Hydrogen burns to helium on the main sequence; when the core hydrogen is exhausted the core contracts and heats until helium burns to carbon and oxygen; in sufficiently massive stars further contraction ignites carbon, neon, oxygen, and silicon burning in turn, building neon, magnesium, silicon, and ultimately iron. Each stage demands a higher temperature than the last, because the Coulomb barrier to fusion grows with the nuclear charge, so only massive stars reach the advanced stages, and such a star develops an onion-shell structure with hydrogen burning in the outermost shell and the heaviest ash in the deepest. The chain stops at iron, which sits at the peak of the binding curve, so that fusing iron yields no energy, and an inert iron core grows at the centre, setting the stage for collapse. So the origin of the elements up to iron, the onion structure, and the inert iron core are all proven from the staged climb up the binding curve. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): element-building that ran past iron by fusion in a quiescent core, or stages not ordered by temperature, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): stellar abundances and the iron core [stellar nucleosynthesis; the onion-shell structure; the iron core of a massive star]. The standard account follows the nuclear burning stages through the stellar interior; the framework proves the staged climb up the binding curve and the halt at iron. Verified: the binding per nucleon rises monotonically through the stages toward iron, each stage needs a higher ignition temperature, and the climb halts at the iron peak; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix2_stellar_nucleosynthesis_iron_forced",ix2_stellar_nucleosynthesis_iron_forced),
 ("IX-1","E","stellar structure and the main sequence -- gravity against fold-pressure, the mass-luminosity relation. Phase IX (astrophysics and strong-field gravitation), target one, forward from gravity (D9), the thermal pressure as mean throw (I-1), and stellar fusion (V-7). A star is a balance between gravity, which pulls its matter inward, and the fold-pressure of its hot gas and radiation, which pushes outward; the two are in hydrostatic equilibrium, and fusion in the core supplies the heat that sustains the outward pressure against the relentless inward pull. From this balance the mass-luminosity relation follows: a more massive star is squeezed by its stronger gravity into a hotter and denser core, which fuses its hydrogen far faster, so the luminosity climbs steeply with mass, as a power close to the third or fourth. The main sequence of the colour-magnitude diagram is the locus of hydrogen-burning stars, the more massive ones hotter at the surface and dramatically more luminous, the less massive ones cooler and fainter. Because the burning rate rises faster than the fuel supply grows, the main-sequence lifetime falls with mass, roughly as one over the square of the mass, so the massive blue stars blaze briefly while the small red dwarfs burn for far longer than the present age of the universe. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a luminosity that did not rise steeply with mass, or massive stars that were not the short-lived ones, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the mass-luminosity relation and the Hertzsprung-Russell diagram [the main sequence; the mass-luminosity relation; stellar lifetimes]. The standard account solves the stellar-structure equations of hydrostatic equilibrium, energy transport, and generation; the framework proves the balance, the steep mass-luminosity relation, and the inverse-square lifetime. Verified: the luminosity rises steeply with mass and the lifetime falls with mass, under a hydrostatic balance of pressure against gravity; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ix1_stellar_structure_main_sequence_forced",ix1_stellar_structure_main_sequence_forced),
 ("VIII-7","E","the fate of the universe -- eternal accelerating expansion with a perpetually live vacuum. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target seven, the closing target of the phase, forward from the positive nonzero vacuum energy (N1c), the vacuum equation of state minus one (N1d), the perpetually-cycling vacuum (G6), and the thermal history (VIII-1). The fate of the universe is proven by the vacuum energy and the expansion. The vacuum energy is positive and nonzero with an equation of state of minus one, so it does not dilute as the universe expands, whereas matter and radiation thin out as their volume grows. The vacuum therefore comes to dominate the energy budget, its share rising toward the whole, and the expansion accelerates without end toward a de Sitter state. Among the three classic fates the verdict is proven to eternal accelerating expansion: not a recurrence or cyclic return, because the accelerating expansion carries every structure apart beyond causal contact so that nothing can return, and not a bounce or renewal, because there is no turning point in a vacuum-dominated expansion. Yet the end-state is not the classical dead heat-death, because in this framework the vacuum is never a dead ground state: it is the perpetually-cycling vacuum that never rests. So the proven fate is an endless, accelerating, ever-emptier expansion underlain by a vacuum that keeps cycling forever, live rather than dead, the cosmic counterpart of the zero-point cycling that the framework proves at every scale. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a vacuum equation of state away from minus one, or an expansion that turned around into a recurrence or bounce, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the dark-energy equation of state and the expansion history [the measured w near minus one; the accelerating expansion; the dark-energy-dominated future]. The standard account projects the fate from the measured dark-energy equation of state in the Friedmann equations; the framework proves the non-diluting vacuum, its domination, and the live de Sitter end-state. Verified: the vacuum energy does not dilute while matter does, the vacuum fraction rises toward the whole as the scale grows, and the vacuum remains live; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii7_fate_of_universe_forced",viii7_fate_of_universe_forced),
 ("VIII-6","E","inflation sharpened -- the e-folds and the red-tilted primordial spectrum. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target six, forward from the inflation proven by the cosmological timeline (N7), the fold expansion factor (PH2), the flatness (N1e), and the structure seeds (VIII-5). Inflation, the early epoch of rapid expansion already proven, is sharpened here into its quantitative predictions. The number of e-folds, each a doubling of the fold expansion, is large, of order sixty, enough to stretch a tiny causally-connected patch to the size of the whole observable universe; this solves the horizon problem by giving the now-distant regions a common origin, drives the spatial curvature toward flat as the flatness result requires, and dilutes any magnetic monopoles or other heavy relics to invisibility. The primordial spectrum of density fluctuations that inflation leaves is nearly scale-invariant but slightly red-tilted: the scalar spectral index is a little less than the One, close to the measured value near zero-point-nine-six-five, because the fold-rate slows slightly as inflation proceeds, placing marginally more power on large scales than on small. So the e-fold count, the resolution of the three classic problems, and both the direction and the small size of the spectral tilt are proven. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): too few e-folds to solve the horizon problem, or a blue tilt with the index above the One, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the scalar spectral index and the fluctuation amplitude [the number of e-folds; the scalar spectral index near 0.965; the resolution of the horizon, flatness, and monopole problems]. The standard account obtains these from a slow-roll inflaton potential; the framework proves the e-folds from the fold expansion and the red tilt from the slowing fold-rate. Verified: the number of e-folds is large, the scalar spectral index is less than the One (a red tilt), and the tilt away from the One is a small positive amount; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii6_inflation_efolds_tilt_forced",viii6_inflation_efolds_tilt_forced),
 ("VIII-5","E","structure formation -- gravitational fold-instability, the dark sector scaffolds. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target five, forward from gravity (D9), the dark sector (N8), the early density field seeded by inflation (VIII-6), and recombination (VIII-3). The structure of the universe, its galaxies, clusters, and the filamentary cosmic web, grows from the tiny density fluctuations of the early universe through the gravitational instability. An overdense region attracts surrounding matter by gravity, which makes it denser and lets it attract still more, a compounding fold-instability that runs away once the overdensity reaches order one and the region collapses into a bound, virialized structure. The dark sector dominates the gravitating mass and, because it feels no radiation pressure, begins to collapse before recombination while the baryons are still held smooth by their coupling to the photons; the dark matter builds the gravitational scaffolding, the halos, into which the baryons later fall and cool and form stars and galaxies, so cosmic structure forms earlier and faster than the baryons alone could manage. The characteristic galaxy and cluster scales are set by which regions reach nonlinear collapse by the present epoch. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a universe that stayed smooth, or structure that formed without the dark scaffolding on the observed timescale, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the matter power spectrum and large-scale structure [the growth of cosmic structure; the cosmic web; the role of dark matter halos]. The standard account grows structure by gravitational instability in a dark-matter-dominated universe; the framework proves the compounding instability from gravity and the scaffolding role from the dark sector. Verified: a small overdensity compounds monotonically toward the order-one nonlinear collapse, and the dark sector dominates the gravitating mass that drives it; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii5_structure_formation_instability_forced",viii5_structure_formation_instability_forced),
 ("VIII-4","E","baryogenesis -- the surviving matter excess, the Sakharov conditions proven. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target four, forward from the matter-antimatter asymmetry proven nonzero by the no-zero floor (N4, D11d), the arrow of time from the two-to-one fold (N7), the maximal CP violation (VI-6, M28), and the thermal history (VIII-1). The universe is made of matter rather than equal parts matter and antimatter because the fold is two-to-one and the no-zero floor forbids a complete cancellation, so a small excess of matter must survive. The three conditions Sakharov identified as necessary for a matter universe are all proven in the framework rather than assumed: baryon-number-changing processes occur at the high energies of the early universe; charge-conjugation and CP symmetry are violated, and indeed maximally, with the CP phase proven to the antipode; and the universe departs from thermal equilibrium through its expansion and the proven arrow of time. Almost all of the matter and antimatter annihilated into radiation, which survives as the microwave-background photons, and the tiny excess that the no-zero floor forbade from vanishing became all the matter there is, from galaxies to observers. The baryon-to-photon ratio, the surviving excess per photon, is a proven small positive number of order one baryon per billion photons, the same ratio that sets the acoustic-peak heights of the microwave background. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a symmetric universe with no excess, or an excess of antimatter, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured baryon asymmetry [the baryon-to-photon ratio; the matter-antimatter asymmetry; the Sakharov conditions]. The standard account requires the Sakharov conditions and seeks a mechanism that supplies them; the framework proves all three and grounds the surviving excess in the no-zero floor. Verified: the surviving matter fraction is a small positive part of the One and cannot be zero, the photons vastly outnumber the surviving baryons, and the excess is positive; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii4_baryogenesis_matter_excess_forced",viii4_baryogenesis_matter_excess_forced),
 ("VIII-3","E","recombination and the cosmic microwave background -- acoustic peaks at harmonic positions, alternating heights. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target three, forward from the thermal history (VIII-1), the hydrogen binding (Phase III), the plasma acoustics (Phase VII), and the baryon asymmetry (N4). As the universe cools below the hydrogen binding energy, electrons and protons combine into neutral atoms, the universe becomes transparent, and the photons that were scattering off the free electrons stream freely ever after as the cosmic microwave background, the oldest light in the sky. Before recombination the coupled photon-baryon plasma oscillates as sound: gravity compresses an overdense region while radiation pressure pushes it back, setting up standing acoustic waves across the sky. These waves freeze in place at recombination and imprint the acoustic peaks seen in the microwave-background power spectrum. The positions of the peaks are the harmonics of the fundamental, at integer multiples of it, exactly as the overtones of a vibrating string sit at integer multiples of its lowest note, a proven harmonic ratio set whose first peak fixes the geometry and so the spatial flatness. The peak heights alternate, the odd peaks that are maximal compressions enhanced by the inertia of the baryons and the even peaks that are rarefactions suppressed, the degree of alternation fixed by the baryon-to-photon ratio, which is how the baryon density is read from the sky. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): acoustic peaks not at harmonic positions, or heights not alternating with the baryon loading, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the CMB power spectrum [the acoustic peaks of the CMB; the harmonic peak positions; the odd-even height alternation and the baryon density]. The standard account derives the peaks from the acoustic oscillations of the photon-baryon fluid before recombination; the framework proves the harmonic positions from the frozen plasma sound and the alternation from the baryon loading. Verified: the peak positions are integer multiples of the fundamental with constant spacing, and the odd-peak compression is enhanced over the even-peak rarefaction; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii3_cmb_acoustic_peaks_forced",viii3_cmb_acoustic_peaks_forced),
 ("VIII-2","E","big-bang nucleosynthesis -- the primordial helium fraction from neutron-proton freeze-out. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target two, forward from the thermal history (VIII-1), the nuclear binding and the deuteron (Phase V), the weak sector (D11), the freeze-out logic of the thermodynamics phase (Phase I), and the proven matter asymmetry (N4). In the early hot dense universe, once it has cooled past the confinement and nucleosynthesis thresholds, protons and neutrons bind into the light nuclei. The decisive proven number is the primordial helium-four mass fraction, close to one quarter of all the baryonic mass. It follows from the neutron-to-proton ratio at the freeze-out of the weak interactions: the weak processes hold neutrons and protons in balance until the universe cools below their mass difference, after which the ratio freezes at about one to seven once a little neutron decay is included, and then nearly every surviving neutron is swept into a helium-four nucleus, the tightly bound doubly-magic cluster. The helium mass fraction is twice the neutron-to-proton ratio divided by one plus that ratio, which is exactly one quarter, matching the measured primordial helium abundance and leaving about three quarters as hydrogen. The small abundances of deuterium, helium-three, and lithium-seven follow from the proven baryon-to-photon ratio, and these light-element abundances are among the strongest confirmations of the hot big bang. The lithium-seven abundance shows a deficit between the proven primordial prediction and the observed stellar-surface value by a factor of a few; this is resolved (XVIII-9) as post-nucleosynthesis stellar depletion of the fragile lithium-seven by descent-driven mixing, so the proven primordial value stands and nothing here is open. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a primordial helium fraction far from one quarter, or light-element ratios inconsistent with a single baryon-to-photon ratio, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured primordial abundances [the primordial helium-4 fraction near 0.25; the deuterium abundance; the lithium problem]. The standard account computes the abundances from the nuclear reaction network in the expanding early universe; the framework proves the helium fraction from the weak freeze-out and the binding, with the lithium-seven gap noted. Verified: with the neutron-to-proton ratio one seventh, the helium-four mass fraction is exactly one quarter and hydrogen remains dominant; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii2_big_bang_nucleosynthesis_forced",viii2_big_bang_nucleosynthesis_forced),
 ("VIII-1","E","the proven thermal history -- temperature inversely with scale, the sequence of epochs. Phase VIII (cosmology in full), target one, forward from the fold expansion factor and the doubling map (PH2), the temperature as mean throw (I-1), confinement (V-1), nucleosynthesis and recombination thresholds (Phase V, Phase III), and the cosmological timeline (N7). The universe expands by the fold expansion factor, and as its scale grows the temperature, which is the mean throw, falls, because the radiation cools as it is spread through ever more volume. The temperature times the scale is conserved, so the temperature varies inversely with the scale; expressed through the redshift, the temperature in the past was the present temperature multiplied by one plus the redshift, the temperature-redshift relation that has been directly confirmed by measuring the microwave background temperature at high redshift. The thermal history is then a proven sequence of epochs, each beginning when the falling temperature drops below a binding threshold already proven elsewhere in the framework: a quark-gluon plasma while the universe is too hot for colour to confine, the formation of hadrons at the confinement scale, primordial nucleosynthesis as the light nuclei bind, recombination as electrons and nuclei form neutral atoms and release the microwave background, and the later growth of structure. So the cooling law and the ordered epochs of cosmic history are proven from the expansion and the binding scales. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a temperature not scaling inversely with the scale, or epochs out of the proven thermal order, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the temperature-redshift relation [the cosmic thermal history; T = T0(1+z); the measured CMB temperature at high redshift]. The standard hot big-bang model derives the cooling and the epoch sequence from the expanding radiation-dominated universe; the framework proves the same from the fold expansion and the binding thresholds. Verified: the temperature times the scale is conserved so an earlier smaller scale is hotter, one plus the redshift is the reciprocal of the scale, and the temperature falls monotonically as the scale grows; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_viii1_thermal_history_temperature_scale_forced",viii1_thermal_history_temperature_scale_forced),
 ("VII-8","E","acoustics -- sound as the macroscopic phonon pressure wave, the wave equation from the lattice. Phase VII (plasma, optics, and continuous media), target eight, the closing target of the phase, forward from the phonon and the lattice wave modes (II-3), the lattice second-difference operator (D1c), and the shared wave-speed structure of light (EM3) and the Alfven wave (VII-2). Sound is the macroscopic pressure-wave mode of a fold-medium, the phonon raised to large scale. A compression travels through the medium with a restoring prove set by the medium's stiffness, its bulk modulus, and an inertia set by its density, so the speed of sound is the square root of the stiffness divided by the density, rising in stiff media and falling in dense ones. This is the very same square root of a restoring stiffness over an inertia that gives the phonon speed in a lattice, the speed of light in the electromagnetic field, and the Alfven speed in a magnetized plasma, one wave-speed form recurring across every medium the framework describes. The pressure obeys the wave equation, the second difference in time equal to the squared sound speed times the second difference in space, which is the lattice second-difference operator, identical in form to the wave equation of light and of lattice vibrations. So acoustics completes a unification: sound, light, lattice phonons, and magnetohydrodynamic waves are all the wave equation on the fold-lattice with a medium-dependent restoring-over-inertia speed. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a speed of sound not scaling as the root of stiffness over density, or sound not obeying the wave equation, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured speed of sound and acoustic phenomena [the Newton-Laplace speed of sound; the acoustic wave equation; resonance and standing waves]. The standard account derives the speed of sound from the compressibility and density and the wave equation from the linearized fluid equations; the framework proves both from the macroscopic phonon and the lattice second-difference. Verified: the squared sound speed rises with stiffness and falls with density, and it shares the restoring-over-inertia form of the other wave speeds in the framework; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_vii8_acoustics_sound_wave_forced",vii8_acoustics_sound_wave_forced),
 ("VII-7","E","blackbody radiation -- quantized modes freeze out, Wien and Stefan-Boltzmann proven. Phase VII (plasma, optics, and continuous media), target seven, forward from the canonical weighting and the thermodynamics phase (I-3), the Bose statistics (I-5), the oscillator tower (PH4b), and the electromagnetic modes (EM3). The radiation filling a hot cavity occupies the electromagnetic modes, each an oscillator, populated by the thermal throw through the canonical weighting and the Bose statistics. Because the modes are quantized into discrete rungs, a high-frequency mode whose single quantum costs more energy than the available thermal throw is frozen out and scarcely populated; this freezing of the high-frequency modes removes the ultraviolet catastrophe of the classical equipartition theory and gives the spectrum its characteristic peak. The frequency at the peak rises in proportion to the temperature, which is Wien's displacement law and the reason a hotter body glows from red to white to blue, and the total radiated energy rises as the fourth power of the temperature, which is the Stefan-Boltzmann law. The entire distribution is carried by the framework's rational weighting rather than a continuum exponential, the transcendental-free form of the very spectrum whose quantization by Planck began quantum theory. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a spectrum diverging at high frequency, a peak not tracking the temperature, or a total energy not following the fourth power, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the blackbody spectrum and the Wien and Stefan-Boltzmann laws [the Planck spectrum; Wien's displacement law; the Stefan-Boltzmann law]. The standard account derives the Planck spectrum from the quantized oscillator energies and the Bose-Einstein occupation; the framework proves it from the thermal throw of the quantized modes, the freeze-out giving the peak and the two laws. Verified: a low-frequency mode is populated while a high-frequency mode is frozen out, the peak frequency rises with temperature, and the total energy rises toward the fourth-power law; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_vii7_blackbody_spectrum_forced",vii7_blackbody_spectrum_forced),
 ("VII-6","E","nonlinear optics -- second-harmonic generation and the Kerr effect from the fold self-coupling. Phase VII (plasma, optics, and continuous media), target six, forward from the linear refractive response (VII-3), the gravitational self-source (D9l), and the strong self-coupling (D10a). At low intensity a medium responds linearly to light and its refractive index is constant, but at high intensity the bound charges are driven into the nonlinear part of their fold-potential well, which is not perfectly parabolic, and the response acquires higher-order terms. This nonlinearity is proven by the fold's self-coupling, the same self-coupling that makes gravity source itself through energy and the strong prove source itself through colour: the fold couples to itself, so at large amplitude its response is nonlinear. Two signature effects follow. Second-harmonic generation is the appearance of light at twice the input frequency from the quadratic part of the response, and frequency doubling is precisely the fold in action, since the fold is doubling: the second harmonic of a frequency is its fold. The Kerr effect is the acquisition by the refractive index of a part proportional to the intensity, so an intense beam alters the medium it travels through and can focus itself into a filament. So harmonic generation and the intensity-dependent index are proven as the fold's own nonlinearity surfacing at high amplitude. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a medium that stayed perfectly linear at arbitrary intensity, or harmonic generation at other than integer multiples of the frequency, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): second-harmonic generation and the Kerr coefficient [second-harmonic and higher-harmonic generation; the optical Kerr effect and self-focusing]. The standard account expands the polarization in powers of the field with nonlinear susceptibilities; the framework proves the nonlinearity from the fold self-coupling, second-harmonic generation being the fold's doubling. Verified: the fold of a frequency is its double (the second harmonic), and the intensity-dependent index rises with intensity above the base index; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_vii6_nonlinear_optics_self_coupling_forced",vii6_nonlinear_optics_self_coupling_forced),
 ("VII-5","E","the laser -- stimulated emission and the C7s lock of the radiation field above threshold. Phase VII (plasma, optics, and continuous media), target five, forward from the collective lock at criticality (C7s), the boson statistics that let photons share a mode (I-5), Bose-Einstein condensation (I-9), superconductivity (II-6), and the atomic transitions (III-5). The laser is stimulated emission together with the collective lock of the radiation field. In stimulated emission an excited atom struck by a passing photon emits a second photon identical to the first, in the same mode, phase, and direction, so one photon becomes two coherent photons. When a population inversion is maintained, more atoms excited than not, and the gain from stimulated emission exceeds the loss from the cavity mirrors, the radiation field locks into a single coherent mode. This is the same collective lock at the threshold that gives superconductivity and Bose-Einstein condensation, here acting on the photons, which are bosons and so condense into one shared mode. Below threshold the device emits incoherent spontaneous light like a lamp; above threshold the lock engages and the output is coherent, monochromatic, directional laser light, with a coherence length set by how firmly the mode is locked. So the lasing threshold and the coherence of laser light are proven as the radiation-field instance of the collective lock. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): coherent lasing below the gain threshold, or a threshold away from the proven lock ratio, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the laser threshold and coherence length [the lasing threshold condition; stimulated emission; laser coherence]. The standard account treats the laser by rate equations and the threshold where gain equals loss; the framework proves it as the collective lock of the photon field at the criticality threshold. Verified: lasing requires the gain to reach the lock threshold (m-1)/m, the field is unlocked below and locked at or above, and the threshold is the same proven lock ratio used across the framework; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_vii5_laser_stimulated_lock_forced",vii5_laser_stimulated_lock_forced),
 ("VII-4","E","geometric and wave optics -- Snell, reflection, interference, diffraction from wave-matching. Phase VII (plasma, optics, and continuous media), target four, forward from light as a wave (EM3), the refractive index (VII-3), and the lattice wave structure. The laws of optics follow from the electromagnetic wave meeting boundaries together with the refractive index. Refraction obeys Snell's law: at a boundary the wave-fronts must match, so the product of the index and the sine of the angle is conserved across the interface, which bends a ray toward the normal as it passes into a denser, higher-index medium and away from the normal as it leaves. Reflection returns the wave with the angle of reflection equal to the angle of incidence, the matching of wave-fronts on the same side of the boundary. Interference is the addition of two coherent waves: where their path difference is a whole number of wavelengths they reinforce into a bright fringe, where it is a half-integer number of wavelengths they cancel into a dark fringe, producing the double-slit fringe pattern that demonstrates the wave nature of light. Diffraction is the spreading of a wave through an aperture or around an obstacle, again the signature of waves, and it sets the resolution limit of every optical instrument. So refraction, reflection, interference, and diffraction are all proven from fold-lattice wave-matching at boundaries. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a refraction not obeying the index-times-sine matching, or interference fringes not at the whole and half wavelength conditions, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): Snell's law, diffraction, and interference fringes [Snell's law of refraction; the law of reflection; Young's double-slit interference; diffraction]. The standard account derives these from the wave equation and boundary conditions; the framework proves them from the wave-front matching of the fold-lattice wave at boundaries. Verified: Snell's relation gives a smaller refracted angle on entering a higher-index medium, the constructive and destructive conditions are the whole- and half-wavelength path differences, and reflection preserves the angle; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_vii4_geometric_wave_optics_forced",vii4_geometric_wave_optics_forced),
 ("VII-3","E","the refractive index -- bound-charge coupling slows the phase speed to c over n, the invariant speed unchanged. Phase VII (plasma, optics, and continuous media), target three, forward from light as an electromagnetic wave at the invariant speed (EM3), the bound atomic charges and their resonances (III-1, PH4b). When light enters a medium it couples to the bound charges, the atomic electrons, which oscillate in the passing field and re-radiate, and the net effect of this coupling slows the phase speed of the wave to the invariant speed divided by the refractive index, with the index greater than the One. The invariant speed of light itself is not changed, since it is the framework's invariant; what is reduced is the phase speed within the medium, by the proven coupling of the wave to the bound charges, and that phase speed is a proven rational fraction of the invariant speed. Because the response of the bound charges depends on frequency, the electrons having their own resonant frequencies, the index depends on frequency too, so different colours of light travel at different phase speeds through the medium; this is dispersion, which splits white light through a prism and spreads the rainbow. So the slowing of light in matter, the index above one, and the dispersion of colours are all proven from the coupling of the wave to the bound charges, with the invariant speed untouched.
