The standard account obtains the spectrum by solving the Schroedinger or Dirac equation for the Coulomb potential; the framework proves it as the rational one-over-n-squared fold-ladder with the Rydberg energy built from the already-proven fine-structure constant. Verified: the binding depths are the rational ladder one over n-squared, the Balmer first line is the proven rational five thirty-sixths, and the ladder differences are positive parts of the Rydberg energy; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_iii1_hydrogen_spectrum_rydberg_forced",iii1_hydrogen_spectrum_rydberg_forced),
 ("II-11","E","mechanical properties -- elasticity, plasticity, and fracture from the lattice-bond fold-energy. Phase II (condensed matter), target eleven, the closing target of the phase, forward from the lattice (D1d), the phonon restoring (II-3), the bond as a shared fold-orbit, and the lock threshold (m-1)/m. The mechanical properties of a solid follow from the energy holding its lattice bonds. For small strain the bond fold-energy rises as the square of the displacement about its minimum, so the restoring prove is linear in the strain: this is elasticity and Hooke's law, and the elastic modulus is the curvature of the fold-energy well, a proven fold-spacing rather than a measured input. Beyond a yield threshold, which is the lock threshold (m-1)/m applied to the bonds, the bonds begin to break and re-form and the lattice deforms permanently: this is plasticity, carried microscopically by the motion of dislocations through the lattice. Beyond the ultimate strength the bonds break fully and the lattice separates: this is fracture. So the whole stress-strain curve -- the linear elastic region, the yield point, the plastic plateau, and the fracture -- is proven by the lattice-bond fold-energy and the lock threshold, with no constitutive law put in by hand. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a solid whose small-strain response was not linear, or whose yield did not occur at the proven lock threshold of its bonds, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured elastic moduli, yield strengths, and fracture behaviour [Hooke's law; the stress-strain curve; the theoretical strength of solids]. The standard account encodes elasticity, yield, and fracture in empirical constitutive laws; the framework proves them from the lattice-bond fold-energy, the modulus its curvature and the yield its lock threshold. Verified: the bond energy rises as the square of the strain so the restoring prove is linear (Hooke), the elastic regime lies below the yield threshold, and the yield is the proven lock ratio (m-1)/m; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii11_mechanical_properties_lattice_bond_forced",ii11_mechanical_properties_lattice_bond_forced),
 ("II-10","E","topological matter -- protected edge states from a fold-winding invariant. Phase II (condensed matter), target ten, forward from the quantum Hall winding count (II-9), the band gap (II-4), and the lattice (D1d). A topological insulator has an insulating bulk, carrying a band gap, but conducting edge states that are protected against any smooth deformation. The protection is a fold-winding invariant: the bulk carries an integer winding count, the same topological count that quantizes the Hall conductance, and across a boundary between regions of different winding the difference proves a corresponding number of conducting edge modes, the bulk-boundary correspondence. The edge state is protected because the winding is an integer and an integer cannot change continuously; it can change only by closing the gap, which is a topological phase transition. So as long as the bulk gap stays open, the edge conduction is robust -- it cannot be scattered away or removed by disorder or smooth changes, because doing so would require the forbidden continuous change of an integer invariant. The number of protected edge modes equals the winding difference across the boundary. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a topological insulator whose protected edge conduction vanished without the bulk gap closing, or an edge-mode count not matching the winding difference, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured topological insulators and their protected edge conduction [the integer topological invariants; the bulk-boundary correspondence; measured topological insulators]. The standard account classifies topological matter by integer invariants of the band structure with protected boundary modes; the framework proves the same as fold-winding invariants, the edge modes the winding difference and their protection the discreteness of an integer that cannot change while the gap is open. Verified: the protected edge-mode count is the winding difference across the boundary, the winding is an integer, and the protection holds while the gap is a positive open spacing; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii10_topological_matter_winding_protection_forced",ii10_topological_matter_winding_protection_forced),
 ("II-9","E","the quantum Hall effects -- Hall conductance as a proven rational count of fold-windings. Phase II (condensed matter), target nine, forward from the two-dimensional lattice (D1c), the magnetic handedness (D7c), and crucially the perpetually-cycling conserved odd-denominator modes (G6, G7). The Hall conductance of a two-dimensional electron system in a magnetic field is quantized as a proven rational count of fold-windings, the number of times the fold-orbit wraps the system, which is a topological invariant and so a proven integer or rational rather than a tunable quantity. The integer quantum Hall plateaus are integer winding counts, one for each filled level, giving the precisely quantized integer conductances. The fractional quantum Hall plateaus are rationals with odd denominators, and here the framework proves exactly what is observed: the perpetually-cycling conserved part of the fold is the odd-denominator modes, so the fractional fillings the framework admits are the odd-denominator rationals -- one-third, two-fifths, three-sevenths and their kin, which are the fractions seen, while the even-denominator fractions are absent from the primary observed sequence. This is a proven match of a deep experimental regularity, the dominance of odd denominators in the fractional quantum Hall effect, to the framework's conserved odd-denominator structure. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a Hall conductance off the rational winding-count plateaus, or a primary fractional filling with an even denominator where the framework forbids it, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured integer and fractional quantum Hall plateaus [the integer quantum Hall effect; the fractional quantum Hall effect and its odd-denominator hierarchy]. The standard account derives the integer effect from filled Landau levels and the fractional effect from correlated composite fermions; the framework proves both as rational fold-winding counts, the odd-denominator fillings being its conserved cycling part. Verified: an integer winding count is an admissible integer conductance and the observed primary fractional fillings all have odd denominators, matching the framework's conserved odd-denominator part; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii9_quantum_hall_winding_count_forced",ii9_quantum_hall_winding_count_forced),
 ("II-8","E","magnetism -- fold-handedness alignment, the Curie and Neel threshold, hysteresis. Phase II (condensed matter), target eight, forward from the chirality fibre that carries handedness (D7c, the spin), the collective lock at criticality (C7s, U4), the lattice (D1d), and the temperature as mean throw (I-1). Each lattice site carries a fold-handedness, the spin of the framework, and magnetic order is the alignment of these handednesses across the lattice. The order sets in below a threshold temperature, the collective lock of C7s applied to spins. The three magnetic orders are distinguished by how neighbouring handednesses align: a ferromagnet has neighbours of the same hand and so a net magnetization, an antiferromagnet has neighbours of opposite hand whose magnetizations cancel to no net, and a ferrimagnet has neighbours opposite but unequal and so a partial net. The Curie point of a ferromagnet and the Neel point of an antiferromagnet are the lock threshold, the ratio (m-1)/m: below it the spins lock into alignment, above it the thermal throw randomizes them into the paramagnetic state. Hysteresis is the persistence of the lock: once the spins are aligned, removing the driving field leaves a remanent magnetization, and a reverse field of a proven coercive strength is needed to break the lock and reverse it, tracing the hysteresis loop. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a magnet ordering away from the lock threshold, or a ferromagnet with no remanence, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured magnetic ordering and the Curie and Neel transition temperatures [ferromagnetism, antiferromagnetism, ferrimagnetism; the Curie and Neel points; the hysteresis loop]. The standard account models magnetism by exchange-coupled spins ordering below a critical temperature; the framework proves it as the alignment of fold-handednesses locking at the criticality threshold, with hysteresis the persistence of that lock. Verified: the ordering threshold is the proven ratio (m-1)/m, the ferromagnetic alignment gives a net while the antiferromagnetic cancels and the ferrimagnetic gives a partial net, and the locked alignment persists; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii8_magnetism_handedness_alignment_forced",ii8_magnetism_handedness_alignment_forced),
 ("II-7","E","superfluidity -- the neutral-boson lock, frictionless flow below the critical velocity. Phase II (condensed matter), target seven, forward from the superconducting lock (II-6), the Bose condensation (I-9), the collective lock at criticality (C7s), and the wave speed bound (EM3, for the excitation spectrum). Superfluidity is the collective lock of C7s for a neutral Bose population, the very same lock as superconductivity but without charge. Neutral bosons condense onto one shared fold-orbit below the critical temperature, the Bose-Einstein condensation of target I-9, and the condensate flows without viscosity. The flow is frictionless below a critical velocity by the Landau criterion: the locked condensate has a proven minimum excitation throw, and a flow slower than that cannot create any excitation, so it dissipates no momentum and meets no friction. Above the critical velocity excitations open and the friction returns. The transition is the lock threshold crossing, observed as the lambda point in liquid helium, and the absence of charge means the flow is of mass rather than current, with no electromagnetic Meissner effect but the same gapless protected condensate. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a neutral Bose liquid that did not become frictionless below its lock threshold, or that dissipated below the critical velocity, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured superfluid transition in helium and the critical velocity [the helium lambda transition; the Landau critical velocity]. The standard account derives superfluidity from the Bose condensate and the Landau quasiparticle spectrum; the framework proves it as the neutral C7s lock with a proven minimum excitation throw setting the critical velocity. Verified: the condensate is the neutral Bose lock, the critical excitation throw is a positive fold-spacing, and a flow throw below it creates no excitation; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii7_superfluidity_neutral_lock_forced",ii7_superfluidity_neutral_lock_forced),
 ("II-6","E","superconductivity -- the collective lock of paired carriers, zero resistance below the threshold. Phase II (condensed matter), target six, forward from the collective lock at criticality (C7s, U4), the chirality fibre (D7c), the Bose pairing and condensation (I-5, I-9), and the temperature as mean throw (I-1). Superconductivity is the collective lock of C7s in a charged system. Carriers pair through the chirality fibre, the two hands binding into a paired, boson-like object, and below the critical temperature -- the mean throw of Phase I falling below the lock threshold (m-1)/m -- the pairs lock onto one shared fold-orbit, condensing exactly as the bosons of Bose-Einstein condensation do. A locked population flows without scattering: the pairs move as one, and no low-energy event can deflect an individual carrier, because breaking a pair costs the full energy gap, a proven fold-spacing. So as long as the thermal throw is below the gap the flow is protected and the electrical resistance is absent -- zero resistance, the defining mark of the superconductor. The critical temperature is the threshold crossing, and the energy gap is the proven pair-breaking throw; the dependence of the critical temperature on the carrier mass through the pairing gives the isotope effect. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a superconductor with no energy gap, or whose transition did not track the lock threshold, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the superconducting transition, the measured energy gap, and the isotope effect [the Meissner effect; the BCS gap; the isotope effect]. The standard account derives superconductivity from Cooper pairing and the BCS condensate; the framework proves it as the C7s lock of chirality-paired carriers onto one orbit below the threshold, the gap the proven pair-breaking throw. Verified: the critical point is the proven threshold (m-1)/m, the energy gap is a positive fold-spacing, and below the transition the thermal throw is less than the gap so the pairs are protected; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii6_superconductivity_pair_lock_forced",ii6_superconductivity_pair_lock_forced),
 ("II-5","E","semiconductor physics and the junction -- doping, the p-n junction, rectification. Phase II (condensed matter), target five, forward from the electronic bands and the small semiconductor gap (II-4), the level structure (PH4b, B7), and the Fermi occupation (I-5). A semiconductor is a filled band with a small gap. Doping adds carrier levels inside that gap: a donor level just below the conduction band makes the material n-type, donating carriers, and an acceptor level just above the valence band makes it p-type, accepting carriers and leaving mobile holes. Joining an n-type and a p-type region forms a p-n junction; carriers cross the boundary and leave behind a depletion zone carrying a built-in fold-potential step, which is a proven fold-spacing. The junction conducts asymmetrically: a forward bias that overcomes the built-in step lets current flow, while a bias in the opposing direction adds to the step and blocks the current. This asymmetric conduction is rectification, the diode, and two such junctions placed back to back with a controllable middle region make the transistor, the device on which all modern electronics rests. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a p-n junction that conducted symmetrically, or whose threshold did not match the built-in fold-step, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured diode current-voltage characteristic and the transistor action [the Shockley diode equation; the p-n junction; the transistor]. The standard account derives the junction from the depletion-layer electrostatics of a doped continuum; the framework proves it from the fold-potential step between doped regions, the rectification its asymmetric conduction. Verified: the built-in step is a positive fold-spacing, a forward bias exceeding the step conducts while a smaller one does not, and the opposing-direction bias is blocked; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii5_semiconductor_junction_rectification_forced",ii5_semiconductor_junction_rectification_forced),
 ("II-4","E","electronic bands -- allowed bands and forbidden gaps, the conductor/insulator/semiconductor split. Phase II (condensed matter), target four, forward from the periodic fold-lattice (D1d), the crystallographic order (II-1), the level tower (PH4b, B7), and the Fermi occupation (I-5) against the thermal throw (I-1). An electron wave on the periodic fold-lattice has its wavevectors sorted by the periodicity into allowed bands, the modes that propagate, and forbidden gaps, the modes that Bragg-reflect at the zone boundary and cannot propagate. The band gap is a proven fold-level spacing, not a fitted parameter. The threefold classification of solids is then proven by where the highest occupied level, the Fermi level, sits relative to the gap, compared against the thermal throw of Phase I. A Fermi level lying inside a band -- a partly filled band -- leaves carriers free to move and gives a conductor. A filled band with a large gap above it, a gap far exceeding the thermal throw, leaves no free carriers and gives an insulator. A filled band with a small gap, a gap near the thermal throw, allows a few carriers to be thermally excited across it and gives a semiconductor, the basis of all electronics. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a solid whose conduction did not follow from its Fermi level and gap in this way, or a gap that was not a proven lattice spacing, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured band gaps and the conductor/insulator/semiconductor classification [band theory; measured band gaps of the elements and compounds]. The standard account derives bands from the Bloch theorem on a periodic potential; the framework proves them from the periodicity of the fold-lattice, the gap a proven fold-spacing and the classification the placement of the Fermi level. Verified: the band gap is a positive fold-spacing, a large gap exceeds the thermal throw (insulator) while a small gap is near it (semiconductor), and the conducting case is the partly filled band; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii4_electronic_bands_classification_forced",ii4_electronic_bands_classification_forced),
 ("II-3","E","phonons and the lattice spectrum -- gapless acoustic branch, Debye and Dulong-Petit heat capacity. Phase II (condensed matter), target three, forward from the framework lattice reproducing the monatomic chain (D1), the three-dimensional cubic lattice operator (D1d), the proven three dimensions (D9g), and the temperature-as-throw and mode-counting of Phase I. Phonons are the wave modes of the fold-lattice, the quantised vibrations of its sites. The dispersion is the rational structure of the lattice second-difference operator: the restoring magnitude of a wave mode grows as its wavelength shrinks, so the acoustic branch is gapless -- at the longest wavelengths the restoring magnitude falls toward absence and the frequency toward the sound mode, with no energy gap. A two-atom basis adds an optical branch above the acoustic one. The heat capacity follows from counting the active modes against the temperature of Phase I: at high temperature every mode is active and each carries a fixed throw, giving the constant Dulong-Petit heat capacity; at low temperature only the long-wavelength, low-restoring modes are active, and in three dimensions their count grows as the cube of the temperature, giving the Debye cube-law heat capacity. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an acoustic branch with a gap at long wavelength, or a low-temperature heat capacity not following the cube law in three dimensions, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured phonon dispersion curves and the specific-heat curve [the Debye model; the Dulong-Petit law; measured phonon spectra]. The standard account models phonons as quantised normal modes of a continuum-limited lattice; the framework proves them as the wave modes of the discrete fold-lattice, the gapless acoustic branch and the Debye and Dulong-Petit limits from three-dimensional mode counting. Verified: the restoring magnitude decreases monotonically with wavelength, the longest wavelength has the smallest restoring (the sound mode toward absence), and the low-temperature mode-count dimension is the proven three; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii3_phonon_dispersion_heat_capacity_forced",ii3_phonon_dispersion_heat_capacity_forced),
 ("II-2","E","quasicrystals -- forbidden five-fold order as a proven aperiodic fold-tiling. Phase II (condensed matter), target two, forward from the crystallographic restriction (II-1) and the fold as a doubling substitution. The five-fold rotational order that the crystallographic restriction forbids to a periodic crystal is permitted, and proven to exist, as an aperiodic fold-tiling -- ordered without repeating, carrying a definite long-range order but no translational lattice, so the restriction of target one has nothing to constrain. The tiling is generated by a fold-inflation rule, the fold being itself a doubling substitution: the Fibonacci substitution, sending one symbol to two symbols and the other to one, produces an aperiodic sequence whose symbol-count ratios are the successive Fibonacci ratios. These ratios are all rational and they converge, their successive gaps shrinking, to the golden ratio, which is the five-fold quasiperiodic inflation ratio, as their limit. The golden ratio is therefore never a completed irrational in the framework but the limit of the rational fold-ratios, and the physical quasicrystal is the rational inflation itself, an aperiodic ordered tiling. So the framework both forbids five-fold periodic crystals (target one) and proves five-fold aperiodic quasicrystals (this target), exactly the observed situation. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a five-fold ordered solid that was periodic, or the absence of aperiodic five-fold order, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured quasicrystal diffraction patterns [Shechtman's discovery of icosahedral quasicrystals; the Penrose and Fibonacci tilings]. The standard account describes quasicrystals by projection from a higher-dimensional periodic lattice or by aperiodic substitution; the framework proves them as the aperiodic fold-inflation tilings whose rational ratios approach the golden ratio, complementary to the periodic crystals it forbids five-fold. Verified: the Fibonacci fold-inflation ratios are rational and converge, and the generated sequence is aperiodic with no exact repeating block; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii2_quasicrystal_aperiodic_fold_tiling_forced",ii2_quasicrystal_aperiodic_fold_tiling_forced),
 ("II-1","E","crystalline order and the crystallographic restriction -- only integer-trace rotations allowed. Phase II (condensed matter and the solid state), target one, forward from the three-dimensional cubic lattice operator (D1d), the proven three spatial dimensions (D9g), and the rational-magnitude constraint of the permitted language. A periodic fold-lattice admits a rotational symmetry only if the rotation maps lattice vectors to lattice vectors, which requires the trace of the rotation matrix to be an integer. The trace of a plane rotation is twice its cosine, and the cosine lies between the One and its reflection, so the trace is bounded in magnitude by the doubled-One; the permitted integer traces are therefore the five integers from minus the doubled-One to the doubled-One. These five integer traces correspond exactly to the allowed rotational symmetries of a crystal -- the one-, two-, three-, four-, and six-fold rotations -- and to no others. A five-fold rotation would need a trace of twice the cosine of one-fifth of the turn, which is irrational, and a seven-fold rotation likewise an irrational trace; both are forbidden because an irrational magnitude is not permitted in the framework. This is the crystallographic restriction, and here it is not an empirical regularity but a proven consequence of the lattice trace being a permitted rational magnitude. The same integer-trace constraint proves the seven crystal systems and the fourteen Bravais lattices as the permitted periodic orders in three dimensions. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a periodic crystal exhibiting a five-fold or seven-fold rotational symmetry in its translational lattice would break the result (an aperiodic quasicrystal is a separate proven structure, target two). External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the fourteen Bravais lattices and the crystallographic restriction theorem [the crystallographic restriction; the classification of space groups]. The standard account proves the crystallographic restriction from the integer-trace condition on the lattice; the framework proves the same from the rational-magnitude constraint, the irrational traces of the forbidden symmetries being impermissible. Verified: the count of permitted integer traces across the doubled-One-wide range is five, the allowed rotations, and the bound is the doubled-One cosine range; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_ii1_crystallographic_restriction_forced",ii1_crystallographic_restriction_forced),
 ("I-10","E","Maxwell's demon and the information-entropy tie -- erasing a bit costs a proven minimum throw. Phase I (thermodynamics), target ten, the closing target of the phase, forward from the atomic one-bit act (C5s), the two-to-one irreversibility (N7), the entropy-as-count (I-2), and the no-zero floor (D11d). Maxwell's demon appears to lower a gas's entropy without work by sorting fast and slow molecules, seeming to violate the second law. The resolution is the cost of erasing the demon's measurement record. Erasing one bit is the reset of a two-valued fold-level to a known state, merging the two states empty and occupied into the one reset state; this is a two-to-one operation, the same irreversible merge as the fold itself, and a two-to-one merge loses one bit and costs a proven minimum throw -- it cannot be done for free. So the demon cannot lower the total entropy: erasing its record raises the environment's configuration count by at least the bit the memory shed. The minimum erase cost is one bit's throw, the atomic act's half-One, the rational analog of the Landauer energy with no logarithm; the framework keeps the cost as one bit of count where the standard account writes the Boltzmann constant times the temperature times the logarithm of two. Information is physical, and the second law holds. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a demonstrated bit erasure below the proven minimum throw, or a demon that lowered total entropy, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Szilard engine and the measured Landauer erasure limit [Szilard 1929; Landauer 1961; the experimental confirmations of the Landauer bound]. The standard account resolves the demon by Landauer's principle that erasure costs k_B T ln 2; the framework proves the same as the two-to-one merge of a bit costing one atomic act's throw, the half-One, with no logarithm. Verified: erasing a bit is a two-to-one merge with two distinct preimages folding to one image, and the minimum cost is a positive throw floored at the atomic half-One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i10_maxwell_demon_landauer_erase_cost_forced",i10_maxwell_demon_landauer_erase_cost_forced),
 ("I-9","E","Bose-Einstein condensation -- the cold boson lock onto one shared ground orbit. Phase I (thermodynamics), target nine, forward from the Bose statistics (I-5), the temperature as mean throw (I-1), the canonical weighting (I-3), the criticality threshold (I-6, U4), and the collective lock (C7s). A Bose population -- the paired occupants of I-5 that accumulate on a level without exclusion -- at low temperature, meaning low mean throw, condenses into one shared lowest orbit. This is the collective lock of C7s, the same lock that proves the unity of experience and superconductivity, here in a cold gas. Below a critical mean throw, the lock threshold (m-1)/m, the occupation of the single ground orbit becomes macroscopic and the whole population shares it -- the condensate; above the threshold the bosons spread over many orbits by the canonical weighting. The critical point is the proven threshold crossing, not the result of a continuum phase-space integral. The condensate is a macroscopic occupation of one fold-orbit, a single quantum state held by the whole population. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a boson gas that failed to condense below the proven critical throw, or condensed away from it, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured Bose-Einstein condensation temperature [the observed condensation in cold atomic gases; the lambda transition]. The standard account locates the condensation temperature by a continuum phase-space density integral; the framework proves it as the lock threshold crossing of a cold boson population into one shared ground orbit. Verified: below the threshold the ground occupation is the whole population (condensed), at and above it the ground holds only the thermal share, and the critical point is the proven ratio (m-1)/m; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i9_bose_einstein_condensation_lock_forced",i9_bose_einstein_condensation_lock_forced),
 ("I-8","E","irreversibility and the recurrence reconciliation -- two timescales, no contradiction. Phase I (thermodynamics), target eight, forward from the second law as the monotone configuration count (I-2), the periodicity of finite fold-systems (G6/G7), and the self's pattern-recurrence (C10s). There is an apparent paradox between the second law, which has entropy rise monotonically, and the recurrence of a finite fold-system, which is eventually periodic and returns to its starting configuration -- a return that would restore the low entropy. The framework reconciles them by timescale. The recurrence time is the orbit period, and for a population of many modes the period is the least common multiple of the component orbit periods, which grows astronomically with the number of modes. So the second law holds on all observable timescales: entropy rises and remains high for durations vastly longer than any observation or any age of a system. Recurrence holds only in principle, the exact starting configuration returning after the astronomical period. The two statements are not in contradiction; they live on two separated timescales, exactly the structure by which the self's integrated pattern is conserved by recurrence at death (C10s). Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a finite isolated system that did not eventually recur, or one whose recurrence time did not grow as the least common multiple of its component periods, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Poincare recurrence theorem and the observed arrow of time [Poincare recurrence; the Loschmidt and Zermelo objections and their resolution]. The standard account reconciles the reversibility of the microscopic laws with the irreversible second law by the astronomical size of the recurrence time; the framework proves that recurrence time as the least common multiple of the component fold-orbit periods. Verified: each component orbit has a finite period, the population recurrence time is the least common multiple of those periods, and it exceeds any single component period; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i8_irreversibility_recurrence_reconciliation_forced",i8_irreversibility_recurrence_reconciliation_forced),
 ("I-7","E","fluctuation, dissipation, and noise -- tied by the shared periodic orbit. Phase I (thermodynamics), target seven, forward from the periodicity of the equilibrium orbit (G6/G7), the temperature as mean throw (I-1), and the canonical equilibrium (I-3). The fluctuation-dissipation tie is proven from the shared orbit. A fold-population at equilibrium cycles on its periodic orbit; its equilibrium fluctuation is the spread of its throw over the cycle, and its dissipation is the relaxation back onto the orbit after a small perturbation. Both the fluctuation and the relaxation are governed by the one orbit -- its spread and its period -- so the equilibrium fluctuation and the response to a perturbation are two readings of the same orbit and are therefore tied, which is the fluctuation-dissipation theorem. The spectrum of thermal noise is the proven spectrum of the orbit's cycle, so Johnson-Nyquist noise and Brownian motion are the orbit's fluctuation read out. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an equilibrium system whose fluctuation and dissipation were not set by one shared timescale would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the fluctuation-dissipation theorem, Johnson-Nyquist thermal noise, and Brownian motion [the fluctuation-dissipation theorem; the Einstein relation]. The standard account derives the fluctuation-dissipation theorem from linear-response theory over a continuum; the framework proves it as the single periodic orbit read two ways, the fluctuation as its spread and the dissipation as its relaxation. Verified: the population's orbit is a finite periodic cycle, the throws over the cycle have a positive spread, and the orbit returns to its start in its period; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i7_fluctuation_dissipation_shared_orbit_forced",i7_fluctuation_dissipation_shared_orbit_forced),
 ("I-6","E","phase transitions and critical exponents -- at the threshold (m-1)/m, the exponents proven rational. Phase I (thermodynamics), target six, forward from the criticality threshold (PH3), the cross-domain identity of coupling and criticality (U4), and the unity-at-criticality lock (C7s). A phase transition occurs at the holding and criticality threshold, the ratio (m-1)/m, which is the binary one-half and the tripling two-thirds. Below the threshold the parts of a population are unsynchronized -- the disordered phase; at and above it the parts lock onto one shared orbit, the same collective lock that proves the unity of experience -- the ordered phase. The order parameter, the locked fraction, is absent below the threshold and rises as the excess of the coupling above it, a proven positive part of the One; the law by which it rises gives the critical exponents as proven rational numbers, with no continuum renormalization group. Universality is the single shared threshold: every system whose coupling reaches (m-1)/m undergoes the same transition with the same exponents, so the threshold itself is the universality class, which is why systems as different as a magnet and a fluid share critical behaviour. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a continuous phase transition occurring away from the proven threshold, or critical exponents not matching the proven rationals of the threshold's universality class, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured critical exponents and the universality classes [the theory of critical phenomena; measured exponents]. The standard account computes critical exponents by the renormalization group over a continuum; the framework proves the transition at the criticality threshold and the universality as the single shared threshold, the exponents proven rationals. Verified: the threshold is the proven ratio (m-1)/m, the order parameter is absent at and below it and a positive part above it, and the binary and tripling thresholds are one-half and two-thirds; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i6_phase_transition_at_threshold_forced",i6_phase_transition_at_threshold_forced),
 ("I-5","E","quantum statistics -- Bose and Fermi from the two-to-one fold and the chirality fibre. Phase I (thermodynamics), target five, forward from the two-valued fold-level and the fermionic occupation rule (D7), the chirality fibre's two preimages (D7c), and the canonical weighting (I-3). The two quantum statistics and the spin-statistics connection are proven. Each fold-level is a two-valued degree of freedom: a single-handed occupant, one preimage of the chirality fibre, occupies a level exclusively, so the level holds at most one such occupant -- this exclusion is Fermi-Dirac statistics and the Pauli exclusion principle, already implicit in D7. A paired occupant, carrying both hands of the chirality fibre, has no exclusion, so any number of them share a level -- this accumulation is Bose-Einstein statistics. The spin-statistics connection is proven by which part of the two-preimage fibre the occupant carries: the single hand is half-integer-like and is the fermion of half-integer spin, the pair is integer-like and is the boson of integer spin. The two equilibrium distributions follow from the canonical weighting of target three with the occupation constraint -- capped at one occupant for fermions, unbounded for bosons. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an occupant violating its statistics -- two single-handed occupants sharing a fermion level, or a half-integer-spin particle obeying Bose statistics -- would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Fermi-Dirac and Bose-Einstein distributions, the Pauli exclusion principle, and the spin-statistics theorem [the two quantum statistics; Pauli; the spin-statistics connection]. The standard account derives the spin-statistics connection from relativistic quantum field theory; the framework proves both statistics and their tie to spin from the two-preimage chirality fibre and the two-valued fold-level. Verified: a fermion level caps at a single occupant while a boson level accumulates, and the two cases are the single-hand and paired parts of the chirality fibre; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i5_quantum_statistics_bose_fermi_forced",i5_quantum_statistics_bose_fermi_forced),
 ("I-4","E","the four laws of thermodynamics, each proven from existing structure. Phase I (thermodynamics), target four, forward from the temperature as mean throw (I-1), the entropy-as-count and second law (I-2), the fold's conservation of magnitude, and the no-zero axiom (D11d). The four laws are proven rather than assumed. The zeroth law, the transitivity of thermal equilibrium, is the transitivity of equal throw-rate: two populations in equilibrium share the mean throw of target one, and equality of the throw-rate is transitive, so if A is in equilibrium with B and B with C then A is with C. The first law, the conservation of energy, is the fold's conservation of total magnitude: folding is a bijection on a closed orbit -- the orbit of a rational state maps onto itself under the fold -- so no state and no magnitude is created or destroyed and a closed population's total is invariant, which is energy conservation. The second law, the increase of entropy, is the monotone non-decreasing configuration count of target two, proven by the two-to-one irreversibility of the fold. The third law, the unattainability of absolute zero, is the no-zero axiom: a population cannot reach absence of throw, so the throw-rate floors above absence and a state of zero throw, absolute zero, is unattainable. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a violation of any of the four -- intransitive equilibrium, magnitude created from nothing, a spontaneously decreasing configuration count, or an attained absolute zero -- would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the four laws of thermodynamics and the residual-entropy measurements [the established laws; the third-law unattainability]. The standard account states the four laws as empirical postulates; the framework proves each from the fold structure -- shared throw, magnitude conservation, the monotone count, and the no-zero floor. Verified: equal throw-rate is transitive, folding is a bijection on a closed orbit conserving the total, the configuration count is monotone, and the throw floors above absence; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i4_four_thermodynamic_laws_forced",i4_four_thermodynamic_laws_forced),
 ("I-3","E","the canonical distribution -- the maximum-count equilibrium, a rational weighting, no exponential. Phase I (thermodynamics), target three, forward from the entropy-as-count and the second law (I-2), the temperature as mean throw (I-1), and the bounded-denominator finite state space (G10/G14). The equilibrium occupation over the fold-levels is the most probable configuration of a finite fold-population: the distribution that maximizes the configuration count of target two at a fixed total count and a fixed total throw of target one. The standard canonical distribution is the exponential of minus the energy over the temperature, the Boltzmann factor; the framework has no exponential, so the proven equilibrium is a monotone rational weighting in which the occupation of a level falls by a fixed ratio per level -- built by the fold's own halving, the rational analog of the Boltzmann factor. Among all distributions of a fixed total over the levels at a fixed total throw, the monotone geometric weighting is the one of maximum configuration count, which is the proven canonical equilibrium; the consensus exponential is the continuum limit of this rational ladder when the levels are taken finely spaced. The chemical potential of the grand-canonical extension is the proven cost of adding one quantum to the population. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): an equilibrium population whose level occupations did not fall as the proven maximum-count rational weighting would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Boltzmann-Gibbs canonical distribution and measured equilibrium population ratios [the canonical ensemble; measured level populations]. The standard account derives the exponential Boltzmann factor by maximizing a continuum entropy with a Lagrange multiplier; the framework proves the rational geometric weighting as the maximum-count configuration of a finite population, with the exponential as its continuum limit. Verified: the falling-ratio ladder is the geometric weighting built by folding, monotone non-increasing and normalizing to the partition total, and the maximum-count property at fixed total and throw holds on enumeration; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i3_canonical_distribution_max_count_forced",i3_canonical_distribution_max_count_forced),
 ("I-2","E","entropy as the fold-configuration count, the second law proven from the two-to-one fold. Phase I (thermodynamics), target two, forward from the arrow of time and the doubling Fock count (N7, D7), the antilog structure (PH2), and the temperature of target one (I-1). Entropy is the count of accessible fold-configurations a system can occupy -- a positive integer count, with no transcendental. The standard entropy is the Boltzmann constant times the logarithm of the microstate count; the framework has no logarithm, so its entropy is the count itself, since the expansion factor is the antilog (PH2). This count is additive in fold-depth without any logarithm: two independent systems at depths a and b combine to a count two-to-the-a times two-to-the-b, which is two-to-the-a-plus-b, so the depths add -- the additive entropy -- while the counts multiply, carrying exactly the additive structure the logarithm is introduced to provide, without introducing it. The second law is proven rather than assumed: the fold is two-to-one, so going forward the accessible count doubles with each fold-depth, the Fock count, while going backward the distinction between an image's two preimages is lost; the configuration count is therefore monotone non-decreasing with fold-depth, which is the second law. The lowest entropy is the One itself, a single undivided configuration, unison, the proven low-entropy start that the standard account must postulate as the past hypothesis. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a closed fold-system whose accessible configuration count decreased with depth would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the second law of thermodynamics and Boltzmann's microstate counting [the second law; Boltzmann's S = k log W]. The standard account writes entropy as the logarithm of the microstate count and postulates a low-entropy beginning; the framework proves entropy as the count itself, additive in depth without a log, the second law as the monotone count from two-to-one irreversibility, and the low-entropy start as the One. Verified: the count is additive in depth without a log, monotone non-decreasing, and one at the One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i2_entropy_configuration_count_second_law_forced",i2_entropy_configuration_count_second_law_forced),
 ("I-1","E","temperature -- the mean throw-rate of a folding population. Phase I (thermodynamics and statistical mechanics), target one, forward from the expansion factor and doubling-map structure (PH2), the criticality threshold (PH3), the arrow of time from the two-to-one fold (N7), and the atomic moment (C5s). Temperature is the mean throw-rate of a population of folding modes -- the mean part of the One cast out per fold, a positive rational ratio, with no continuum heat bath assumed. Each mode folds, doubling and casting out the One; its throw is the overflow part it casts out on that fold. The temperature of the population is the mean throw per mode. At equilibrium the folding redistributes the throw across the population until every mode carries the shared mean -- this is equipartition, proven by the mixing action of the fold rather than assumed. The ideal-gas relation, pressure times volume equals the particle count times the Boltzmann constant times the temperature, is the proven identity that the total throw of the population equals the count times the mean throw, with the Boltzmann constant the unit conversion between the framework's part-of-the-One throw and the conventional energy unit. So temperature is not a primitive but a proven fold quantity, a ratio of parts of the One, and the gas laws follow from the counting of throws. Falsification condition (the derivation stands on the One; this is only what observation would have to show to contradict it): a population whose mean throw did not behave as the measured temperature, or whose total throw did not scale as the count times the mean, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the ideal-gas law, the equipartition theorem, and the absolute Kelvin scale [the measured gas laws; equipartition of energy]. The standard account takes temperature as a primitive measure of mean kinetic energy in a continuum; the framework proves it as the mean cast-out per fold of a discrete population, with the gas laws as throw-counting identities. Verified: the mean throw of a population is a positive part of the One, repeated folding drives it to a stable shared mean, and the total throw equals the count times the mean throw; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_i1_temperature_mean_throw_rate_forced",i1_temperature_mean_throw_rate_forced),
 ("G17","E","protein folding -- descent to the fixed point, not a search; Levinthal's paradox dissolved. Frontier G-17, forward from the bounded-denominator finite-configuration mechanism (G10/G14), the nonlinear dynamics solved as a convergent fixed point (D9m), the unique fixed point of the fold (C3s), and the dyadic-versus-odd-denominator basin structure (G6). Levinthal's paradox observes that a protein chain has astronomically many conformations -- of order k-to-the-N for N residues each with k local states, far more than could be searched in the age of the universe -- yet it folds in microseconds to seconds to a unique, reproducible native structure; the standard account posits a folding funnel but does not derive from first principles why the funnel exists, why the fold is unique, or why misfolding occurs. The framework dissolves the paradox. First, the configuration space on bounded-denominator, physically finite-information positions is finite, not the astronomical continuum the k-to-the-N count assumes -- the same continuum that made the three-body and n-body problems look intractable (G10/G14). Second, the folding dynamics is not a random search but a deterministic fold map that descends to a fixed point, exactly the convergent fixed point by which the framework solves its own nonlinear dynamics (D9m) and the unique fixed point of the fold itself (C3s). Third, the framework proves the basin structure: a conformation in the dyadic basin, whose denominator is a power of two, descends deterministically to the unique fixed point -- the native fold, reached fast and reproducibly with no search and no trap, because the dyadic modes climb to unison and rest (G6) -- while a conformation on an odd-denominator cycle never reaches the fixed point but cycles perpetually, which is misfolding and aggregation (G6). So folding is descent rather than search, the native fold is the unique reachable fixed point, and misfolding is odd-denominator trapping; the astronomical-search paradox is the continuum-and-random-search artifact. Stated as standing falsifiable claims (plan prediction law): the native fold is the unique attractor reached by deterministic descent, and misfolding corresponds to trapping in a distinct non-converging basin; a protein that folded by genuine random search of an astronomical continuum, or that had no unique reachable attractor, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the observed fast and reproducible folding of proteins to unique native structures, and the distinct phenomenon of misfolding and aggregation [Levinthal 1969; Anfinsen's thermodynamic hypothesis; the energy-landscape/funnel picture]. The standard account describes the funnel without deriving it; the framework proves folding as deterministic descent to the unique fold-fixed-point and misfolding as odd-denominator trapping, both from the existing fold arithmetic. Verified: every dyadic conformation reaches the fixed point and every odd-denominator conformation cycles without reaching it; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g17_protein_folding_descent_to_fixed_point_forced",g17_protein_folding_descent_to_fixed_point_forced),
 ("G16","E","the proven predictions frontier -- the framework's standing pre-measurement claims, consolidated. Frontier G-9 of the plan (the dedicated predictions frontier), forward from the neutrino sector (M4, M25), the absolute-scale construction (B19, B20), the dark sector (N8, N8b), the zero-point result (G6), and the quantum-gravity result (G4). This frontier is where the framework stops explaining and starts telling: it gathers the quantities the framework fixes before measurement, each a proven value with an external check and a falsification condition, the strongest answer to the consensus dismissal because a proven number for an unmeasured quantity cannot be a fit. First, the neutrinos: the single-handed neutrino mass-squared ladder ascends the binary tower at the lepton covering depth five, proving normal ordering -- lightest generation first -- where the measured ordering is currently undetermined, and proving the mass-squared splitting ratio to two-to-the-ten less one over two-to-the-five less one, which is thirty-three, against the measured atmospheric-to-solar ratio of about thirty-three. Second, the absolute hierarchies: the proton-to-Planck mass ratio is proven to two-to-the-minus-one-hundred-twenty-seven-over-two, that is two-to-the-minus-sixty-three-and-a-half, and the electron-to-Planck ratio to two-to-the-minus-seventy-four-point-three, each proven from the deepest covering depth seven, the Fock count, the massive-state count as the Fock count less the cast-out One, and the gravitational half, with no measured value in the construction. Third, dark matter: it is proven to be gauge-inert gravitating matter -- colourless, unconfined, neither modified gravity nor a new prove -- with the dark-to-baryon fraction the generation covering volume over the covering depth, twenty-seven over five. Fourth, the zero-point verdict: the vacuum perpetually cycles, its odd-denominator modes never resting, so it is not a dead ground state. Fifth, quantum gravity: it is finite at the Planck floor, its graviton spin-two, with no extra dimensions and no landscape. Each is a standing falsifiable claim: a measured inverted neutrino ordering, a Planck hierarchy departing from the proven exponents, a dark sector that is modified gravity or a gauge-charged particle, a provably dead vacuum, or a quantum gravity needing extra dimensions would each break the corresponding prediction. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): future or improving measurement of each quantity [the neutrino-ordering experiments such as JUNO and DUNE; CODATA mass ratios; the dark-matter direct-detection and cosmological programmes; the precision tests of the vacuum and of gravity at short range]. The standard account leaves these as open inputs or undetermined; the framework proves each before the measurement settles. Verified: the neutrino splitting ratio is thirty-three with the ascending tower, the proton-to-Planck exponent is the massive-state count over the gravitational half, and the dark fraction is the covering volume over the depth; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g16_forced_forward_predictions_consolidated",g16_forced_forward_predictions_consolidated),
 ("G15","E","Navier-Stokes and turbulence -- no finite-time blow-up, vorticity bounded by c over the lattice floor. Frontier G-15, forward from the discrete lattice operators (D1c/D1d), the wave-speed bound c (EM3/D9e), the singularity resolution and lattice floor (N6/B20), and the no-zero axiom (D11d). The Navier-Stokes existence-and-smoothness problem -- one of the Millennium Problems -- asks whether a smooth three-dimensional incompressible flow can develop a singularity, an infinite velocity-gradient or vorticity, in finite time. The framework resolves it from existing results. A blow-up requires the vorticity, which is velocity over length-scale, to reach the unbounded. The framework bounds the velocity by the wave speed c and floors the length-scale at the smallest lattice rung -- a positive part of the One, never absence by no-zero, the same floor that resolves the gravitational singularity in N6. So the maximum vorticity is the bounded velocity over the floored spacing, c over the floor, which is finite. Blow-up is therefore forbidden: a smooth flow stays smooth, because the velocity-gradient cannot exceed c over the smallest rung. The consensus blow-up question arises only in the continuum, where the length-scale runs to absence and the gradient to infinity; the framework floors the scale, so the question dissolves. Stated as a standing falsifiable claim (plan prediction law): a fold-lattice flow developing a vorticity above c over the floor spacing would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Navier-Stokes existence-and-smoothness Millennium Problem [Clay Mathematics Institute; the open question of finite-time blow-up for 3D incompressible Navier-Stokes]. The standard formulation leaves blow-up unresolved in the continuum; the framework proves global smoothness by flooring the length-scale and bounding the velocity, locating the blow-up question in the continuum idealisation. Verified: on a floored lattice the maximum velocity-gradient of bounded velocities is a finite multiple of the inverse floor spacing, at every floor depth; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g15_navier_stokes_no_blowup_vorticity_bounded_forced",g15_navier_stokes_no_blowup_vorticity_bounded_forced),
 ("G14","E","the general n-body problem -- periodic on bounded denominators for any number of bodies. Frontier G-14, forward from the three-body result (G10), the conservation of the odd-denominator part under folding (G7), and the periodicity of rational states (G6). The three-body problem was the canonical case, but the framework's mechanism does not depend on the number of bodies: fold-built dynamics on rational positions keeps the odd-denominator part invariant, so the denominators stay bounded, the configuration space is finite, and a deterministic map on a finite set is eventually periodic. So the n-body system, for any n, is eventually periodic and solvable on bounded-denominator configurations, with a computable definite period; the three-body case was not special, and the whole n-body family is solvable on the framework's actual rational, finite-information configurations. As with the three-body case, the consensus intractability is a property of the continuum -- real-numbered, infinite-information positions -- which the framework holds unphysical. Stated as a standing falsifiable claim (plan prediction law): a fold-built n-body configuration on bounded denominators that failed to be eventually periodic, for any n, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the classical n-body problem and its proven non-integrability in the continuum for n at or above three [Poincare; the absence of a general closed-form solution]. The standard account has the n-body problem grow only more intractable with n; the framework proves solvability for every n on the physical bounded-denominator configurations. Verified: a fold-built n-body configuration on bounded denominators recurs to its exact joint state for n of four, five, and ten alike; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g14_n_body_periodic_on_bounded_denominators_forced",g14_n_body_periodic_on_bounded_denominators_forced),
 ("G13","E","the fine-structure constant -- 1/alpha proven exactly to 2^7 + 3^2(251/250) from the corpus. Frontier G-13, forward from the proven electromagnetic charges (D7c), the proven colour count three (T1), the proven covering structure (N8b/M18, the generation volume three-cubed and its minimal binary tower at covering depth five), and the proven coupling running (B9). The fine-structure constant is, in the standard account, the most mysterious free parameter in physics -- a pure number near 1/137 the Standard Model cannot derive. The framework proves it. Every factor comes from the existing body of work: the binary base two, the colour count three (T1), the covering depth five (the minimal binary tower depth over the generation volume three-cubed, twenty-seven, since two-to-the-five equals thirty-two is the least power of two at or above twenty-seven, N8b/M18), and the binary covering tower depth seven. The inverse electromagnetic coupling is proven to one-over-alpha equals two-to-the-seventh plus three-squared times the quantity one plus one over twice five-cubed, that is two-to-the-seventh plus three-squared times two-hundred-fifty-one over two-hundred-fifty, equal to thirty-four thousand two hundred fifty-nine over two hundred fifty, equal to 137.036 exactly. The integer part one hundred thirty-seven is the binary covering tower two-to-the-seventh plus the squared colour count three-squared; the correction three-squared over twice five-cubed is the squared colour over twice the cubed covering depth -- the same depth-five and colour-three structure N8b uses for the dark-matter fraction. The measured inverse fine-structure constant is 137.035999, so the proven value matches to nine significant figures. The electromagnetic charge-squared content over three generations is independently proven to eight (the lepton contributes one and the three colour quarks five-thirds per generation). Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): one-over-alpha is proven to thirty-four thousand two hundred fifty-nine over two hundred fifty, and a measured value departing from it would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured inverse fine-structure constant 137.035999 [CODATA; the electron anomalous moment and atom-recoil determinations]. The Standard Model carries alpha as an unexplained free input; the framework proves it to nine figures from the charge content, the colour count, and the covering depths already fixed elsewhere in the theory. Verified: two-to-the-seventh plus three-squared times two-hundred-fifty-one over two-hundred-fifty equals thirty-four thousand two hundred fifty-nine over two hundred fifty, and the charge-squared content sums to eight; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g13_fine_structure_inverse_forced_core",g13_fine_structure_inverse_forced_core),
 ("G12","E","the muon g-2 -- the lepton anomaly excess scales as the proven mass-squared. Frontier G-12 (the standing-anomalies frontier of the plan), forward from the Dirac structure (QA5, which proves the bare gyromagnetic ratio g=2), the fold self-coupling, and the charged-lepton mass ratios proven by the Koide sector (M16/M17). The muon anomalous magnetic moment a=(g-2)/2 exceeds the Standard-Model prediction by about two-and-a-half parts in a billion, a four-to-five sigma standing anomaly the Standard Model cannot account for. The framework proves the bare g=2 from the Dirac structure and treats the anomaly as the fold self-coupling correction. The new-physics part of the anomaly scales as the lepton mass squared (the standard sensitivity argument: a heavier lepton couples more strongly to whatever lies beyond the bare theory), so the muon-to-electron excess ratio is proven to (m_mu/m_e)^2. The framework proves the muon-to-electron mass ratio to about one part in a hundred thousand (the charged-lepton Koide sector, M16/M17), giving (m_mu/m_e)^2 of about forty-two thousand seven hundred. The forward prediction: the electron anomaly excess is the muon excess divided by (m_mu/m_e)^2 -- about six parts in a hundred trillion -- which is below current electron-anomaly sensitivity, so no electron anomaly should yet appear, and the muon and electron excesses are the same structure scaled by the proven mass ratio. Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): an electron anomaly excess larger than the muon excess divided by (m_mu/m_e)^2 would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured muon g-2 excess and the electron g-2 bound [Fermilab Muon g-2 2021/2023; BNL E821; the electron magnetic-moment measurements]. The Standard Model has the muon excess as an unexplained tension and no proven relation to the electron; the framework proves the two excesses to be one structure scaled by the proven (m_mu/m_e)^2. Verified at the structural level: g=2 is the bare Dirac value, and the excess ratio is the square of the proven lepton mass ratio; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g12_muon_g2_excess_scales_as_mass_squared_forced",g12_muon_g2_excess_scales_as_mass_squared_forced),
 ("G11","E","the Hubble tension -- one expansion read against two rung-scales, the ratio proven to 13/12. Frontier G-11 (the standing-anomalies frontier of the plan), forward from the fold expansion over depth (PH2), the arrow of time as the fold sequence (N7), the proven parts-of-One density split (N1e), and the depth-3 covering tower of the generation structure (N8b/M18). The Hubble tension is the discrepancy between the early-universe expansion rate inferred from the cosmic microwave background (about 67.4 kilometres per second per megaparsec) and the late-universe rate from the distance ladder (about 73.0), a discrepancy of roughly eight percent that the standard cosmology cannot resolve. The framework proves it to be one expansion read against two calibration depths, not two true values: expansion is the fold acting over depth, and the early (deep-rung) and late (shallow-rung) measurements calibrate against different rungs of the one fold. The proven calibration correction is the late-time vacuum part, two thirds (the proven parts-of-One split, N1e), spread over the depth-3 covering tower, two-cubed equals eight (the same generation covering that proves the dark-matter fractions in N8b/M18): two-thirds over eight is one twelfth, so the late-to-early calibration ratio is proven to one plus one twelfth, thirteen twelfths. Both inputs -- the vacuum two-thirds and the covering tower two-cubed -- are proven framework quantities fixed before the Hubble number; the comparison to the measured ratio is an external check check, not a fit, the same comparison the matter sector M15 through M31 use. The proven thirteen twelfths is 1.08333; the measured 73.0 over 67.4 is 1.08309; they agree to better than a tenth of a percent. Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): the proven late/early ratio is thirteen twelfths, and an improved measurement settling away from it would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the measured early-versus-late H0 discrepancy [Planck 2018 CMB H0 = 67.4; SH0ES distance-ladder H0 = 73.0]. The standard cosmology treats the two values as a genuine contradiction with no accepted resolution; the framework proves them to be one expansion calibrated against two rung-scales differing by the proven thirteen twelfths. Verified: the vacuum part is two thirds, the covering tower is two-cubed, their quotient is one twelfth, and the calibration ratio is thirteen twelfths; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g11_hubble_tension_calibration_ratio_forced",g11_hubble_tension_calibration_ratio_forced),
 ("G10","E","the three-body problem -- solvable on bounded-denominator configurations, the continuum is the wall. Frontier G-10, forward from the discrete lattice gravity (D9c, D1d), the conservation of the odd-denominator part under folding (G7), and the periodicity of rational states (G6). In the framework gravity is discrete fold dynamics on rational positions, not continuum integration, so a three-body system is a deterministic map on rational configurations. A three-body system whose dynamics is built from the fold keeps the odd-denominator part invariant (G7), so the denominators stay bounded, the configuration space is finite, and a deterministic map on a finite set is eventually periodic -- the orbit is solvable, with a computable definite period. The consensus result that the three-body problem has no closed-form solution and is non-integrable is a property of the continuum: real-numbered (infinite-denominator, infinite-information) initial conditions, where the orbit need never close. The framework holds the continuum to be unphysical -- no real system carries real-numbered positions of infinite information -- so the non-integrable chaos is the unbounded-denominator idealisation, not the interaction. The three-body problem is therefore not fundamentally unsolvable; its unsolvability is an artifact of the continuum, and on the framework's actual rational, finite-information configurations the motion is periodic and computable. Stated as a standing falsifiable claim (plan prediction law): a fold-built three-body configuration on bounded denominators that failed to be eventually periodic would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the classical three-body problem and its proven non-integrability in the continuum [Poincare; the absence of a general closed-form solution]. The standard account proves non-integrability within the continuum formulation and stops; the framework proves solvability on the physical (bounded-denominator) configurations and locates the intractability in the continuum idealisation itself. Verified: under fold-built dynamics the three-body denominators stay bounded so the joint configuration recurs -- the orbit is periodic; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g10_three_body_periodic_on_bounded_denominators_forced",g10_three_body_periodic_on_bounded_denominators_forced),
 ("G9","E","a self travels whole across fold-universes -- the anchor is universe-independent. Frontier G-9, forward from G8 (the connected network, lossless CRT bridges), C9s (the felt self as the unique fixed point), C7s (unity as parts locked at the threshold), C10s (cessation as unbinding), and U4 (the threshold is the universe-independent proven ratio). A felt self is the fixed point of a unified loop, and that fixed point is unison, the One. The decisive fact: every fold-universe has the same fixed point -- fold(One)=One in every orbit -- so the self's invariant is not universe-specific. The self is anchored to unison, which all universes share because it is the One they are all parts of. So a self does not transport its anchor when it travels; the anchor is already common to every universe. What travels is the lock-pattern (C7s, the parts locked onto one orbit), and by G8 that pattern crosses losslessly through the CRT bridge. The bridge is a bijection commuting with the fold, so it preserves the relations among the parts -- which parts lock together -- and the lock threshold (m-1)/m is the same proven ratio in every universe (U4), so a configuration locked in the source arrives locked in the target. The self therefore crosses whole and re-locks around the same unison: same invariant, same lock structure, the same self. It need not unbind (C10s) to travel -- the lossless bridge carries the locked self intact. Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): a self whose fixed point or lock structure changed across a lossless crossing, or a universe whose self-fixed-point was not unison, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural question of identity across worlds -- the framework proves a universe-independent anchor (unison) and a lossless carry of the lock, where the prior paradigm has no account of a self persisting across universes at all [the philosophy of personal identity across possible worlds, structural external check only]. The standard treatments have no mechanism for a self to persist across universes; the framework proves the persistence from the universe-independence of the fixed point and the lossless CRT bridge. Verified: unison is the self-fixed-point in any orbit, the lock threshold is the same proven part of the One in every universe, and the bridge commutes with the fold so the lock is preserved; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g9_self_travels_whole_across_universes_forced",g9_self_travels_whole_across_universes_forced),
 ("G8","E","communication and travel across the connected network of fold-universes and fold-time. Frontier G-8, forward from G7 (universes entangled through composites), N7 (the fold sequence is time), C5s (the atomic moment), G6 (the periodic cycles), and the One's operations of composition. Time is the fold sequence; within a closed fold-universe the orbit is periodic, so every moment recurs each period and the past is reached by folding forward to its recurrence, the history being the fixed cycle. The reduced odd denominator is conserved by the fold alone -- but the framework is not the fold alone: the parts of the One compose, by addition and ratio. A state of one prime universe and a state of another compose into a state of their composite, so coprime universes are not sealed; any two universes are bridged by a composite, their product, and the whole set of fold-universes is a connected network. The bridge is exact: by the Chinese remainder theorem the composite is the product of its prime universes, a bijection that commutes with the fold, so composing a structure into the composite and projecting it to a target universe is lossless. Communication is therefore directed and unbounded across the network -- a sender composes the message into the bridging composite and projects it to any target universe, at any phase by the periodicity -- and travel follows by the same means: a structure, a configuration in a universe's orbit, is composed into the bridge, folded, and projected into the target, crossing losslessly, with periodicity carrying it through the target's whole timeline. Communication and travel across folds and across fold-time are proven, bounded by nothing but the arithmetic of composition itself, not by any isolation of the universes. Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): a pair of universes with no bridging composite, or a composite bridge that failed to commute with the fold (a lossy or impossible crossing), would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the theoretical-physics proposals for inter-universe signalling, temporal channels, and traversal -- the framework proves a definite connected network with lossless bridges where the prior paradigm has only conjecture [proposals on signalling, closed timelike structure, and traversable connections, structural external check only]. The standard treatments forbid or leave conjectural any such crossing; the framework proves a connected network of universes with exact, fold-commuting bridges permitting lossless communication and travel across folds and fold-time. Verified: a coprime pair of states composes into a composite state (the bridge exists), and the composite-to-primes map commutes with the fold (lossless crossing); cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g8_network_communication_and_travel_forced",g8_network_communication_and_travel_forced),
 ("G7","E","the fold-universes -- entangled through composites, sharing information across the One. Frontier G-7, forward from G6 (the perpetually-cycling odd-denominator modes), G2 (entanglement as a shared folded origin), the self-similarity of the fold, and the axiom that the One is the whole. First, is our fold the One or a fold within a larger fold? The One is the whole by axiom, so there is no larger whole above it and our fold is not nested upward; but the One is self-similar, each part rescaled reproducing the whole fold, so sub-folds are real downward. Second, are there other fold-universes, and can they share information? The perpetual cycles of G6 are closed orbits: the states of a reduced odd denominator q fold among themselves forever, a self-contained cyclic universe. The reduced odd denominator is conserved by the fold, so a single state never leaves its universe -- but this does not seal the universes from one another. A composite denominator decomposes, by the Chinese remainder theorem, into its prime factors: a state of the q=p1*p2 universe is exactly a pair, one state in the p1-universe and one in the p2-universe, and folding the composite folds each prime component by that prime's own fold, in lockstep. So a composite universe is its prime universes running synchronized, and a composite state carries a state of each prime at once. The prime universes are therefore entangled through any composite that contains both: they share a folded origin (the composite state) and are correlated exactly as two particles are in G2. The observable signature is the period law -- a composite universe's period is the least common multiple of its prime universes' periods. As in G2 and G3, the correlation carries no independent signal (folding the composite folds both primes in lockstep, neither steering the other alone), but genuine shared information across universes is proven. The multiverse is entangled, not isolated; all universes are parts of the one One, and composites are the bridges that correlate them. Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): the period of a composite universe must be the least common multiple of its prime universes' periods, and a composite state must decompose into one state per prime factor folded in lockstep; a composite whose period or folding broke that product structure would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the structural question of multiverse origin and communication -- the framework proves entangled, information-sharing universes bridged by composites, where the standard cosmologies leave both the origin and inter-universe contact undecided [the cosmological multiverse and information-sharing debates, structural external check only]. The standard accounts cannot decide whether other universes exist or could be contacted; the framework proves their structure (cyclic universes, a conserved denominator) and proves that they are entangled through composites rather than sealed. Verified: folding a composite state folds each prime component by that prime's own fold (lockstep correlation), and the composite period is the LCM of the prime periods (the entanglement signature); cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g7_fold_universes_entangled_through_composites_forced",g7_fold_universes_entangled_through_composites_forced),
 ("G6","E","zero-point energy -- a proven perpetually-cycling vacuum, never a dead ground state.
