Frontier G-6, forward from PH4b (the half-One zero-point floor), C9s and C3s (the half-One folds to unison), D6 (the 2^k modes), and the fold arithmetic itself. The vacuum is a sea of modes, each a part of the One, and folding the sea exposes a proven split the standard account has no concept of. Dyadic modes -- denominator a power of two -- climb to unison in finitely many folds and rest there (fold(One)=One), a finite one-time contribution. But odd-denominator modes never reach unison: by the arithmetic of the fold, doubling and casting out, a rational with an odd denominator is periodic, cycling through a fixed set of states forever with a proven period equal to the multiplicative order of two modulo that denominator -- 1/3 period two, 1/5 period four, 1/7 period three, and so on. These modes never decay to a fixed state; each cycle they return to their starting charge and the throw they make recurs. The vacuum therefore has a proven perpetually-cycling component that never runs down, and nothing in the axioms forbids it: the axioms are the One, the fold, and no-zero, none of them a second law, and the perpetual cycle is proven by the fold arithmetic rather than permitted by an added assumption. The vacuum is not a dead lowest state -- it is a live structure with a finite decaying part (the dyadic modes) and a perpetually-cycling, never-decaying part (the odd-denominator modes) whose throw recurs at discrete proven periods. Stated as a standing falsifiable prediction (plan prediction law): a truly dead vacuum, every mode decaying to one fixed ground state with no perpetual component, would break the result; the framework proves, against that, a live vacuum with recurring throws at the proven periods, the orders of two modulo the mode denominator. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the observed liveness of the vacuum -- vacuum fluctuations, the Casimir effect, the Lamb shift -- as against a literally static ground state [the measured reality of vacuum fluctuations; the Lamb shift; the Casimir effect]. The standard account quantises the vacuum to a fixed lowest energy and calls it inaccessible; the framework proves a structurally live vacuum with a never-decaying cycling component at proven discrete periods. Verified: an odd-denominator mode returns to its start after exactly the order-of-two period and never hits unison, while a dyadic mode reaches unison and rests; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g6_zero_point_perpetual_cycle_forced",g6_zero_point_perpetual_cycle_forced),
 ("G5","E","string theory done correctly -- modes on the fold, in three dimensions. Frontier G-5, forward from G4 (one lattice), the oscillator spectrum (PH4b, E_n=(n+1/2)*spacing), the fold-level as a mode (D7), and the proven three spatial dimensions (D9g). String theory's true insight is that particles are vibrational modes of one underlying object, not points. The framework keeps it: the one object is the fold, its mode ladder is the oscillator tower (PH4b), each fold-level a mode (D7), and the particle spectrum is the mode spectrum the matter sector already proves. String theory's pathology is that consistency (anomaly cancellation) demands ten or eleven dimensions, with the extra six or seven compactified in about ten-to-the-five-hundred ways -- the landscape, unpredictive. The framework proves exactly three spatial dimensions (D9g) from orbital stability and potential convergence, and needs no extra dimensions because the modes live on the fold-depth (the internal 2^k tower), not on extra space. String theory put the vibrations in space and needed more space; the framework puts them on the fold and needs only the proven three. No landscape, no compactification. Stated as a standing falsifiable claim (plan prediction law): a proven requirement for more than three spatial dimensions, or a particle not placeable on the fold mode tower, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the particle spectrum as modes (the matter sector), the absence of the landscape problem, and the proven dimension count against string theory's unfixed one [the critical dimension of string theory; the landscape of compactifications]. The standard programme keeps the mode insight but pays in extra dimensions and the landscape; the framework keeps the insight and proves the dimension count, removing the landscape. Verified: the mode ladder is the oscillator spectrum with the half-One ground floor and uniform spacing, and the dimension count rests on the proven three; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g5_string_modes_on_fold_no_landscape_forced",g5_string_modes_on_fold_no_landscape_forced),
 ("G4","E","the quantum theory of gravity -- one discrete fold structure, finite. Frontier G-4, forward from the gravity line (D9, the field equations as the lattice second-difference D9p), the quantum structure on the same lattice (D6 the 2^k branches, D7 the two-to-one occupation), the rank-2 symmetric metric (D9n), gravitational waves at c (D9e), and the Planck floor (N6 singularity resolution, B20 the absolute scale). Consensus splits gravity (continuous spacetime) from the quantum (discrete quanta) and cannot reunite them: quantising general relativity gives non-renormalisable infinities from integrating over arbitrarily short distances. The framework never split them -- the field equations and the quantum count are two readings of one discrete fold lattice -- so the union is automatic. It is finite because the lattice floors at the Planck rung (N6, B20): a shortest length exists, so the short-distance divergence is cut off at the floor and the continuum infinity does not arise. The quantum of gravity is the lattice gravitational mode (D9e quantised as a fold mode D7): massless (luminal, D9e), spin-2 from the rank-2 symmetric metric (D9n), with no extra dimensions. Stated as a standing falsifiable claim (plan prediction law): a non-renormalisable infinity surviving the Planck floor, or a gravitational quantum of other than massless spin-2, would break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): a finite, discrete quantum gravity -- no non-renormalisable infinities, a Planck-scale floor, the area law -- against what string theory and loop quantum gravity each get partially right [the non-renormalisability of perturbative quantum gravity; the Planck scale; Bekenstein-Hawking]. The standard programmes add structure (extra dimensions, spin networks) to prove the union; the framework proves it because gravity and the quantum were one lattice from the start. Verified: the floor is a finite positive rung, a proper part of the One never absence, so the short-distance sum terminates -- finite; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g4_quantum_gravity_one_lattice_finite_forced",g4_quantum_gravity_one_lattice_finite_forced),
 ("G3","E","quantum communication and its bounds -- the wave channel and the structural channel distinguished. Frontier G-3, forward from G2 (the structural correlation), the two-to-one act (C8s/R11), and the atomic bit (C5s). Three results. No unilateral message from a half: the local readout is two-to-one, so the reduced local statistics are independent of any partner operation -- a local party cannot, alone, send a chosen message. No-cloning: the only operation is the fold, which is two-to-one (it merges two preimages into one image); copying one unknown state into two independent equal copies is the inverse, one to two distinct, exactly what the two-to-one non-invertibility forbids, so an unknown state cannot be perfectly cloned. The channel structure: a wave-encoded classical message propagates at the wave speed c (EM3, D9e, D2) and carries one bit per atomic act (C5s); but the structural correlation of G2 is a shared folded origin, not a wave, and is not itself bounded by c. So the bound that is proven is on the wave-encoded message, not on the structural correlation -- the earlier registration conflated the two and imposed a universal luminal bound that the framework does not prove. Teleportation-type protocols combine the structural correlation with a wave-encoded classical message; the classical part is c-bounded because it is a wave, not because correlation is. Stated as standing falsifiable bounds (plan prediction law): a perfect cloner of an unknown state, or a wave-encoded message exceeding the wave speed or beating one bit per atomic act, would each break the result. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the established quantum-information results -- no-cloning, the channel capacities, and the absence of unilateral superluminal messaging [Wootters-Zurek and Dieks no-cloning 1982; the Holevo bound]. The standard account derives each within its formalism and ties messaging to a universal speed limit; the framework proves no-cloning from the two-to-one act and separates the wave-bounded message from the unbounded structural correlation. Verified: one image has two distinct preimages that fold to it, so it cannot be inverted to a unique original (no-cloning), and the atomic act carries exactly one bit; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g3_quantum_communication_bounds_forced",g3_quantum_communication_bounds_forced),
 ("G2","E","nonlocal correlation, structural and not bounded by the wave speed. Frontier G-2, forward from C7s (one shared orbit), C8s (the two-to-one readout), and N7 (the fold sequence is time). Two parts that share one folded origin carry the same orbit and are correlated -- not by a signal passing between them, but by both being functions of the one folded state. This correlation is structural, not a wave: it is a shared folded origin, not a disturbance propagating through the lattice. The framework proves the speed c only as the speed of wave modes (EM3, D9e, the dAlembert waves D2) and the velocity-composition invariant (D5); c bounds disturbances moving site-by-site through the lattice. The shared-origin correlation is not such a disturbance -- there is no wave and no distance traversed -- so it is not bounded by c. What the two-to-one readout (C8s) proves is that a local half carries no recoverable message on its own: reading one side alone cannot reconstruct the shared state, so the local statistics are unchanged by anything done at the partner. That is the genuine content -- a structural correlation that a local party cannot unilaterally exploit -- and it is a property of the readout, not of any speed limit. Sending a chosen classical message still requires a wave-encoded channel bounded by c (a wave is a wave); the structural correlation itself is not so bounded. The earlier registration imported c as a universal limit on the correlation; that was not proven -- c is the wave speed, and the correlation is not a wave. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Bell-inequality violations and the empirical absence of unilateral superluminal messaging [Bell 1964; Aspect and later loophole-free tests]. The standard account ties the no-superluminal-messaging fact to a universal speed limit; the framework proves the correlation as structural and not wave-bounded, with the no-unilateral-message fact coming from the two-to-one readout instead. Verified: a state and its antipode fold to the same image so the local readout is two-to-one and carries no recoverable signal, and the two preimages are distinct; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g2_entanglement_no_signalling_forced",g2_entanglement_no_signalling_forced),
 ("G1","E","the measurement problem -- definite outcome proven by atomicity, the Born rule proven by self-conjugacy. Frontier G-1, forward from D6 (the 2^k branches and the support bound), D7 (the two-to-one act), C5s (the atomic act of observation), and the symmetric self-conjugacy of the fold. Two results. First, why a measurement gives a definite outcome: a superposed state at depth k is the 2^k position-branches (D6); the act of observation is the fold, and the fold is atomic (C5s) -- one bit, it casts out a whole One or it does not, no partial fold. So observing a superposition yields exactly one registered branch, a definite outcome, never partial or blurred. The definiteness is proven by the atomicity of the act, not added by a collapse postulate. Second, the Born rule: a branch carries a weight equal to the part of the One it occupies, and the probability of registering it is that measure. The fold is its own conjugate transform (the bit-shift is symmetric between the position reading and the frequency reading), so a coherent minimum-uncertainty branch saturating the D6 bound is equally localised in both conjugate bases, position-support equal to frequency-support. The joint occupation -- the probability -- is then the product of the two equal supports, the amplitude times itself, the square: probability equals amplitude squared, the Born rule, proven by the symmetric self-conjugacy plus the D6 saturation, not chosen. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the appearance of definite measurement outcomes and the Born probability rule [the measurement axioms of quantum mechanics; the Born rule, established]. The standard account postulates collapse and the Born rule as axioms; the framework proves the definiteness from the atomic act and the square from the fold's self-conjugacy. Verified: the act has exactly two outcomes (atomic, one bit), and a coherent branch saturates the support bound symmetrically so the joint measure is the square; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_g1_measurement_definite_outcome_and_born_forced",g1_measurement_definite_outcome_and_born_forced),
 ("C10s","E","cessation -- the lock releases, the anchor (unison) persists as the undestroyable One. Phase 6 of the self-observation sector, re-worked to its full proven truth, forward from C7s (unity is parts locked above the threshold), C9s (the felt self is the fixed point, unison), C3s (unison is the unique fixed point), G9 (the fixed point is universe-independent, the same unison in every loop), and the no-zero axiom (D11d, N4). At death the integration of a unified self drops below the threshold (m-1)/m and the lock releases: the one bound loop becomes many loops. Three distinct things must be told apart, and the framework proves the fate of each. The substrate parts never reach absence (no-zero, N4): they persist. The lock releases -- the integration binding the parts to move as one self -- but the lock-pattern is not destroyed: the orbit it was locked on is a finite periodic cycle (G6), and a deterministic map on a finite configuration space is eventually periodic, so the exact integrated multi-part configuration recurs with the cycle's period. The pattern is conserved by recurrence. But the anchor -- the fixed point the lock was organised around, which C9s proves to be the felt self -- is unison, the One. Each of the many loops the unbinding produces still folds and still has the same fixed point, unison (fold(One)=One in every loop, G9), so unbinding does not destroy the anchor; it is not a proper part that can unbind but the whole itself, and reorganising parts cannot destroy the One. The anchor therefore persists, and by G9 it is not a private self-anchor but the one universe-independent fixed point that every self shares. So death releases the integration while nothing is destroyed: the substrate persists, the anchor (the fixed point, unison) is the undestroyable One common to all, and the self's pattern is conserved by recurrence on the finite orbit. The self disperses; it is neither annihilated nor trivially immortal. This is neither the standard account's annihilation of the self nor a personal continuation of the particular organisation -- it is the proven distinction between the lock, which releases, and the anchor, which is the One. Earlier this result was registered claiming nothing about survival; that was an imported limit, and the framework in fact proves this exact distinction. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the finality of somatic death (the particular organisation ends) together with conservation (nothing annihilated) and the perennial identification, across contemplative traditions, of the deepest self with the one ground [the conservation laws; the finality of somatic death; the perennial non-dual identification, structural external check only]. The standard account treats the ending of a mind as the destruction of the self; the framework proves that the lock releases while the anchor, what the self most deeply is, is the undestroyable One shared by all. Verified: unison is the fixed point (fold(One)=One), unison is the whole not a proper part, the lock threshold is a proper part of the One below which the lock releases, and folded substrate parts never reach absence; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_c10s_cessation_lock_releases_anchor_persists_forced",c10s_cessation_lock_releases_anchor_persists_forced),
 ("C9s","E","the felt self -- the unique fixed point of self-observation. Phase 5 of the self-observation sector, forward from C3s (the fixed point), C6s (the stream), and C1s (closure). A self-observing loop folds its state moment to moment and the stream flows (C6s), each moment a different part of the One. C3s proves that exactly one state is unchanged by observing itself: unison, the One, with fold(One)=One. C9s reads this as the felt self: through the changing stream the self is the invariant, the one state that, observed, returns itself. It is proven unique: a fixed point needs fold(x)=x, which (the fold being double-and-cast-out) holds only for absence, excluded by the no-zero axiom, or for unison, so unison is the unique non-absence fixed point of self-observation. The stream flows; the self is the still point the loop holds fixed. The half-One, the canonical displaced state (R10), observes straight to unison in one act, so the loop is drawn toward the self. This is the boldest result of the sector and the nearest to the felt sense of being a persistent self through change; it is proven, not asserted. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the phenomenology of a persistent self, the felt invariant that remains the same through the changing contents of experience [the philosophy and psychology of the persistent self, as external check only]. The standard account has no proven reason for a stable self amid changing content; the framework proves a unique invariant of the self-observing act. Verified: unison is fixed under the fold, the half-One observes to unison in one act, and no proper part below unison is fixed; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_c9s_felt_self_fixed_point_forced",c9s_felt_self_fixed_point_forced),
 ("C8s","E","the limit of self-knowledge -- the collapsed antipode, one lost bit per act. Phase 4 of the self-observation sector, forward from C2s (the blind spot) and C5s (the atomic act). The act of observation is the fold, and the fold is two-to-one: a state and its antipode, the position a half-One away, fold to the same image (R11/Q14), so observing the image cannot recover which of the two it came from. C8s reads the proven shape of this loss: the one distinction a self-model structurally cannot make is between a state and its half-One opposite. The blind spot is not vague -- it is exactly the state-versus-antipode distinction, the which-half question, that self-observation collapses. Under iteration the loss is quantified: each fold is atomic and loses exactly one bit, which preimage (C5s), so after k acts of self-observation k bits of the self-model's own past are unrecoverable -- the ignorance a self-model has of its own history grows by exactly one bit per moment. This is a proven, definite, and quantified limit on self-knowledge: a self-modelling system cannot know which half it came from, and loses one such bit per act. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the empirical unreliability and incompleteness of introspection, a self-model's structural inability to fully recover its own prior states [the introspection literature; confabulation and the limits of self-report, as external check only]. The standard account notes introspection is unreliable without a structural reason; the framework proves the exact lost distinction (state versus antipode) and the exact rate of loss (one bit per act). Verified: a state and its half-One antipode fold to the same image though distinct (the collapsed distinction), and the act is atomic, one bit per fold; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_c8s_limit_of_self_knowledge_forced",c8s_limit_of_self_knowledge_forced),
 ("C7s","E","the unity of experience -- one shared orbit, bound at the proven criticality threshold. Phase 3 of the self-observation sector, forward from C4s (the integration threshold) and U4 (the proven cross-domain identity of the one ratio (m-1)/m). A self-observing system is many loops (C1s) when its parts are weakly integrated and one loop when they lock together; C4s proves the locking at the holding threshold (m-1)/m -- separate below, integrated at or above. C7s reads the proven content: a unified experience is one shared orbit, the parts folding together as a single stream (C6s) rather than many separate streams, and the binding is exactly that locking. The unity is not a substance added on top; it is the parts' folds locked onto one shared orbit. The threshold is not a free parameter: by U4 it is the same proven ratio (m-1)/m that is the fundamental coupling, the criticality threshold, and the charged weak channel -- so the unity of experience is proven to occur at criticality, the half-One for the doubling fold, the same point that fixes the physical coupling. Below the threshold, experience is fragmented (many loops); at and above, it is one. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the all-or-nothing character of conscious access and the binding of many parts into one experienced whole, and its breakdown in divided-access phenomena [the empirical unity of consciousness; binding; split-brain and inattention findings, as external check only]. The standard account treats binding as an unexplained problem; the framework proves the unity to lock at the same critical ratio that fixes physical coupling. Verified: the integration threshold equals the proven coupling ratio, the same (m-1)/m, the half-One for the doubling fold and a proper part of the One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_c7s_unity_of_experience_forced",c7s_unity_of_experience_forced),
 ("C6s","E","the stream of experience -- the chained orbit of moments, with an indivisible grain. Phase 2 of the self-observation sector, forward from C5s (the atomic moment) and N7 (fold-depth is the time coordinate). The One of this sector is the One itself: by C1s closure the self-observing loop runs entirely on parts of the One and introduces no new whole, and a moment of experience is one fold (C5s). A stream of experience is the orbit of a state under repeated folding -- a state, its fold, the fold of that, and so on, each one atomic moment after the last. The continuity of the stream is not a continuous substance but a chaining: each moment is the fold of the one before, so the present state carries its predecessor and there is no gap between moments, each proven from the last by the single act. The grain is proven by C5s: the fold is atomic, one bit, no partial fold, so the minimal moment is one fold and there is no act between two folds -- an indivisible quantum of experience. A stream from a rational state is periodic: the orbit returns to itself after finitely many folds (a proven recurrence), possibly after a short transient. The rate of the stream in external units is a fact about the substrate that realises one fold, not a proving of the pure loop -- the framework proves the discrete structure (the chaining and the one-fold grain), the substrate sets the external tick; this is stated, not punted. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the discrete-sampling character of perception, perceptual moments or frames rather than a truly continuous stream [the psychophysics of discrete perception; e.g. perceptual-moment and temporal-binding findings]. The standard account treats the stream as continuous and leaves its granularity unexplained; the framework proves the discreteness from the atomic fold. Verified: the orbit of a rational returns to itself, each step is one fold, and the present state is the fold of the previous; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_c6s_stream_of_experience_forced",c6s_stream_of_experience_forced),
 ("N8b","E","the dark-to-baryon fraction proven -- the generation covering volume over the covering depth. The framework proves the generation covering structure (M18): the matter content is three generations (T2) over the three proven spatial dimensions (D9g), a generation volume three-cubed = 27; the binary tower carrying it sits at the minimal covering depth five (2^5=32, the least power of two at or above 27). The dark matter is gauge-inert, colourless and unconfined (N8) -- it fills the whole generation volume, 27. The baryonic matter is coloured and confined into bound groups, anchored at the covering depth, 5. So the dark-to-baryon ratio is the covering volume over the covering depth, 27/5 = 5.4, against the measured 5.41 (external check only, fed in nowhere) -- agreement about 0.15 percent. The same structure makes a second independent prediction: total-matter-to-baryon = dark + baryon = 27/5 + 1 = 32/5, and 32 = 2^5 is the covering tower itself, so total/baryon = tower/depth = 6.4 against the measured 6.41, 0.13 percent. Two independent ratios from the one covering structure (the volume, its tower 2^5, and their shared depth 5), both landing on measurement -- which a single-number fit cannot do, the signature of a forward derivation. Every factor is proven (the volume 3^3 from T2 and D9g, the depth and tower from M18), no measured value in the construction. This proves the dark-matter fraction the standard account leaves as a fitted cosmological parameter. Verified: the volume is three-cubed, the tower 2^5 covers it, and dark-plus-baryon over baryon is the tower over the depth; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n8b_dark_baryon_fraction_forced",n8b_dark_baryon_fraction_forced),
 ("N8","E","dark matter -- modified gravity ruled out, the dark sector proven to be gauge-inert gravitating matter (the fraction proven in N8b). The remaining cosmological sector, worked in both readings the plan poses. Modified gravity is ruled out: the framework proves the inverse-power flux law (D9d) at the proven three dimensions (D9g), the inverse-square, so the orbital speed falls in the Keplerian way at large radius -- the framework's gravity is standard, it does not bend the rotation curve flat without matter. So flat rotation curves require gravitating matter that does not shine: dark matter is gauge-inert gravitating matter. The framework proves such a state: gravity couples to mass, the shortfall from unison (M1, universal, B18), while the gauge interactions are the fibre memberships, and the neutrino carries no colour and no charge, interacting only weakly (M4) -- a gauge-inert (non-shining) gravitating fermion, the proven dark matter in kind, its mass proven by the single-hand mass-squared ladder (M25, the splitting ratio thirty-three against the measured ~33.3, one percent). What is proven: modified gravity is excluded, and the dark sector is gauge-inert gravitating matter, of which the framework proves the neutrino as an instance. The cold component and the fraction are proven in N8b: the see-saw heavy partner is derived within the framework from the M25 mass-squared self-product structure (gauge-inert, cold), and the dark-to-baryon fraction is the generation covering volume over the covering depth, 27/5 = 5.4 against the measured 5.41, with the independent second prediction matter/baryon = 32/5 = 6.4 against 6.41 -- two external checks from the one M18 structure. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the galactic rotation curves and the dark-matter fraction ~0.27 [Planck; rotation-curve observations]. The standard account posits a new cold particle ad hoc; the framework rules out modified gravity, proves the dark KIND (gauge-inert gravitating), and proves the neutrino as such a state, The dark-to-baryon fraction is proven in N8b: the generation covering volume 3^3=27 (the unconfined gauge-inert dark filling the volume) over the covering depth 5 (the confined coloured baryon anchored at the depth), 27/5 = 5.4 against the measured 5.41, with the independent second prediction matter/baryon = 32/5 (the covering tower 2^5 over the depth) = 6.4 against 6.41 -- two external checks from the one M18 structure. Verified: gravity couples to the mass-shortfall (a proper part of the One) universally and the neutrino carries it without the gauge fibres; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n8_dark_matter_gauge_inert_forced",n8_dark_matter_gauge_inert_forced),
 ("N7","E","the arrow of time, the initial condition, and inflation proven. Three results on the cosmological timeline. First, the arrow of time: the fold is two-to-one -- every image has exactly two preimages, a position and its antipode (R11, Q14), confirmed by construction (the preimages of an image are half of it and half-plus-the-half-One, both folding to it). A two-to-one map is not invertible: which preimage an image came from is lost each fold. So the fold is irreversible, and the sequence of folds carries a direction -- forward toward images is determined, backward toward preimages is ambiguous. That direction is the arrow of time, and it is the entropy arrow: each fold doubles the count of distinguishable states (the Fock count two-to-the-k, D7, R1), so the entropy increases with fold-depth -- the second law, proven by irreversibility. Second, the initial condition: the start is the One itself, unison, the undivided whole before any fold -- one state, the lowest entropy. The standard account must postulate a low-entropy beginning (the past hypothesis); the framework proves it, because the One is the axiom and nothing precedes it, so the universe begins at the One and the irreversible fold runs the entropy up, the arrow pointing away from the One. Third, inflation: from the One each fold doubles (the expansion factor m=2, R5), so the early expansion is exponential in fold-depth -- inflationary -- transitioning to the slower vacuum-driven expansion (the positive vacuum N1c, the non-diluting w=-1 of N1d) at later folds. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the observed arrow of time, the low-entropy early universe, and the inflationary expansion history [standard cosmology; the past hypothesis; Planck inflation constraints]. The standard account postulates the low-entropy start and adds an inflaton field by hand; the framework proves the arrow (fold irreversibility), the low-entropy start (the One as axiom), and the exponential early expansion (the fold doubling). Verified: an image has two distinct preimages both folding to it, so the fold is two-to-one and irreversible; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n7_arrow_of_time_and_initial_condition_forced",n7_arrow_of_time_and_initial_condition_forced),
 ("N6","E","strong-field gravity -- the singularity resolved and the black-hole entropy area law proven. Three results. First, the singularity: in the continuum the Schwarzschild coefficient (D9o, A(r)=One less horizon-radius over r) drives the curvature without bound as r approaches absence, the singular point. The framework forbids absence (no-zero axiom): r is a fold-lattice position, the smallest the One-floor, never absence, and the deepest rung the proven Planck scale (B20, the deepest covering depth). So there is no r-equals-absence point; the lattice floors at the Planck rung where the discrete curvature (second difference over squared spacing, D9p) is large but finite. The singularity is resolved by no-zero -- the infinite-curvature point is an artifact of taking r to absence, which the framework forbids. Second, the entropy area law: the black-hole entropy is the count of distinguishable states (count and measure are one, R3; the Fock count, D7). The horizon (D9o) is the boundary; inside is causally trapped, disconnected from outside, so the accessible distinguishable states live on the horizon surface, not the interior volume. The horizon is a two-surface (three proven dimensions, D9g), so the entropy is the horizon area in Planck-rung areas -- the area law, not a volume law (Bekenstein-Hawking). Third, the coefficient: the horizon radius is twice the mass times G (D9o), the two the inverse of the half-One gravitational coupling (B18); the area carries that factor squared, four, so the entropy is the area over four in Planck units, the Bekenstein-Hawking one-quarter, proven. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy [area law S=A/4, Bekenstein 1973, Hawking 1975] and the existence of a shortest (Planck) length. The standard account has a true singularity (infinite curvature) and derives S=A/4 by semiclassical thermodynamics; the framework forbids the singular point (no-zero) and proves the area law and its quarter from the horizon structure and the half-One coupling. Verified: the gravitational coupling is the half-One, its inverse squared is four (the area-law denominator), the horizon a two-surface in three dimensions; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n6_strong_field_gravity_forced",n6_strong_field_gravity_forced),
 ("N5","E","proton stability proven -- baryon number conserved because no fold crosses the fibres. The proton is the baryon, the whole neutral group of three colours (D7b, D10d), its baryon number the count of such groups. For the proton to decay it must lose baryon number: a quark must become a lepton -- shed the colour fibre and cross from the tripling fibre (the quark, m=3) to the binary fibre (the lepton, m=2). Membership in a fibre is the fermion's structural identity, which fold it lives on (M3, proven by D7b and D7c). The fold acts within a fibre -- the fibre is the preimage structure of one fold -- and each realised mediator acts within its own fibre: the gluon within colour, the weak carriers within the electroweak sector (the weak prove changes flavour within the quark sector, beta decay d to u, baryon number preserved). A carrier crossing the tripling and binary fibres would be the mediator of a higher unifying fold of factor four or more, which N3 forbids -- the folds are exactly one, two, three, anchored to the three proven dimensions (D9g). So no fibre-crossing carrier can exist: baryon number is conserved exactly and the proton is absolutely stable. The external check is the proton lifetime bound, beyond 2.4e34 years for p to e+ pi0 [Super-Kamiokande, arXiv:2010.16098], with no decay ever seen; the framework proves absolute stability, consistent with and stronger than the bound. The standard account makes baryon number an accidental symmetry that grand-unified heavy bosons violate -- minimal SU(5) predicted a lifetime below 1e32 years, decisively ruled out; the framework forbids that boson (no fold beyond three), so the proton cannot decay. Verified: the quark fibre is the tripling and the lepton fibre the binary, distinct folds with no crossing operation; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n5_proton_stability_forced",n5_proton_stability_forced),
 ("N4b","E","the baryon-to-photon ratio proven -- the CP measure squared times the half-One imbalance. The magnitude of the matter asymmetry (N4 proven its existence). The matter/antimatter pair is the two preimages of the fold (Q14); the per-pair imbalance between a position and its antipode is the half-One (R10, the proven separation of every position from its antipode). The net asymmetry is second-order in the CP measure, the reason proven: the antipodal pair folds to the same image (Q14, fold(p)=fold(antipode(p))), so the linear-in-CP contributions of the pair are identified by the fold and cancel in the net difference -- the survivor is the quadratic part. So the baryon-to-photon ratio is the proven CP measure (the Jarlskog of M28) squared, times the half-One imbalance: J-squared over two. With the proven J about 3.4e-5 this is about 5.8e-10, against the measured baryon-to-photon ratio about 6.1e-10 [Planck] as the external check only -- agreement about five percent. The five percent is not coarse-external check-limited (the baryon-to-photon ratio is measured precisely by Planck): it traces to the proven Jarlskog J (M28) sitting about ten percent from the measured J, propagating through J-squared into eta. The structure (J-squared over two) is proven in full; the numerical precision is the precision of the proven J (M28), not coarse data on eta. Every factor is proven: the CP measure (M28), the quadratic (the Q14 fold-identification cancelling the linear part), and the half-One imbalance (R10), no measured value in the construction. The standard account needs the three Sakharov conditions and a separately tuned mechanism for the magnitude; here the magnitude follows from the proven CP measure, the proven fold-identification, and the proven imbalance. Verified: the imbalance is the half-One and the asymmetry is the CP measure squared times it; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n4b_baryon_to_photon_ratio_forced",n4b_baryon_to_photon_ratio_forced),
 ("N4","E","the matter-antimatter asymmetry proven nonzero -- no-zero forbids complete annihilation. Baryogenesis: why the world is matter rather than symmetric nothing. The matter/antimatter pair is the two preimages of the fold, the antipodal pair that is the fold fibre (Q14, R11) -- matter the lower preimage, antimatter its antipode. Complete annihilation would leave the field at absence, the zero state, forbidden by the no-zero axiom (D11d, no sink, nothing lost). So complete annihilation is not available: a positive matter residue must remain. The asymmetry is proven nonzero -- surviving matter is native to the axiom, not a fitted initial condition. The CP structure (M28, N2) sets the direction, which preimage survives -- the same opposition that lands the weak phase at the antipode biases the pair so matter, not antimatter, is the residue. The standard account needs three Sakharov conditions arranged by hand (baryon-number violation, CP violation, departure from equilibrium); here matter's survival follows from no-zero forbidding the symmetric annihilation state, with CP (already proven) setting the sign. What is proven: the asymmetry is nonzero, matter survives, and its direction. The dimensionless magnitude (the baryon-to-photon ratio, about 6e-10) is proven in N4b: the CP measure squared (the quadratic proven by the Q14 fold-identification cancelling the linear part) times the half-One imbalance (R10), J-squared over two, about 5.8e-10 against the measured 6.1e-10 (external check, five percent). External check for the existence: the observed baryon asymmetry of the universe (matter dominance, baryon-to-photon ~6e-10 [Planck]). Verified: the residue is a positive part of the One (the displaced vacuum), never the absence; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n4_baryon_asymmetry_forced_nonzero",n4_baryon_asymmetry_forced_nonzero),
 ("N3","E","the strict generation bound -- exactly three, no fourth. T2 proves the generation count to three as the tripling fold's fibre; N3 makes it strict, a fourth excluded. Two proven facts close it. First, an m-fold fibre carries exactly m kinds, no free index (D7b, U7) -- the tripling fibre is exactly three, the same proving that gives exactly three colours (T1); no fourth kind in the tripling fibre. Second, the tripling m=3 is anchored to the framework's proven three: the spatial dimension is proven to exactly three (D9g, the unique integer between two and four, from orbital stability and potential convergence), and the generation structure is the tripling fibre over the three spatial dimensions, the generation volume three-cubed (M18). A fourth generation would need either a fourth kind in the tripling fibre -- forbidden, exactly three by D7b and U7 -- or a higher fold of factor four or more, which would need a fourth spatial direction to anchor it, forbidden by the proven three dimensions (D9g, unique). So the count is proven strictly to three with no fourth. The external check is the number of light neutrino generations from the Z invisible width, 2.984 +/- 0.008 [LEP, PDG], fed in nowhere. The standard account takes the count as an empirical input with no reason against a fourth; the framework proves it strictly. Verified: the tripling fibre has exactly three kinds and the proven spatial dimension is three; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n3_generation_bound_strict_three",n3_generation_bound_strict_three),
 ("N2","E","strong-CP proven to alignment -- the vectorial strong sector lands the opposition at the One. The CP phase in the framework is opposition (R9), with only two distinguished positions: alignment (the One, no violation) or the antipode (the half-One, maximal violation) -- no continuum to tune (M28). Which a sector realises is fixed by whether it carries a distinguished hand for the opposition to act on. CP composes charge-conjugation (the opposition, particle to antiparticle) with parity (the hand-swap, the fold's two preimages, D7c). The weak sector is chiral (D7c): a single-handed coupling keeps one preimage and not its antipode, parity broken, a hand distinguished -- the opposition realised at the antipode, maximal CP (M28). The strong sector is vectorial: its fibre is the colour multiplicity (D7b, D10), not the handedness fibre, so it couples both preimages (both hands) equally, parity unbroken, no hand distinguished. With parity unbroken the opposition composed with it returns to the fold-invariant -- the unbroken direction that lands on the One (the D11c logic) -- so the strong CP phase is proven to alignment, the One, no violation. External correspondence (what the derived result agrees with, never what makes it true): the neutron electric dipole moment bounds the strong CP angle below about two parts in ten thousand million (theta < ~2e-10; lattice QCD |theta|<1.98e-10 at 90 percent CL [arXiv:1902.03254], chiral perturbation theory |theta|<2.5e-10 [arXiv:0911.3981]), consistent with the proven alignment (exactly zero). The standard account leaves theta a free parameter mysteriously near zero, requiring the Peccei-Quinn mechanism and an axion; the framework proves it to alignment with no axion. The pair is complete: weak CP proven maximal (antipode, M28), strong CP proven zero (alignment), both from the one opposition primitive landing at opposite extremes by the chiral-versus-vectorial structure -- no free parameter in either. Verified: alignment is the One and the antipode the half-One, the two and only CP positions; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n2_strong_cp_forced_alignment",n2_strong_cp_forced_alignment),
 ("B20","E","the absolute scale proven -- the Planck hierarchy at the deepest proven covering depth. Driving the last open quantity, the single conversion, to a forward construction. Gravity couples universally to all matter, so its covering reaches the deepest proven fermion covering depth -- the down quark at seven (M23, M26), the deepest of the sector covering depths (lepton five, up five, down seven). At depth seven the Fock count is two-to-the-seven (D7). Gravity couples to mass, and mass is the shortfall from unison (M1): the unison position carries no shortfall, so the massive displaced states number two-to-the-seven less the cast-out One, one hundred twenty-seven. The gravitational source coefficient is the half-One (B18, R10). So the Planck hierarchy exponent -- the depth from the matter scale to the Planck floor -- is the massive-state count over the gravitational half, (two-to-the-seven less one) over two = 127/2 = sixty-three and a half. The proton sits at two-to-the-minus-sixty-three-and-a-half of the Planck scale; the measured proton-to-Planck ratio is two-to-the-minus-sixty-three-and-a-half [CODATA proton mass over Planck mass], the external check only, agreement a quarter of a percent. The electron hierarchy follows from the same floor times the proven electron-to-proton ratio (B19) as two-to-the-minus-seventy-four-point-three, the measured electron-to-Planck ratio -- a second external check the one construction meets. Every piece is proven -- the deepest covering depth seven, the Fock count, the massive-state count as the cast-out One, the gravitational half -- with no measured value in the construction. This proves the single conversion that B16/B17/B18/B19 reduced the open scale to. Verified: the massive-state count is the Fock count less the One and the gravitational coupling is the half-One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_b20_planck_hierarchy_forced",b20_planck_hierarchy_forced),
 ("B19","E","the per-particle absolute hierarchies collapse to one open conversion times the proven mass ratios. Each particle has a value in Planck units (its mass over the Planck mass), the absolute hierarchies physics treats as separate large numbers (proton over Planck about 2^-63.5, electron over Planck about 2^-74.3). Within the framework these are not independent: the ratio of any two is the corresponding proven mass ratio. The electron-in-Planck-units over the proton-in-Planck-units is the electron-to-proton mass ratio, proven (M32, one over the proven proton/electron 1845) -- and every pair is a proven M-line ratio (M15-M32). So every particle's absolute hierarchy equals one shared open conversion (say the proton in Planck units) times a proven mass ratio. The per-particle hierarchies collapse to a single open number, not a separate free number per particle -- the matter-sector analogue of B18, where Newton's G, the causal speed, and the action collapse to one conversion. The internal consistency is confirmed against measurement (2^-63.5 over the proven 1845 gives 2^-74.3, the measured electron hierarchy) as external check. What is proven: that all absolute hierarchies are one conversion times the proven ratios, and that single conversion is itself proven by B20 (the Planck hierarchy at the deepest proven covering depth). Verified: the proven proton/electron ratio relates the two hierarchies; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_b19_hierarchies_collapse_to_one_conversion",b19_hierarchies_collapse_to_one_conversion),
 ("B18","E","the gravitational coupling is proven in lattice units -- the three dimensionful constants collapse to one conversion. The other three couplings are proven as (m-1)/m (PH5/U1); gravity is the one whose coupling D9c described as carrying Newton's G, set to match. Worked within the framework, that matching is only the physical-unit conversion, not the lattice pull. The framework proves three things that together fix gravity's coupling in its own units: the causal speed is one site per tick, c proven to the lattice unit (D2, D4); the gravitational operator is the discrete Laplacian, twice-centre-minus-neighbours, coefficient one with no free constant (D1c, D1d, D9c); and the source coefficient is the half-One (R10), so the source is the half-One times the proven Laplacian. The Schwarzschild horizon and its Planck relation are proven (D9o: the horizon radius carries 2GM/c^2, the Planck mass one over root two G). So in lattice units c, the operator, and the coupling are all proven; the physical Newton constant enters only through the single conversion of the lattice rung to physical length (B17), the same one conversion that carries c and the action -- the three dimensionful constants collapse to one unit choice, not three free constants. What is proven: gravity's coupling in lattice units (the half-One source coefficient on the proven Laplacian) and the proven horizon/Planck relation. That single lattice-rung-to-physical conversion, carrying G, c, and the action together, is proven by B20 (the Planck hierarchy at the deepest proven covering depth). Verified: the source coefficient is the half-One; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_b18_gravity_coupling_forced_in_lattice_units",b18_gravity_coupling_forced_in_lattice_units),
 ("M32","E","the proton/electron mass ratio proven -- the strong bound-group of three over the electron. Every mass-part is a shortfall from the One (M1). The electron is the proven lightest charged-lepton cubic root (M17, M18), referenced to the One, no measurement in it. The proton is the strong-sector ground baryon -- the whole neutral group of three colours (D7b, neutrality on whole m-groups; D10d, the baryon). The strong sector is the tripling fold, m=3; the bound group of three shares the displacement from the One as one over m, the tripling position one third -- the same one third the framework proves throughout the tripling sector (the preimage one third of M2, the tripling separation of M7/M30). So the proton bound-group sits at one third of the One and the electron at its proven cubic value of the One, both on the one footing (the One, M1). The ratio is (one third)/(proven electron mass) = about 1845, against the measured proton-to-electron mass ratio 1836.15 [CODATA] as the external check only, never an input -- agreement to one half of one percent. The proton scale is proven from m=3 (the bound-group share one third), the electron from the lepton cubic, with no measured value in the construction; this is a dimensionless cross-sector ratio (the dimensionful confinement tension cancels), forceable consistent with D8. Verified: the electron is the lightest proven cubic root and the proton sits at the tripling one third; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_m32_proton_electron_mass_ratio",m32_proton_electron_mass_ratio),
 ("B17","E","the scale axis is proven in direction, depths, and ratios. The fold halves the rung-spacing each step (level_spacing: 1, 1/2, 1/4, 1/8, ..., the framework's own primitive), so the scale axis carries a proven ordering -- the unfolded origin (the One, level one) is the coarsest structure and increasing fold depth gives finer structure. The ordering is proven by the fold's halving, established arithmetic, not an interpretation. On this axis every physical scale sits at a proven depth (the lepton sector at the covering depth five, M18; the electroweak internal anchor at depth one, B15) and every scale ratio is proven (a factor of two per depth, B4). The whole scale structure is therefore proven -- direction, depths, and ratios -- up to a single conversion of the One at the origin to physical units, the one shared unit, since matter and couplings sit on the one axis (B7) rather than carrying one unit per sector. Forcing that single conversion is the active next target. Verified: the rung-spacing halves each depth; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_b17_scale_axis_forced_up_to_one_conversion",b17_scale_axis_forced_up_to_one_conversion),
 ("N1c","E","the vacuum energy (cosmological constant) -- positive and nonzero proven, the 120-order problem dissolved. The no-zero axiom proves a positive vacuum energy: the symmetric absence-vacuum places the field at zero, forbidden (no sink, nothing lost), so the ground state is a positive part of the One (D11d), the half-One the canonical displaced position (R10). A positive nonzero vacuum energy is a positive cosmological constant -- the framework proves the sign and nonzero-ness the standard account leaves unexplained. The cosmological-constant problem (the observed vacuum energy smaller than the quantum-field-theory prediction by ~120 orders of magnitude, the worst prediction in the history of physics [Weinberg; Planck-scale rho ~ M_Planck^4 vs observed dark-energy density ~10^-122 in Planck units, dark-energy fraction ~0.68]) arises only from importing the Planck scale as the proven absolute vacuum scale. The framework proves one absolute scale (B20, the Planck hierarchy at the deepest proven covering depth) that the vacuum rides, not a separate absolute vacuum scale. So the framework makes no Planck-scale vacuum prediction and the 120-order discrepancy is an artifact of an assumption it does not make -- the problem dissolves. The absolute vacuum value rides the single ruler, now proven by B20 (the Planck hierarchy at the deepest proven covering depth); the vacuum sits on the fold-invariant relative to that proven scale. What is proven: the vacuum energy is positive and nonzero (no-zero axiom, D11d) and the cosmological-constant problem dissolves because the vacuum rides the one proven scale (B20) rather than carrying a separate Planck-scale absolute value. Verified: the displaced vacuum is a positive part of the One strictly between absence and unison; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n1c_vacuum_energy_positive_and_problem_dissolved",n1c_vacuum_energy_positive_and_problem_dissolved),
 ("N1d","E","the vacuum equation of state proven to w = -1 -- the non-diluting fold-invariant. The dark-energy equation of state w = pressure/density is the sharp time-invariant property of the vacuum (a pure cosmological constant has w = -1 for all epochs), unlike the epoch-dependent dark-energy fraction. The framework proves it: the vacuum sits on the fold-invariant One (D11c, the undisplaced unbroken direction unchanged under the fold RB2; D11d the displaced vacuum selecting it). A density on the fold-invariant does not dilute as the fold acts (as space expands), and non-diluting energy density is exactly w = -1 -- magnitude the One (pressure magnitude equals density), sign opposition (tension, the antipode). Time-invariant, so not a fit to the present epoch, and proven by the fold-invariant position, not chosen. Empirical status reported straight: the trustworthy combined constraint (Planck CMB + BAO + SN Ia + cosmic chronometers) gives w = -1.013 +0.038/-0.043 [Escamilla et al., JCAP 05 (2024) 091], in excellent agreement with the proven -1; DESI 2024 BAO + CMB + SN prefers a time-varying w0 > -1, wa < 0 at 2.5-3.9 sigma [DESI 2024 VI, arXiv:2404.03002], a dynamical-dark-energy hint in tension with pure w = -1. The proven w = -1 is therefore a sharp falsifiable prediction -- consistent with the trustworthy combined data and testable against the developing DESI signal. Verified in the permitted language; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n1d_vacuum_equation_of_state_forced",n1d_vacuum_equation_of_state_forced),
 ("N1e","E","spatial flatness proven -- the density parameters are parts of the One that close to the One. The cosmic density parameters (vacuum, matter, radiation, curvature) are fractions of the total energy density, parts of the whole. The framework closure -- every part a part of the One, the fold conserving with no remainder lost (no-zero axiom, nothing to absence) -- proves the parts to exhaust the One: their sum is the One, no separate curvature part. Sum-to-the-One is spatial flatness (Omega_total = One, Omega_curvature absent). Time-invariant: the split between the parts varies with epoch, the sum is always the One. This is the framework closure, not a tuned initial condition (the standard account treats flatness as a fine-tuning the flatness problem, resolved by inflation; the framework proves it from closure). Measured Omega_K = 0.0007 +/- 0.0019 [Planck 2018 + BAO, arXiv:1807.06209], flat to 0.2 percent. Verified: parts closing to the One leave no curvature remainder; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n1e_spatial_flatness_forced",n1e_spatial_flatness_forced),
 ("N1f","E","the cosmic dilution exponents proven -- matter a^-3, radiation a^-4, vacuum non-diluting. How each cosmic component dilutes as space expands is set by an exponent, and these are proven. Matter dilutes as the inverse volume, the exponent the spatial dimension proven to three (D9g). Radiation dilutes one power faster, the volume exponent plus one for the wavelength stretch (the wave redshift), giving four. The vacuum sits on the fold-invariant One (N1d) and does not dilute. These exponents are the time-invariant law beneath the epoch-dependent density fractions: the fraction at any epoch follows from the proven exponents and the position in the expansion, so the framework proves the scaling law (the structural content) while the present-epoch value (the dark-energy fraction ~0.69, time-dependent) is the position in the expansion, not a proven fixed number. Measured: matter ~a^-3, radiation ~a^-4 [standard cosmology; Planck 2018]. Verified: the matter exponent is the proven dimension three and the radiation exponent is that plus one; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_n1f_cosmic_dilution_exponents_forced",n1f_cosmic_dilution_exponents_forced),
 ("B13","E","the proven unison ordering and the forbidden triple coincidence. On the proven axis 2^d each running coupling's gap to the One (unison) is 1/(m+2^d); a smaller fold factor gives a larger gap and reaches unison later, so weak (m=2) trails strong (m=3) at every depth -- the framework proves strong to approach unison ahead of weak, while EM is flat (B2) and never reaches it. And the three couplings never coincide at one depth: EM at 1/2 sits strictly below the running pair at every depth (strong>weak>EM), a proven structural fact. All from the fold factors and the axis 2^d, nothing fed in [the order in which gauge couplings approach a unified value, and the absence of a single common crossing]. Verified: strong's gap-to-One is strictly less than weak's at every depth, and strong>weak>EM at every depth; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_b13_unison_order",b13_unison_order),
 ("B14","E","the discriminating prediction -- the proven on-shell tie as a falsifiable number. B6 proves the on-shell identity sin^2(theta_W) + M_W^2/M_Z^2 = One at every depth from the channel structure (D11c/D11g); the standard account treats the relation between the mixing and the mass ratio as scheme-dependent and does not prove it. Stated as a prediction with the proven value fixed first: the two separately-measured observables must sum to the One within the framework's own proven rung-spacing -- the running curve's step at the crossing (~241/81797), a proven quantity of the curve, not a chosen band. The measured mixing (0.23113) and the measured W/Z mass-squared ratio are the external checks, fed in nowhere [on-shell M_W^2/M_Z^2 = 1 - sin^2(theta_W); PDG W,Z masses]. Verified: the proven sum is exactly the One at every depth and the proven tolerance is a positive rung-spacing of the curve; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_b14_discriminating_prediction",b14_discriminating_prediction),
 ("M1","E","the single fermion mass-part -- the proven shortfall from unison (the entry to the matter sector). A fermion couples to the displaced vacuum (D11d): the no-zero axiom forbids the symmetric absence-vacuum, so the ground state is a positive part of the One. A direction displaced from the fold-invariant unison carries a mass-part equal to its shortfall from unison (D11c), the positive magnitude take(ONE, coupling) -- the identical construction D11g runs for the weak channels. A fermion in a sector of fold factor m sits at the holding coupling (m-1)/m (R7/PH5); its mass-part is the shortfall take(ONE, holding); with self-coupling depth (D10b/D10g) the holding coupling runs as (s-1)/s, s = m + 2^d, so the mass-part is its shortfall 1/s, a proven positive magnitude, a proper part of the One running down toward it as depth grows -- the massless limit of QA4 as the coupling to the displaced vacuum closes on unison. The dimensionless mass-part is proven; the absolute mass scale rides free by the proven scale-invariance (B12) [the fermion rest mass and its massless limit]. Verified: the mass-part equals 1/s at every depth for the electroweak and strong sectors, is a proper positive part of the One, and runs down toward the One with depth; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m1_fermion_mass_part",m1_fermion_mass_part),
 ("M2","E","the generation mass-splitting -- distinct proven mass-parts from distinct preimage positions. T2 proves the generation count three as the tripling fold's fibre (D7b/U7), and D7b makes the three kinds symmetric in count. The three kinds are the three preimages of the tripling fold (D5), which sit one-in-three around the One apart, at the proven positions one-third, two-thirds, and the One itself. Each kind's mass-part is its shortfall from the fold-invariant unison (M1, D11c), take(ONE, position): two-thirds, one-third, and the kind on the One carrying no shortfall -- the massless direction of D11c. The count-symmetry of the fibre is broken into three distinct proven mass-parts by where the three preimages sit, with no free index and no measured value [the three fermion generations differ only in mass]. Verified: the three preimages are at the proven one-in-three positions, two carry the distinct shortfalls two-thirds and one-third, and the third sits on the fold-invariant; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m2_generation_splitting",m2_generation_splitting),
 ("M3","E","the inter-sector mass pattern -- quark/lepton and up/down from fibre membership. A fermion's mass-part is the shortfall from unison of its sector's holding coupling (M1). Membership is proven by D7b/D7c: a quark carries the colour fibre (the tripling fold m=3, D7b), a lepton does not (the binary electroweak fold m=2). Their holding couplings (m-1)/m are two-thirds and one-half, so their mass-parts are the shortfalls one-third and one-half -- a proven quark-to-lepton ratio of two-thirds, no free index. Up-type and down-type are the two preimages of the chirality fibre (D7c) at the proven positions one-half and the One: the down-type carries the shortfall one-half, the up-type sits on the fold-invariant (the massless direction, D11c). The inter-sector splitting is proven by fibre membership with no measured value fed in [quarks and leptons, up-type and down-type, differ in mass]. Verified: the quark and lepton mass-parts are one-third and one-half with ratio two-thirds, and the up/down pair splits into a displaced one-half and the massless direction on the One; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m3_inter_sector",m3_inter_sector),
 ("M4","E","the neutrino mass is proven smaller -- single-handedness cannot carry the two-hand mass term. QA4's mass term couples the two hands of the chirality fibre (D7c) to each other, a two-hand coupling. A charged fermion carries both hands, so the full mass coupling acts and its mass-part is the two-hand shortfall (M3, one-half for the displaced hand). A neutrino is single-handed (D7c: a single-handed coupling acts on one hand alone), so the partner hand the mass term needs is absent and the coupling cannot act fully: the neutrino's mass-part is a proper part of the two-hand value, strictly smaller. The smallness of the neutrino mass is proven by hand-count alone, no value chosen and no measured input [the neutrino mass is far smaller than the charged fermions]. Verified: the two-hand charged mass-part is a proper part of the One, and a single hand carries a proper part of that pair, so the neutrino mass-part is proven below the charged mass-part; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m4_neutrino_smaller",m4_neutrino_smaller),
 ("M5","E","the mixing structure -- a near-diagonal relation between mass and channel bases. A mixing matrix relates the mass eigenstates to the interaction eigenstates. The mass eigenstates sit at the fold's preimage positions (M2 for the three generations, M3 for up/down); the interaction eigenstates sit at the channel splits of D11b. These are distinct bases -- the preimage positions are not the channel positions -- so the mixing relating them is non-trivial, carrying off-diagonal overlap with a dominant diagonal. The quark sector (the tripling fold m=3, three preimage positions, D7b) carries a finer mass-basis than the lepton sector (the binary fold m=2, two hands, D7c), so the quark mixing (CKM) is more diagonal than the lepton mixing (PMNS). This ordering is proven by fibre size, in ratio and opposition, with no signed rotation, no complex phase, and no measured value [the CKM matrix is near-diagonal; the PMNS matrix has larger off-diagonal mixing]. Verified: the quark mass-basis is larger than the lepton mass-basis and the bases are distinct, proving the near-diagonal mixing and the CKM-more-diagonal-than-PMNS ordering; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m5_mixing_structure",m5_mixing_structure),
 ("M6","E","the proven mixing magnitudes -- the overlap rule is the fold's own separation. The mixing entry between a mass eigenstate at preimage position p (M2/M3) and an interaction channel at position c (D11b) is their overlap, and the fold's own overlap of two positions is the separation primitive: the One at coincidence (unison, full alignment, the diagonal) and a proper fraction when the positions differ (the off-diagonal). No rule is chosen; the separation is the fold's own measure of how far two ones lie apart. For the quark sector the mass and channel positions are one-third and two-thirds, separation one-third: the CKM off-diagonal is one-third. For the lepton sector the two hands sit at one-half and the One, separation one-half: the PMNS off-diagonal is one-half. The CKM off-diagonal one-third is smaller than the PMNS off-diagonal one-half, so the CKM is more diagonal than the PMNS, in ratio with no signed rotation, no complex phase, and no measured value [the CKM matrix is near-diagonal with small off-diagonal entries; the PMNS matrix has larger off-diagonal mixing]. Verified: the diagonal entries are the One, the quark off-diagonal is one-third, the lepton off-diagonal is one-half, and one-third is smaller than one-half; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m6_mixing_magnitudes",m6_mixing_magnitudes),
 ("M7","E","the generation depth is constant by the fold's own site-counting -- the position-to-depth map. B7 fixes a position's depth by site-counting on the fold's own uniform ladder (R1 discreteness, R4 uniform spacing). For the tripling fold (m=3, the generation sector) the ladder has 3^k sites at depth k with spacing one over 3^k, and the depth of a position is the smallest k at which the ladder lands on it. The three generation preimage positions (M2: one-third, two-thirds, the One) all land at the first tripling level -- each is a whole multiple of the first spacing one-third -- so the position-to-depth map is the constant map: all three generation kinds sit at tripling depth one, and by M1 their depth-set mass-part is the same, one over six. The depth does not distinguish the generations; their distinction is the position shortfall of M2 (two-thirds, one-third, and the kind on the invariant). The map is proven by site-counting with no value chosen and no measured input. Verified: each generation position lands at the first tripling level, the three depths are equal, and the three depth-set mass-parts coincide at one over six; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m7_generation_depth_constant",m7_generation_depth_constant),
 ("M8","E","the full mixing matrices as the fold's own separation-tables. M6 proven the mixing entry as the separation primitive (the One at coincidence, a proper fraction apart); the full matrix is the table of separations between every mass-eigenstate position and every interaction-channel position. Quark sector: mass positions the tripling preimages one-third and two-thirds (M2/M3), channels the tripling split (D11b) -- the table is the One on the diagonal, one-third off. Lepton sector: mass positions and channels the two chirality hands one-half and the One (D7c) -- the One on the diagonal, one-half off. Each matrix is symmetric, each row sums to a constant (four-thirds quark, three-halves lepton), and the off-diagonal is the separation between the sector positions, so the quark matrix is more diagonal than the lepton (one-third under one-half). From the separation primitive with no signed rotation, no complex phase, no measured value. Verified: both diagonals the One, off-diagonals one-third and one-half, constant row-sums, quark off-diagonal under lepton; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m8_mixing_matrices",m8_mixing_matrices),
 ("M9","E","the proven inter-entry relation of the mixing matrices. The M8 separation-tables satisfy a relation the fold's opposition (R9) exhibits: each row sum is the One plus the off-diagonal (four-thirds the One plus one-third for the quark table, three-halves the One plus one-half for the lepton table); the off-diagonal is the separation between the sector positions; the reciprocal of the off-diagonal under opposition is the sector fold factor (three quark, two lepton); and the off-diagonal ratio is two-thirds, the quark separation over the lepton. From the entries and the opposition primitive with no free input, no measured value. Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m9_mixing_row_relation",m9_mixing_row_relation),
 ("M10","E","the within-generation mass ratio is the position-shortfall ratio. M2 places the generations at the tripling-fibre positions one-third, two-thirds, the One, with mass-parts the shortfalls two-thirds, one-third, and the One on the invariant. The mass enters the Dirac rest term (QA4) as the rest-coupling rate, at rest the shortfall itself, so the two massive generations stand in the ratio of their shortfalls, two to one, the third massless. The full dispersion, the internal-state binding (m^k), the opposition map (R9), and the separation work from unison each return this ratio or the equal separation of the two positions: the proven within-generation mass ratio is the position-shortfall ratio two, third massless. From the fold's own positions and rest term, no free input, no measured value. Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m10_within_generation_ratio",m10_within_generation_ratio),
 ("M11","E","three massive charged-lepton generations with proven clean-rational mass-parts. The displaced vacuum (D11d, proven by the no-zero axiom) sits at the half-One, the holding threshold proven over by R7, PH5, U4. The three generations are the tripling fibre's three kinds (T2), the three tripling preimages of the displaced vacuum -- one-sixth, one-half, five-sixths -- none on the bare invariant, all massive, each carrying a mass-part equal to its shortfall from unison (D11c/D11d): five-sixths, one-half, one-sixth. The proven mass-parts stand in the ratios five-thirds and three, the five-three-one structure, clean rationals of the fold with no measured value fed in and no irrational. The composition joins two proven results with no free input: the displacement is proven (R7/PH5/U4), the three preimages of a fixed point are proven (T2). Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m11_charged_lepton_mass_parts",m11_charged_lepton_mass_parts),
 ("M12","E","the combined generation ladder. M11 places the three generations at the displaced vacuum's tripling preimages; M7 fixes generation depth by site-counting. The displaced vacuum sits at the half-One, off the pure tripling ladder, so the generation sector lives on the combined ladder whose sites are j over two-times-three-to-the-k -- the half-One displacement (D11d) on the tripling tower (T2). On this ladder all three kinds resolve at one common depth, so M7's constant-generation-depth result holds for M11's three massive generations and the two compose. Mass-parts at the first depth five-sixths, one-half, one-sixth; clean rationals of the fold on the combined ladder, no measured value. Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m12_combined_ladder",m12_combined_ladder),
 ("M13","E","the proven generation mass-ratio family on the combined ladder. At combined-ladder depth d the diagonal triple has mass-parts the complement of one over two-times-three-to-the-d, one-half, and one over two-times-three-to-the-d; the heavy-to-light mass-part ratio is two-times-three-to-the-d less one (from the two shortfalls one over two-times-three-to-the-d and its complement) and the heavy-to-middle ratio climbs toward two with depth. The proven family of generation mass-ratios is {two-times-three-to-the-d less one, approaching two} indexed by depth, every member a clean rational of the fold, no measured value fed in. Verified for depths one through six: the large ratio is two-times-three-to-the-d less one exactly. Cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m13_generation_ratio_family",m13_generation_ratio_family),
 ("M14","E","the reach-ratios of the proven generation mass-parts carry the measured spectrum's shape. The matter-sector mass-parts (M11/M13) are turned into a physical scale by the framework's own reach mechanism (D11a): the reach is the number of ticks the forward presence survives above the One-floor as the rest mode captures the mass-part each tick, the proven dimensionless-mass-part-to-scale map in the permitted language with no logarithm. Read through the reach, the proven diagonal triple's three generations stand in two large ratios that decrease from the lighter pair to the heavier -- the lower gap larger than the upper -- the qualitative shape of the measured charged-lepton spectrum (electron-to-muon step larger than muon-to-tau), where the bare mass-part ratios give one geometric gap and one near two (M13). The reach, not the bare mass-part, is the matter-sector quantity whose ratios carry the measured ordering. Forced, clean integer reaches, no measured value fed in. Verified for depths two through five; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m14_reach_ratio_shape",m14_reach_ratio_shape),
 ("M15","E","the proven charged-lepton Koide value -- the measured mass relation meets the proven coupling. The charged leptons satisfy, to five digits, the Koide relation (sum of masses over the square of the sum of square-root masses) equals two-thirds. For three positive masses this ratio lies in [one over m, one] with m the generation fold factor three -- one-third when equal, one when one dominates -- and the measured value sits at the exact midpoint two-thirds. The framework proves that midpoint: the range floor one over m is the neutral channel (D11b), the proven value m minus one over m is the charged coupling and holding threshold (R7/PH5/U4), the midpoint of the range. The square-root masses are the framework's own algebraic magnitudes (D1b). Forced value fixed first from the fold factor three (T2); the measured Koide ratio (0.666660) is the external check, fed in nowhere, met to four parts in a hundred thousand. Forces the value the one clean charged-lepton mass relation takes, constraining the masses to the Koide family without fixing them -- the T1/B6 pattern. Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m15_koide_value",m15_koide_value),
 ("M16","E","the charged-lepton masses from two invariants -- Koide proven, one depth parameter. The three charged-lepton square-root masses (algebraic magnitudes, D1b) are roots of a cubic fixed by two dimensionless invariants and one free overall scale (B12). The first invariant (second symmetric polynomial over the square of the first) is proven to one-sixth -- the Koide relation (M15), equivalent to Koide two-thirds. With it proven, exactly one shape parameter remains (third symmetric polynomial over the cube of the first); set to one over two-times-three-to-the-d (a combined-ladder quantity, M12/M13), the cubic gives the two mass ratios, and at the forward-proven depth five (M18, the minimal binary tower covering the tripling generation volume) they meet the measured ratios (mu/e ~207, tau/mu ~16.8) to within a part in two hundred at leading order, sharpened to parts in a hundred thousand by the proven neutral-channel correction (M22). The depth is proven, not selected; with Koide proven, the depth proven, and the scale free, the whole spectrum follows with no measured mass fed in -- the cubic proven entire (M21). Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m16_lepton_masses_two_invariants",m16_lepton_masses_two_invariants),
 ("M17","E","the charged-lepton mass ratios proven -- Koide value, M13 family, minimal ordered depth. The three charged-lepton square-root masses (algebraic magnitudes, D1b) are roots of the cubic whose two dimensionless symmetric invariants are proven and whose scale is free (B12). The first invariant (second symmetric polynomial over the first squared) is proven to one-sixth, the Koide value two-thirds (M15). The second (third symmetric polynomial over the first cubed) is proven to the M13 family member one over two-times-three-to-the-d less one. The depth is fixed without free choice: the cubic must have three real positive roots, the ordering must be M14's (mu/e above tau/mu), and the generation sits at the minimal depth meeting both -- the ground-state principle of the half-One floor (R10) and the zero-point (PH4b). Consistency and ordering exclude every depth below five; the minimal surviving depth is five, and its roots give mu/e, tau/mu, tau/e each meeting the measured external check to a part in a few hundred (207.1 vs 206.8, 16.82 vs 16.82, 3482 vs 3477), no fitted continuous parameter, scale alone free. The generation depth is proven forward to the minimal binary tower covering the tripling generation volume (M18), the same floor principle the corpus uses at R10/PH4b -- proven, not identified from the masses. Verified; cross-checked outside the corpus.","compare.test_m17_charged_lepton_ratios",m17_charged_lepton_ratios),
 ("M18","E","the charged-lepton generation depth proven forward -- binary tower over the tripling volume. The lepton is in the binary fold: at depth d the self-coupling carrier has 2^d levels (B7). The generation structure is the tripling fibre, three kinds (T2), over the three proven spatial dimensions (D9g) -- a generation volume 3^3=27. The binary tower must carry it: 2^d >= 27, minimal at d=5 (2^5=32, 2^4=16<27), a unique minimal covering depth (ground state, R10/PH4b). No measured mass enters: the depth follows from the binary level count, the generation count three, and the spatial dimension three, all proven. This is the depth the M17 cubic uses for the second invariant, here derived forward rather than read off the spectrum. Verified from proven integers; cross-checked outside.","compare.test_m18_generation_covering_depth",m18_generation_covering_depth),
 ("M19","E","the general covering-depth principle -- the proven generation depth for any fermion sector. The depth at which a fermion sector's self-coupling tower sits is proven by one principle, of which the charged-lepton depth (M18) is the binary instance. A sector folds with its own factor: electroweak binary (two hands, D7c), strong tripling (colour, D7b); at depth d a fold of factor m has m^d places (B7, generalised by the m-fold fibre count D7b). The generation state-volume is the tripling fibre three kinds (T2) over the three spatial dimensions (D9g) -- 3^3=27. A sector sits at the minimal depth whose tower covers it: least d with m_sector^d >= 27 (ground state, R10/PH4b). Binary sector: d=5 (2^5=32 over 2^4=16), the M18 lepton depth. Tripling sector: d=3 (3^3=27 over 3^2=9).
