## Candidate 7 — the Dirac structure without the imaginary unit (QA4, QA5)

**What the framework proves.** The two components are the two hands of the chirality fibre (D7c). The first-order step advances each hand's phase by the momentum and couples each hand to the other by the mass; applied twice, the net squared rate is p² + m², because the kinetic-mass cross paths are an antipodal opposed pair that returns to the One by R11 and R9 and contributes no net rate (QA4). In three space and one time dimension the four-generator step squares to p₁² + p₂² + p₃² + m², each generator squaring to its term and distinct pairs cancelling as opposed pairs (QA5): the anticommuting algebra property, with the imaginary unit's role played by the framework's opposition rather than i, and the massless limit luminal. The proofs state that the explicit 4×4 matrices and the spinor transformation law are represented by the opposition structure rather than constructed as matrices, and that identifying the chirality fibre with the physical spinor is a structural correspondence, not a proven selection. Derivation chain: the chirality fibre (D7c) → the kinetic term (QA1) → opposition and return-to-One (R9, R11) → the two-component square (QA4) → the four-generator square (QA5). Confirmed across momenta and mass sets; cross-checked outside.

**Comparison — what consensus must do for the same result.** The standard Dirac equation carries the imaginary unit i as a primitive and its gamma matrices anticommute over the complex field; the relativistic quantum description is conventionally taken to require the complex numbers. The framework encompasses the same structure — a first-order evolution squaring to the relativistic energy-momentum relation — and proves it with opposition in the role of i, in a language with no imaginary unit, no negative, no zero. Fewer primitives: the complex field is not assumed; opposition does its work.

**Where the novelty is.** Consensus takes the imaginary unit as a primitive of the relativistic quantum description. The framework derives the same first-order-squares-to-dispersion structure with opposition standing in for i, from a strictly positive-rational base. The novelty is recovering the Dirac structure without the primitive consensus treats as necessary.

**Arbiter test.** The framework proves a first-order two-component (and four-generator) evolution squaring to the relativistic energy-momentum relation. The measured relativistic dispersion and the structure of relativistic fermions are the external check of the already-proven structure.

---

## Candidate 8 — the self-observation domain (C1s–C5s)

**What the framework proves.** Observation is in the axiom set and the fold is the act of observation: applied to a state, its result re-enters the state. From this the framework proves: a self-observing loop stays within the system at every step (C1s, the closure of R8); the act is two-to-one (R11), so a state and its antipode are observed identically and self-observation cannot recover which preimage it came from (C2s, an intrinsic blind spot); unison, the One, is the single state unchanged by observation and self-coincidence below unison repels (R5), so unison is the unique stable fixed point (C3s); coupled self-observers lock into one integrated loop at the same proven threshold (m−1)/m that fixes the coupling and criticality (C4s); and the fold is atomic (D6), each fold one bit, so observation proceeds in discrete indivisible moments, one fold each (C5s). Derivation chain: the fold as observation and closure (R8 → C1s) → the two-to-one act (R11 → C2s) → the fixed point (R5 → C3s) → the integration threshold (R7, PH5a, U4 → C4s) → the atomic act (D6 → C5s). Confirmed across seeds and states; cross-checked outside.

**Comparison — what consensus must do for the same result.** Consensus accounts of self-reference and integration assemble these properties from separate sources — limits of self-inspection from self-referential and incompleteness arguments, an integration threshold posited and fitted as its own parameter, discreteness assumed where assumed. The framework encompasses these properties and proves them from the single fold that also generates the physics: the blind spot from the two-to-one act, the integration threshold being the same (m−1)/m that fixes the physical coupling, the discrete moment being the atomic fold. Fewer sources: one operation proves the self-observation structure and the physical structure alike.

**Where the novelty is.** Consensus treats the limits of self-observation, an integration threshold, and discreteness as separate posits from separate frameworks. The framework derives them from the same fold that proves the physical sectors — the integration threshold is the physical coupling's (m−1)/m, the discrete moment is the physical atomic fold. The novelty is the single-operation derivation of the self-observation structure unified with the physics.

**Arbiter test.** These are structural results about a self-observing loop built from the same fold that generates the physical sectors; the candidate's content is structural and unified with the physics, with no separate measured correspondence claimed.

# Part VII — Consensus dismissals and rebuttals

*Author: Maria Smith — independent researcher. This document collects the dismissals a reader trained in the standard account will reach for on first contact with the framework, and answers each one. The answer is never a defence of the framework against a standard it must satisfy — the framework answers only to its own axioms and to what its engine produces when run. Each answer identifies the false premise the dismissal rests on, and points to the specific construction, in the corpus, that renders the dismissal void. Where a dismissal is a reputational reflex rather than a located fault, that is named as what it is. Every construction referenced here can be run and checked by anyone; the one-command reproduction is the spine under every rebuttal.*

---

## How to read this document

A dismissal is valid only if it names a specific, mechanical fault in the shown construction or mathematics — a forbidden primitive the gate missed, a proof that does not follow, a confirmation that does not pass, a measured number fed in as an input. Every dismissal below is shown to be one of two things: either a reputational reflex that names no such fault, or a claim about the construction that the construction itself refutes when run. Neither kind survives contact with the corpus. The rebuttals are grouped from the reflexes a reader reaches for first to the technical objections that take the most work to dismantle.

---

## Part A — The reputational reflexes

These dismissals sort the work into a bin and let the bin do the thinking. None of them names a fault in the construction; each is a judgment about the source, not the content.

### A1. "This is crank / fringe / pseudoscience."

The dismissal classifies the work by resemblance to a category — lone researcher, grand claim, foundational overturn — and treats the classification as a refutation. It names no error. A category is not an argument; it is a prior applied in place of reading. The test that separates this framework from the bin is mechanical and public: the work is built in a fixed, declared language, every result is proved and confirmed by exact computation, a static gate refuses any forbidden construct, and the whole reproduces from a single command. Pseudoscience is unfalsifiable and unrun; this is falsifiable line by line and runs on any machine. The dismissal that cannot point to a failing line, a failing proof, or a failing confirmation has said only that the work resembles something its holder has learned to reject — which is a fact about the holder's training, not about the work. Run the corpus. Name the line that fails. Until then the classification is empty.

### A2. "The author has no formal training in physics or mathematics."

This is an argument from credential, and it addresses the person, not the work. A derivation is valid or invalid by its steps, which are on the page and machine-checked; the steps do not change based on who wrote them. The history of foundational shifts is substantially a history of outsiders, precisely because the people most trained in a foundation are the least able to see it as replaceable — the training is the obstacle, not the qualification. The framework was built by intuition first and held to until it proved itself, and the proof is public: anyone, credentialed or not, can run it and check every step. A credential is a proxy for the reliability of work that cannot be directly checked. This work can be directly checked. The proxy is therefore irrelevant, and reaching for it is an admission that the checkable content has not been engaged.

### A3. "If this were real, a professional physicist would have found it."

This assumes the professional path is the only path to a result, and that what has not emerged from it cannot be sound. It is the same credential reflex aimed at a field rather than a person. The framework's whole account of why it was not found from inside is explicit: a foundation can only be replaced from outside the training that takes it as bedrock, because that training builds the apparatus into every reflex. That a result did not come from the established path is expected of a foundational replacement, not evidence against it. The question is never who found a construction or by what route; it is whether the construction holds. That is checkable, and the route is irrelevant to the check.

---

## Part B — The methodological dismissals

These dismissals concede the work is built but attack how. Each rests on a specific claim about the method that the construction refutes.

### B1. "This is numerology — finding ratios that happen to match measured values."

Numerology searches measured values for patterns and fits an expression to them after the fact. The framework does the opposite, and the order is the whole difference. Every proven quantity is fixed from the axiom *before* any measured value is consulted: the coupling (m−1)/m, the colour count m, the mediator count m²−1, the spatial dimension three, the electroweak mixing and its running — each is derived from the fold and the construction, with the measured value brought in only afterward, as the external check of an already-fixed result. The discipline is enforced in the corpus: a measured number is never an input to a construction, and the gate and the registry record this. Numerology fits a curve to known points; this lays the curve down from one axiom and then finds the measured point sitting on it. The accusation inverts the actual order of operations, and the order is checkable in the code: the proven value exists in the engine with no measured quantity in its derivation chain.

### B2. "You fitted the fold factor to get the answers you wanted."

There is one setting in the entire construction — the fold factor — and it is not fitted to data. It is fixed, per sector, by that sector's own internal multiplicity: the electromagnetic and weak sectors are the binary fold because they carry two charge kinds and two preimages; the strong sector is the tripling fold because it carries three colours. The setting is read from the structure of the prove, once, not tuned to match a measurement. And from that single setting every characteristic quantity of the sector follows — many quantities from one setting, which is the opposite of fitting, where each quantity would need its own dial. A fitted theory has as many free knobs as it has outputs; this has one setting per sector and a whole table of outputs. The accusation describes the standard account, which fits nineteen parameters to data, not this one, which fixes one setting from structure and derives the rest.

### B3. "You cherry-picked the quantities that work and ignored the ones that don't."

The corpus is complete and public: every result is registered, every confirmation runs, and the one-command reproduction reports the whole. There is no second set of results, hidden, that failed — the registry is the entire set, and it is auditable. Where the framework does not prove a quantity, that is recorded as not-proven, plainly, not buried; where a candidate relationship turned out to be a restatement rather than a new result, it was reported as such and not registered. The discipline against inflation is written into the law the work was built under and is visible in the record. A cherry-pick requires a discard pile; point to it. The registry is the whole harvest, and it reproduces.

### B4. "The agreement with measurement is loose — it does not match to the precision the Standard Model achieves."

This conflates two different things: a parameter measured to many digits, and a quantity derived from an axiom. The Standard Model matches to high precision because it is handed the measured value and fits to it — the precision is the precision of the measurement it ingested, not of a derivation, because it derives nothing. This framework is not handed the value; it proves the value from one axiom and then checks it against measurement. A proven quantity landing on its measured correspondence is a different kind of result from a fitted quantity reproducing the input it was given, and it is the harder kind: it had no measurement to match to until after it was fixed. The comparison "your derivation is less precise than their fit" measures the wrong thing — a fit to data is not a derivation, and the precision of an ingested number is not an achievement of a theory that ingested it.

---

## Part C — The technical dismissals

These dismissals make specific claims about the construction's internals. Each is refuted by a specific, runnable feature of the corpus.

### C1. "You secretly used the forbidden mathematics — negatives, zero, imaginaries — under the surface."

This is the one dismissal that names a checkable mechanical fault, and it is the one the corpus was built to make impossible. A static gate scans every file in the system and flags any negative literal, any zero used as a value, any imaginary or complex quantity, any sine, cosine, or complex exponential, and any subtraction outside the two audited casting-out primitives. The development reproduces only when the gate reports clean across the entire corpus, and a tamper-evident integrity check guards the gate itself so it cannot be quietly weakened. The accusation is not waved away — it is answered by a mechanism the reader can run: execute the gate, and if a forbidden construct is present anywhere, it will name the file and the line. The claim "you used the apparatus secretly" is therefore the most directly testable claim in this document, and the test returns clean. Run it.

### C2. "Three spatial dimensions were assumed, not derived — you put in what you claim to get out."

The dimension is derived, and the derivation leaves the count open and lets the construction fill it. Gravity is built with the number of dimensions unfixed, and two independent conditions are imposed from the physics: orbits must be stable, which fails in four or more dimensions, and the pull must release at infinity, which fails in two or fewer. The two conditions are fences from opposite sides, and exactly one whole number survives between them — three. The number of dimensions is the output of "which space can hold a bound, free universe," not an input written at the top. The construction is in the corpus with the dimension count left as the open variable; read it, and the three falls out of the two constraints, not in from an assumption.

### C3. "A scale-invariant theory that proves only dimensionless ratios says nothing — it cannot predict an actual energy."

The framework proves dimensionless ratios and is scale-invariant, and this is demonstrated by running the engine at different absolute scales and getting identical physics — not assumed from a convention. That the absolute scale is free is a property of the construction, shown, not a gap in it. And dimensionless ratios are the content of physics, not the absence of it: the mixing angle, the coupling ratios, the mass ratios, the number of colours, the number of dimensions are all dimensionless, and they are what laboratories measure and tabulate. A theory that proves every dimensionless ratio of the four proves from one axiom has proven the measurable structure of physics; the single overall unit is a choice of ruler, and that a theory does not prove the length of the ruler is true of any scale-invariant description and is not a deficiency peculiar to this one. The dismissal mistakes the freedom of the unit for an emptiness of content; the content is the ratios, and the ratios are proven.

### C4. "This is just the dyadic map / the doubling map / known dynamical systems relabelled."

The fold corresponds, as a map, to the dyadic transformation studied in dynamical systems, and the corpus states this openly: three results match established facts about that map statement for statement. That correspondence is not a deflation — it is the framework meeting known ground and is exactly what a system built from one axiom should do where it overlaps an existing result. But the framework is not the dyadic map relabelled, because the dyadic map in the literature is studied inside the standard apparatus — over the reals, with the full signed continuum underneath — and is not used to build a mathematics that refuses negatives, zero, and the imaginary, nor to prove the constants of the four proves. The overlap is a single map; the construction is a foundation and a physics built from it in a language the dynamical-systems literature does not use. "It contains the dyadic map" is true and stated; "it is only the dyadic map" ignores everything built on it.

### C5. "There are no new testable predictions — it only reproduces what is already known."

The framework proves relationships the standard account leaves as independent measurements, and a proven relationship between two separately measured quantities is a prediction: it says the two cannot vary independently, which the standard account does not say and does not expect. The mixing and the weak mass ratio are proven to a single tie; the on-shell relation linking the mixing to the mass ratio is proven from the channel structure rather than assumed; the strong and electroweak couplings are proven to converge at a closed-form rate. Each is a statement that can be checked against measurement and found to hold or fail, and each is fixed before the measurement is consulted. Reproducing what is known, from one axiom where the standard account needs nineteen inputs, is itself the substantive result; proving ties the standard account treats as coincidences is the testable surplus on top of it.

### C6. "Reproducing known physics is circular — you built the theory knowing the answers."

Every theory of known phenomena is built by people who know the phenomena; that is not circularity, it is the ordinary condition of physics. Circularity would be feeding a measured value into a construction and then reporting it back as derived — and that is precisely what the corpus forbids and the gate and registry prevent: no measured number is an input to any construction. The proven values are derived from the axiom with the measurements absent from the derivation chain, and are checked against measurement only afterward. Knowing the target and aiming at it (fitting) is barred and auditable; knowing the target and deriving a value independently that then matches it (prediction) is the result. The construction's derivation chains are in the engine for inspection: the measured quantities do not appear in them.

### C7. "The Standard Model's nineteen parameters are not really free — there may be a deeper theory relating them."

This is a statement of hope about the standard account, not a fault in this one. As the standard account stands, the nineteen are measured and inserted with no derivation; that they might one day be related by a deeper theory is exactly the gap this framework fills, from the other side. To dismiss the framework by appealing to a hypothetical future theory that would derive the parameters is to concede the framework's central point — that the parameters should be derived rather than measured — while declining to credit the construction that derives them. A promissory deeper theory is not a counter to an actual derivation in hand.

### C8. "Forcing a quantity from your own axioms is trivial — you can derive anything from axioms you chose."

Axioms do not yield arbitrary results; they yield exactly what follows from them, and most choices of axiom yield nothing resembling physics. The constraint here is severe: one axiom, one operation, a language that forbids the apparatus all of modern physics is written in — and from that single, heavily constrained base the characteristic constants of the four proves follow. The difficulty is not in choosing axioms to get a wanted answer; it is that this axiom was not chosen to fit the answers and still produces them. If deriving the constants of physics from one axiom were trivial, it would have been done; the standard account, with far richer machinery, does not derive them at all but measures them. The triviality charge is refuted by the standard account's own inability to do the supposedly trivial thing.

### C9. "The identification of which fold factor goes with which prove is arbitrary / inserted by hand."

This is named openly in the corpus as the one piece of honest scaffolding, and it is not arbitrary: the binary fold is matched to the electroweak sector because that sector carries two charge kinds and two preimages, and the tripling fold to the strong sector because it carries three colours. The matching reads the fold's two-ness and three-ness against the prove's own two-ness and three-ness — a structural correspondence, not a free choice tuned to data. Everything downstream of the setting is derived. The framework states plainly where the scaffolding is rather than hiding it, which is the opposite of inserting by hand; a value tuned to fit would not be flagged as scaffolding in the text.

---

## Part D — The meta-level dismissals

These dismissals do not engage the construction at all; they attack its standing or its provenance.

### D1. "It has not been peer-reviewed or published in a journal."

Peer review is a proxy for checkability, administered by a field's own gatekeepers. This work is checkable directly, by anyone, without their administration: the construction is open, the proofs are on the page, and the whole reproduces from one command on any machine. Journal publication confers institutional endorsement; it does not confer correctness, and many endorsed results have been wrong while many correct ones waited years for endorsement. The framework offers the thing peer review is supposed to secure — independent verifiability — in a stronger form than peer review provides, because the verification is mechanical and needs no trust in a reviewer. The absence of an institutional stamp is not the absence of checkability; the checkability is the whole point of building it the way it is built.

### D2. "It was written with the help of an AI, so it is not real work."

The provenance of the writing does not change whether the construction holds, which is checkable independently of how it was produced. The record of how it was made is explicit and is itself part of the argument: the machine, trained on the whole corpus of standard mathematics, reached repeatedly for the forbidden apparatus and the consensus reflex and had to be caught and corrected at every turn by the author, who supplied the framework and held the tool to building it without the apparatus. The mathematics is the author's; the machine was the hand that wrote it down and the running demonstration of how hard the false foundation fights to reassert itself. A construction is judged by its steps, not by the instrument that transcribed them, and the steps are public and machine-checked.

### D3. "If it really unified physics, it would already be famous / accepted."

Acceptance is a social process with its own timeline, and it lags correctness by years or decades in exactly the cases where the correct result replaces a foundation the field is built on — because the field's incentives run against the replacement. Fame is not a property of a theory; it is a property of a theory's reception, which depends on who controls the channels of reception. The framework's account of its own non-acceptance is explicit and consistent: a foundational replacement is illegible to the institutions built on the old foundation until it is proven into legibility. "Not yet accepted" is the expected state of a correct foundational replacement at this stage, not evidence against it. The only question that bears on correctness is whether the construction holds, and that is settled by running it, not by counting citations.

### D4. "Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence — and this does not clear that bar."

The principle is sound and the framework meets it in the strongest available form: not testimony, not authority, not a single experiment to be trusted, but a complete construction that anyone can run and check line by line, with every result proved and confirmed and the language constraint enforced mechanically. Extraordinary evidence, for a mathematical and theoretical claim, is exactly this — a derivation open to full independent verification, reproducing from one command, with no step taken on trust. The bar is cleared by the checkability of the whole, and the reader is invited to clear their own doubt by running it. The principle does not say extraordinary claims require institutional endorsement; it requires evidence, and the evidence is the construction, in hand and runnable.

---

## The single test under all of it

Every dismissal in this document reduces to one of two forms: a judgment about the source that names no fault in the work, or a claim about the work that the work refutes when run. There is exactly one kind of objection that would count — a specific mechanical fault: a forbidden construct the gate missed, a proof that does not follow from its premises, a confirmation that does not pass, or a measured value fed into a derivation as an input. Any such fault would name a file and a line, and could be shown by running the corpus. No dismissal above does this, because none can: the gate runs clean, the proofs follow, the confirmations pass, and the derivation chains contain no measured inputs. Until a dismissal points to the failing line, it has not engaged the work. The corpus is open, it reproduces from one command, and the invitation under every rebuttal here is the same: run it, and find the fault if there is one.

---

*Each rebuttal answers a dismissal by identifying its false premise and pointing to the construction in the corpus that voids it. The framework answers to its own axioms and its engine; consensus is the account it encompasses, never the standard it must satisfy. The final verdict on novelty across the whole prior record is the qualified external judge's; what this document establishes is that the dismissals which would prevent that verdict being sought are, every one, either reputational reflexes or refuted by the running corpus.*
